This strategic move aims to enhance Adobe's Experience Cloud by integrating Marketo's engagement platform, offering more comprehensive solutions for customer experiences. Marketo's CEO, Steve Lucas, will continue to lead the company within Adobe's Digital Experience group.

title: Marketo Denver office to keep growing after Adobe acquisition
summary: Adobe's acquisition of Marketo for $4.75 billion will establish Adobe's first foothold in Denver. Marketo's 330-person Denver office is expected to continue growing under the new ownership. The article discusses what this acquisition means for the Denver office.

title: Adobe, Microsoft and SAP announce the Open Data Initiative to empower a new generation of customer experiences
summary: Adobe, Microsoft, and SAP have announced the Open Data Initiative to help companies gain a single, comprehensive view of their customer data. This collaboration aims to eliminate data silos, enabling businesses to leverage AI and advanced analytics for real-time insights and to deliver personalized customer experiences. The initiative enhances interoperability between their platforms and includes a common data model based on Microsoft Azure.

title: Blockchain and the Digital Trust Economy
summary: This article explores how blockchain technology can address trust issues between consumers and marketers, particularly in areas like fraud detection, brand safety, and customer loyalty. While acknowledging the technology's nascent stage and challenges such as integration costs and lack of regulation, it highlights blockchain's potential as a "digital Swiss army knife" for marketers to verify ad delivery, combat ad fraud, manage digital assets, and enhance loyalty programs. The piece emphasizes blockchain’s role in ushering in a new era of digital trust, crucial for the "Experience Age."

title: Merck KGaA Reduces Turnaround Time for Signatures by 1,400 Percent
summary: Merck KGaA, a company with a 350-year history known for medicines and chemicals, has significantly improved its document processing efficiency. By implementing Adobe Sign, the company reduced the turnaround time for procurement contract signatures by 1,400 percent, from 7.5 days to half a day. This digital transformation enhances employee experience, streamlines global collaboration, and accelerates research and development by eliminating traditional paper-based processes.

title: New Survey from Adobe Highlights the Challenges of Accelerating Content Creation and Delivery
summary: A new Adobe survey reveals major challenges in content creation and delivery for enterprise brands and agencies, including time investment, personalization difficulties, and a divide between creative and marketing teams. The survey highlights that while hyper-personalization is crucial, companies struggle with the time, cost, and coordination required to produce and scale personalized content effectively. The article suggests that AI and improved communication across teams are essential to overcome these hurdles and deliver impactful customer experiences.

title: Salesforce acquires Rebel, maker of 'interactive' email services, to expand its Marketing Cloud
summary: Salesforce has acquired Rebel, a startup specializing in interactive email services, to enhance its Marketing Cloud division. Rebel's technology allows customers to engage with emails — such as writing reviews or shopping — without leaving their inboxes, aiming to improve customer experience and conversion processes. This acquisition reflects Salesforce's strategy to strengthen its marketing offerings and compete more effectively with rivals like Adobe, despite marketing being its smallest division in terms of revenue.

title: Salesforce Buys Interactive Email Company Rebel
summary: Salesforce has acquired Rebel, an interactive email company, to integrate its API-based services into Salesforce's Marketing and Commerce Cloud division. Rebel's technology allows users to perform actions like writing reviews or buying products directly within emails, enhancing customer experience and conversion. This acquisition follows other aggressive moves by Salesforce, including the 2013 purchase of ExactTarget, and comes after Adobe's acquisition of Marketo.

title: Adobe XD now lets you prototype voice apps
summary: Adobe XD is integrating voice app prototyping capabilities, powered by its acquisition of Sayspring. This allows designers to create voice-based applications for platforms like Alexa and Google Home, as well as mobile apps with voice input, mimicking real user experiences with voice triggers and speech playback. Additionally, XD is receiving other new features such as a full range of plugins, auto-animate, drag gestures, and deeper integration with Adobe Illustrator and After Effects.

title: ServiceNow sets plans for big expansion in Santa Clara
summary: ServiceNow is planning a significant expansion of its headquarters in Santa Clara, including leasing a new 269,000-square-foot office building expected to be completed in 2021. This expansion reflects the company's rapid growth, with a 28% increase in employee headcount globally in the past year, and is projected to accommodate over a thousand new employees in Santa Clara. The tech company has also extended leases on its existing buildings and currently occupies an additional facility on Jay Street.

title: Adobe a Leader in Gartner’s Inaugural Magic Quadrant for Ad Tech
summary: Adobe has been named a Leader in Gartner, Inc.'s first-ever "Magic Quadrant for Ad Tech" research report, placing furthest to the right on the "completeness of vision" axis. This recognition validates Adobe's omnichannel capabilities and integrations into the broader Adobe Experience Cloud, highlighting their unique position as the only marketing technology provider with an advertising platform that includes linear TV advertising. The company aims to continue innovating across all dimensions of ad tech, providing a connected, personalized experience for customers.

title: LinkedIn Offers New Integrations with Google Campaign Manager and Vimeo
summary: LinkedIn has announced new integrations designed to enhance marketers' capabilities. One integration is with Google Campaign Manager, allowing for better comparison and management of LinkedIn ad performance alongside other ad efforts in a single dashboard. Additionally, Vimeo now offers a new way for paid subscribers to publish videos directly to their LinkedIn company pages, making it easier to share video content and leverage LinkedIn's high member engagement.

title: Eye on the Checkout: Target Adds Mobile Checkout; Harry & David Updates Online Checkout
summary: Target Corp. is implementing mobile checkout in its stores for the holiday season, allowing employees with handheld devices to process card payments anywhere in the store. Concurrently, Harry & David has updated its mobile and desktop checkout experiences to improve speed and streamline shipping and payment forms, anticipating significant mobile online traffic. Adobe Inc. estimates U.S. online sales will reach $124.1 billion this holiday season.

title: Why style differentiation matters in US equity investing
summary: This article discusses the importance of style differentiation in US equity investing, particularly highlighting the outperformance of large-cap growth funds over the last five years. It examines how technology stocks have driven this growth, recent reclassifications of S&P 500 sectors, and the debate among investors regarding the sustainability of this trend versus the potential for value-based strategies to rebound, emphasizing the merits of combining growth and value styles.

title: Germany's SAP to Buy Qualtrics for $8 Billion in Cash
summary: SAP SE announced its largest acquisition to date, purchasing Qualtrics International Inc. for $8 billion in cash. This strategic move aims to bolster SAP's presence in the fast-growing customer relations management (CRM) sector, where it competes with rivals like Salesforce.com Inc. The acquisition is seen as an effort to accelerate growth by expanding SAP's software offerings.

title: 10 Things You Didn’t Know About Eli Lilly CEO David Ricks
summary: This article highlights ten interesting facts about David Ricks, the CEO of Eli Lilly and Company. It covers his career trajectory within the company across different countries, his educational background, his previous work experiences outside of pharmaceuticals, and his commitment to community involvement, patient expectations, urgency in healthcare solutions, and transparency.

title: ICF to Launch ICF Next
summary: ICF (NASDAQ:ICFI) is launching ICF Next, an integrated marketing services agency combining ICF’s existing marketing, communications, and technology businesses. Led by John Armstrong, ICF Next will consist of over 1,700 creatives, communicators, strategists, and technologists across multiple global offices. This new entity aims to provide clients with a broader range of expertise in areas like communications, public relations, technology, loyalty, and marketing.

title: Watch AI conjure up an entire city from scratch
summary: Nvidia, in collaboration with the University of California, Berkeley, has developed an AI-powered driving simulator that generates entire cities in real-time. This technology, learned from segmented driver camera footage, showcases the potential for AI to automate virtual world creation, providing a rapid prototyping tool for architects and city planners. It also highlights the future where AI graphics could make digital fiction indistinguishable from reality.

title: Building a More Just Marketplace: The 2018 Rankings of America’s Most JUST Companies
summary: This report details JUST Capital's 2018 rankings of America's Most JUST Companies, identifying corporations that excel in areas important to the American public, such as fair pay, environmental impact, and community support. The rankings highlight that JUST 100 companies outperform their peers financially and socially, with Microsoft topping the list. The article emphasizes how these rankings can guide consumers and investors toward businesses driving a more just economy.

title: Adobe Named The Best Tech Company to Work For in India List
summary: Adobe has been named the top tech company to work for in India in a 2018 report by job search portal Indeed, followed by Nvidia and Microsoft. The report highlights that Indian job seekers prefer large international firms for career advancement and work exposure in the technology sector. Multinational corporations dominated the list, reinforcing the demand for such companies in India's job market.

title: Google, Netflix, Costco ranked among top companies for diversity by workers
summary: Google, Netflix, and Costco have been recognized among the top companies for diversity, according to a survey by Comparably. The ranking defines diversity based on how non-Caucasian employees rate their workplace experiences. A consistent focus on diversity from leadership at all levels contributed to the high marks received by these companies.

title: Ciena Stock Soars After Reporting Upbeat Earnings
summary: Ciena Corp. (CIEN: NYSE) shares surged after the company reported strong fourth-quarter earnings and revenue that exceeded analyst estimates, along with an increase in full-year earnings. The company also announced a new share repurchase program of up to $500 million and its stock has climbed significantly year-to-date, outperforming the S&P 500. CEO Gary B. Smith attributed the success to innovation, market trend interception, and consistent market outperformance.

title: These are the best tech companies to work for in India
summary: A new study by jobs site Indeed identifies the top tech companies to work for in India, revealing that multinational corporations are preferred over homegrown firms. Adobe, Nvidia, and Microsoft lead the list, emphasizing job seekers' desire for clear progression structures and positive workplace environments. The article details the top 15 companies, highlighting their employee ratings, key facts, and offices in India.

title: Adobe acquires Allegorithmic, makers of the Substance texture tools
summary: Adobe has acquired Allegorithmic, the French company behind the Substance texture tools widely used by game creators, VFX artists, animators, and designers. This acquisition aims to integrate Allegorithmic's technologies into Adobe's Creative Cloud tools, enhancing Adobe's focus on 3D design and content creation, especially for augmented reality experiences. Adobe sees 3D design as an increasingly important area for creatives and wants to broaden the accessibility of 3D object creation for various applications, including game development.

title: Adobe acquires Allegorithmic
summary: Adobe has acquired Allegorithmic, the developer of Substance Painter and Substance Designer, for an undisclosed sum, causing significant discussion in the CG industry regarding future licensing and development. The acquisition is seen by Adobe as a way to expand its 3D initiatives and Creative Cloud offerings, while Allegorithmic views it as an opportunity for growth and access to more resources. The move has raised concerns among users about the potential end of perpetual licenses and the pace of software development under Adobe's ownership.

title: John Warnock: Utah’s Engineering Initiative has boosted state’s high-tech boom
summary: John Warnock, co-founder of Adobe Systems Inc., argues that Utah's Engineering Initiative has been a critical factor in the state's high-tech economic boom. The initiative, launched in 2001, has significantly increased engineering and computer science graduates, attracting major company investments and creating high-paying jobs. Warnock emphasizes the need for continued investment to maintain Utah's competitive edge in the tech sector.

title: Global Smart Learning Systems Market 2019-2025 Enhancement
summary: The Global Smart Learning Systems Market is projected to grow between 2019 and 2025, driven by technological enhancements, recent trends, and opportunities. Key market players include Adobe Systems Inc., Smart Technologies, Cisco Systems, and Intel Corporation. The report highlights growth factors such as increased demand for collaborative learning, innovative schools, and e-learning importance, with the Asia Pacific region anticipated to experience the fastest growth.

title: Adobe Closing Israeli R&D Center, Letting Employees Go
summary: Adobe Inc. is closing its Israeli research and development center in Tel Aviv and laying off all 25 employees. This decision is part of Adobe's global strategy to consolidate operations, relocating them to the U.S. The center was originally formed from Marketo's 2013 acquisition of Insightera Ltd., which Adobe subsequently gained in its 2018 acquisition of Marketo.

title: Adobe Releases Patches for Critical Flaws in Photoshop CC and Digital Edition
summary: Adobe has released its March security updates, addressing two critical arbitrary code execution vulnerabilities in Photoshop CC and Digital Editions. These flaws, if exploited, could allow an attacker to take control of affected systems, though Adobe has found no evidence of in-the-wild exploits. Users are urged to update their software to the latest versions to mitigate these risks.

title: Roper Technologies acquires Foundry
summary: Roper Technologies is set to acquire Foundry, a developer of key CG industry tools like Nuke, Modo, Mari, and Katana, from its current owner, Hg, for £410 million in an all-cash transaction. This marks Foundry's third ownership change in eight years, with its value more than doubling since Hg acquired it in 2015. Both companies indicate that the acquisition will allow Foundry to remain independent and continue investing in product development and innovation without immediate changes to management or applications.

title: Mobile Software Firm Movius Raises $45M From JPMorgan Chase, Adds Silicon Valley CEO
summary: Movius, an Atlanta-based mobile communications software provider, has secured $45 million in Series D funding led by JPMorgan Chase & Co., bringing its total equity raised to $91 million. The company also announced the appointment of John Loiacono, a seasoned Silicon Valley technology executive, as its new CEO. Movius plans to use this funding to expand its global enterprise client acquisition, leveraging its cloud-based software that enables secure personal and professional communication on a single mobile device for highly regulated industries.

title: Adobe Loses Copyright Infringement Case to Software Surplus
summary: The Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals ruled against Adobe in a copyright infringement case, affirming that Joshua Christensen's resale of Adobe software through Software Surplus was protected by the "first sale" doctrine. Adobe failed to prove that the transaction was a license rather than a sale, which would have negated Christensen's defense. The court emphasized that copyright holders like Adobe are in a better position to prove licensing agreements.

title: Adobe releases Experience Platform and CDP, iterates Sensei
summary: Adobe has globally released its Customer Experience Platform, an overarching infrastructure for its marketing tools, along with new features across its product suite. The platform includes a new Customer Data Platform (CDP) and an iterated Adobe Sensei, the company's AI/ML technology, designed to provide real-time, personalized customer experiences. These developments highlight Adobe's strategy to unify data and content across various channels and software tools.

title: Omkaram Nalamasu, Applied Materials
summary: This article profiles Dr. Omkaram Nalamasu, Senior Vice President and Chief Technology Officer of Applied Materials, highlighting his role in developing disruptive products and leading strategic investments. It details his extensive background as a world-renowned expert in materials science, including his prior academic and research leadership positions, and his numerous contributions to the fields of optical lithography and polymeric materials science. The piece also notes his election to the U.S. National Academy of Engineering and his involvement on various boards.

title: Adobe transforms their global indirect tax process with ONESOURCE Determination
summary: Adobe significantly streamlined its global indirect tax process by implementing Thomson Reuters' ONESOURCE Determination, turning a "perfect storm" of tax challenges into an opportunity for transformation. This change reduced the time to complete sales tax returns from two weeks to 30 minutes, decreased SAP customizations by 95%, and saved over $1 million by reducing tax management costs. The solution also enabled Adobe to meet new EU e-commerce tax regulations and allowed the tax department to focus more on strategic analysis.

title: The Information Technology Industry: Comparing the cultures of Adobe, Dell, HP, IBM and Intel
summary: This article compares the company cultures of Adobe, Dell, HP, IBM, and Intel across various aspects, including overall culture, CEO and leadership, compensation, perks and benefits, and diversity, based on anonymous employee feedback. Adobe consistently emerges as the top-rated company in nearly all categories. The analysis highlights employee perceptions of strengths and weaknesses within each organization.

title: What’s Changed: 2019 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Experience Platforms
summary: This article reviews the 2019 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Experience Platforms (DXP), highlighting significant changes and market trends. It discusses the evolution of DXP solutions, the growing importance of AI, machine learning, and cloud-based technology, and analyzes the performance and strategies of 17 key vendors evaluated by Gartner. The piece details which companies have gained or lost ground and what new features are driving their positions in the market.

title: HPE opens new headquarters in north San Jose
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise officially unveiled its new headquarters in north San Jose, bringing 1,300 workers to the America Center complex. San Jose Mayor Sam Liccardo lauded HPE's commitment to the community. The company also announced new corporate initiatives, including enhanced parental leave benefits and flexible work options, aiming to attract and retain top talent.

title: Tech Moves: F5 Networks lands former Marketo and J.P. Morgan execs; AI2 adds managing director; Groupon snags REI vet; and more
summary: F5 Networks announced two new senior executives: Mika Yamamoto as EVP and chief marketing & customer experience officer, and Geng Lin as CTO. AI2 appointed Bryan Hale as managing director of its incubator, while Groupon hired REI veteran Craig Rowley as chief marketing officer. The article also details several other notable tech hires and promotions across various Seattle-area companies, including EagleView, Adaptive Biotechnologies, Reverb, Genesys, Olis Robotics, and HNTB Corporation.

title: Adobe's Path From Boxed Software to SaaS Software And The CIO's Role In That Change
summary: This article chronicles Adobe's journey from a boxed-software company to a SaaS-based software company, highlighting the critical role of CIO Cynthia Stoddard in this transformation. Stoddard discusses modernizing technology, prioritizing customer experience, adopting a cloud-centric DNA, and the importance of architecture in managing legacy systems. She also touches on the increasing importance of AI/ML, operational excellence, and developing female talent in IT.

title: Adobe XD streamlines team collaboration with support for design systems
summary: Adobe XD has released a significant update, introducing full support for design systems built on cloud documents. This update allows designers to create reusable colors, character styles, and components that can be centrally managed by a team, pushing updates to everyone simultaneously. New features also include artboard guides, keyboard and gamepad triggers, a polygon tool, enhanced text formatting, and improved permissions and plugin management.

title: How Adobe designer Khoi Vinh is bringing voice controls to app development
summary: Adobe principal designer Khoi Vinh has been recognized as one of Fast Company’s Most Creative People of 2019 for his work in integrating voice controls into app development. He spearheaded a feature in Adobe XD that allows designers to easily add voice commands to app prototypes, leveraging technology from a startup called Sayspring. This innovation aims to enable designers to creatively explore voice interfaces from the early stages of app design.

title: IBM Garage
summary: IBM Garage is a methodology designed to help businesses de-risk and rapidly scale transformations by aligning work to value drivers, tracking progress, and integrating design thinking, agile development, and DevSecOps. It aims to turn ideas into measurable outcomes through co-creation, co-execution, and co-operation phases. The IBM Garage Experience (IGX) platform provides transparency and tools, including generative AI assistants, to prioritize and track initiatives, understand their value, and monitor program health.

title: Meet the billionaires of the new cloud boom
summary: The growth in cloud software has created a new wave of billionaires, with founders of companies like Atlassian, Zoom, Shopify, and Dropbox amassing significant wealth. This trend highlights investors' interest in "land and expand" business models and recurring revenue, making cloud software a fertile ground for new tech giants. While Salesforce's Marc Benioff was once the dominant figure, many new billionaires now rival or exceed his net worth in the cloud industry.

title: Meet the top seven Indian American CEOs
summary: This article highlights seven prominent Indian American CEOs leading major US companies, whose combined 2018 revenue exceeded $360 billion. It notes the historical rise of Indian American executives in corporate America, starting from the 1990s. The CEOs featured include Sundar Pichai (Google), Satya Nadella (Microsoft), Vivek Sankaran (Albertsons), Sanjay Mehrotra (Micron Technology), Ajay Banga (MasterCard), Shantanu Narayan (Adobe Inc.), and Dinesh Paliwal (Harman International Industries).

title: Is Cool Math Games Shutting Down? Here's What You Need to Know
summary: Rumors have been circulating that Cool Math Games, a popular educational gaming website, is shutting down due to Adobe discontinuing Flash support in 2020. However, the company has confirmed that these rumors are false, stating they have been converting games to HTML5 for years and will continue operating. A Change.org petition against the alleged shutdown garnered over 600,000 signatures, highlighting widespread concern.

title: Salesforce swallows Tableau in US$15.3bn buy
summary: Salesforce.com Inc has agreed to acquire big data firm Tableau Software Inc for US$15.3 billion. This acquisition is intended to strengthen Salesforce's position against Microsoft Corp in the market for data analysis and visualization tools, helping businesses target customers more effectively. The deal underscores the growing competition in the market for business intelligence and analytics software.

title: Adobe has an ambitious plan to help the public spot fake images
summary: Adobe is working with UC Berkeley scientists and DARPA to develop AI-powered software capable of detecting, analyzing, and reversing facial manipulation in images. The tool focuses on detecting alterations made with Photoshop's "liquify" warping tool, aiming to democratize image forensics in the face of increasingly sophisticated AI manipulation. While the technology is in early stages, the company acknowledged the urgent need for such tools as AI-driven visual manipulation becomes more seamless and harder to detect.

title: Adobe’s Project Gemini will be called Adobe Fresco, is a new painting app for iPad
summary: Adobe has officially named its "Project Gemini" painting application for iPad as Adobe Fresco. The app, which was first revealed in October 2018, aims to replicate real-world painting effects like oil and watercolor using AI-powered Live Brushes. Adobe Fresco is designed for both professionals and art novices, offering tools like layers, masking, and export to Adobe Photoshop CC or Illustrator.

title: How Fortune 500 Company CDW Fuels Steady Growth by Focusing on Customers
summary: CDW, a Fortune 500 company, sustains steady growth by prioritizing Customer Experience Management (CXM) and leveraging Adobe Experience Cloud solutions. The company uses Adobe Experience Manager Assets as a digital asset management platform to efficiently deliver valuable content and advice to its 250,000 customers. This approach has led to improved content creation, streamlined workflows, and a focus on personalization to enhance customer experiences and foster loyalty.

title: Can Adobe's Digital Experience Revenues Exceed Digital Media Revenues By 2025?
summary: This article examines whether Adobe's Digital Experience revenues can surpass Digital Media revenues by 2025, noting the former's faster growth rate. While Digital Experience is expanding rapidly, driven by market growth and strategic acquisitions, Digital Media is expected to remain the larger segment through 2025 due to its established market share and steady expansion. The analysis provides detailed forecasts and market share projections for both segments.

title: Adobe Breaks Ground on North Tower at San Jose Headquarters
summary: Adobe Systems Inc. has commenced construction on its North Tower in downtown San Jose, which will be the fourth tower at its headquarters. The all-electric tower is designed to house 4,000 employees and will connect to the existing campus via a pedestrian bridge upon its completion in 2022. This expansion also includes new retail space, further solidifying Adobe's presence in San Jose.

title: Puget Systems launches a new FREE app: Adobe Premiere Pro CC Benchmark by Jose Antunes - ProVideo Coalition
summary: Puget Systems has released a new, free Adobe Premiere Pro CC Benchmark application for both Mac and Windows, allowing users to test their computers' performance with the software. This tool, following their After Effects and Photoshop benchmarks, helps users determine how well their current system runs Premiere Pro CC and compare it to results from professional workstations. The benchmark tests live playback and export performance across various codecs, resolutions, and frame rates, and also includes sequences to stress the GPU and CPU.

title: Supermicro Brings Unprecedented Performance and Configurability to the Intelligent Edge for New Security, 5G, and AI Solutions
summary: Supermicro announces new intelligent edge platforms designed for network security, 5G, and AI solutions, offering unprecedented performance and configurability. These solutions support advanced networking software, GPU acceleration, and are powered by Intel Xeon D-2100 series processors, addressing the needs of micro data centers and intelligent edge computing.

title: Craig Cuyar, Omnicom Group technology executive council
summary: This article introduces Craig Cuyar, Senior Vice President and Global Chief Information Officer at Omnicom Group, as a member of CNBC's Technology Executive Council. It highlights his role in global IT transformation, focusing on strategies for optimization, innovation, and revenue growth. The article also details his professional background, achievements, and academic affiliations, including his role as an adjunct Professor at Columbia University.

title: Paycom No. 6 on Fortune’s list of fastest-growing companies
summary: Paycom Software Inc., a provider of cloud-based human capital management software, has been ranked No. 6 on Fortune magazine’s 2019 100 Fastest-growing Companies list. The Oklahoma City-based company, which is the only human capital management provider on the list, also saw its CEO, Chad Richison, recognized among Glassdoor's Top 100 CEOs for 2019. Paycom's strong performance was driven by significant revenue growth, a high employee approval rating, and positive business outlook among its staff.

title: Cognizant helps MG Motor India with Hector SUV sales experience
summary: Cognizant has partnered with MG Motor India to develop an integrated customer experience solution for the Hector SUV, the country's first connected vehicle. This Adobe Experience Cloud-based solution aims to personalize the customer journey from pre-sales to post-sales and enhance the vehicle's unique "internet car" feature. Cognizant also developed an SAP-based enterprise management solution to support the SUV's back-end operations.

title: Gartner Identifies Four Emerging Trends That Will Transform How Marketers Run Their Technology Ecosystems
summary: Gartner has identified four emerging trends in its Hype Cycle for Digital Marketing and Advertising 2019 report that are set to transform how marketers manage their technology ecosystems: Customer Data Platforms (CDPs), AI for Marketing, Blockchain for Advertising, and Real-time Marketing. These technologies are expected to have a significant impact on delivering meaningful customer experiences and driving business value. The report details the current state and future implications of each trend, highlighting ongoing challenges and opportunities for marketers.

title: How Adobe InDesign took over publishing with Steve Jobs' help
summary: This article details how Adobe InDesign, with key assistance from Steve Jobs and strategic decisions, rose to dominate the publishing industry. It chronicles InDesign's competition with QuarkXPress, its integration into the Adobe Creative Cloud, and current challenges related to forced software updates. The piece also reflects on the history of desktop publishing and the rivalries between Adobe, Apple, and Aldus.

title: If you invested $1,000 in eBay 10 years ago, here's how much you'd have now
summary: A $1,000 investment in eBay 10 years ago would now be worth over $4,500, representing a 350% return, significantly outperforming the S&P 500 during the same period. The article discusses eBay's evolution from an auction site, its split from PayPal, and its future plans, while also noting PayPal's stronger stock performance since the separation. It concludes with general investment advice about index funds.

title: Adobe's Omniture purchase a decade ago, set stage for cloud shift, Experience Cloud
summary: This article reflects on Adobe's acquisition of Omniture a decade ago, highlighting how it was a pivotal moment for the company's shift to a cloud and subscription-based model. Initially met with skepticism from Wall Street and even some Omniture employees, CEO Shantanu Narayen's vision of connecting creativity, content, and data laid the groundwork for Adobe's current Experience Cloud and its broader enterprise software strategy. The move, though painful financially at first, ultimately provided a blueprint for other software vendors transitioning to the cloud.

title: Adobe expands its data analytics tools to customer journeys
summary: Adobe has introduced Customer Journey Analytics to its Adobe Analytics offering, aiming to provide companies with deeper insights into customer behavior by integrating data from various enterprise systems. This new tool leverages the Adobe Experience Platform to create comprehensive customer profiles and offers machine learning models for predictive analysis and automation, addressing the challenge of scattered customer data across digital and physical touchpoints. Ray Wang, Principal Analyst at Constellation Research, noted the ambitious scope of the offering in orchestrating processes and building digital feedback loops.

title: Ten years after Adobe bought Omniture, the deal comes into clearer focus
summary: This article reflects on Adobe's acquisition of Omniture ten years prior, highlighting how the $1.8 billion deal was a pivotal moment in Adobe's transformation. It discusses how this acquisition marked Adobe's shift from packaged software to the cloud and from consumer to enterprise focus, integrating content with data. The article emphasizes that what initially baffled many observers ultimately laid the groundwork for Adobe's current success and subscription-based model.

title: Marketo CEO Steve Lucas steps down a year after Adobe acquisition
summary: Marketo CEO Steve Lucas is stepping down from his role at Adobe, one year after the San Jose software giant acquired the company. This departure comes as Marketo's subscription bookings growth in the mid-market did not meet Adobe's expectations, according to Adobe CFO John Murphy.

title: DBX Stock Price, Quote & Chart | DROPBOX INC-CLASS A (NASDAQ:DBX)
summary: This article provides a comprehensive overview of DROPBOX INC-CLASS A (NASDAQ:DBX) stock, including its current price, performance metrics, and key statistics. It also details the company's technical and fundamental analysis ratings from ChartMill, recent earnings report with future estimates, and ownership information, alongside a company profile and list of competitors.

title: Adobe Once Again a Leader in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for Ad Tech, 2019
summary: Adobe has once again been recognized as a Leader in Gartner, Inc.'s Magic Quadrant for Ad Tech, 2019, placing furthest to the right on the "completeness of vision" axis. This recognition is attributed to Adobe's integration of martech and adtech capabilities across the Adobe Experience Cloud, media neutrality, and cross-channel media capabilities. The company highlights customer successes using Advertising Cloud and Adobe Analytics, and plans for future innovations in UI, dashboards, workflows, and media-planning capabilities.

title: What’s Changed: 2019 Gartner Magic Quadrant for CRM Lead Management
summary: This article provides an analysis of the 2019 Gartner Magic Quadrant for CRM Lead Management, highlighting key changes and trends. It discusses the importance of analytics, content management, and sales/marketing coordination in lead management, and evaluates the strengths of 15 significant providers across the four quadrants. The report also notes new entrants, significant movements, and specific functionalities of leading vendors like Oracle, Marketo, Salesforce, and Creatio.

title: Venezuela designers turn to piracy after Adobe announces it will cut service
summary: Venezuelan designers are resorting to piracy following Adobe's decision to cut service to the country due to U.S. sanctions, a move that critics argue demonstrates the unintended consequences of U.S. policy. The disruption affects freelance professionals who rely on Adobe's software amidst Venezuela's economic collapse, prompting calls for exemptions to the sanctions.

title: Adobe shuts down service to Venezuela
summary: Adobe has ceased operations in Venezuela to comply with a US executive order banning trade between the two countries, requiring users to save their documents by October 28. This move has sparked debate over the subscription model for software and raised concerns among human rights advocates about its impact on NGOs and independent media. While Adobe monitors the situation, other major tech companies like Google, PayPal, and Microsoft have not yet followed suit.

title: Adobe Quadruples Lease in Times Square
summary: Adobe Inc. has renewed and significantly expanded its lease at 1540 Broadway in Times Square, signing a ten-year lease for a total of 109,296 square feet across four full floors. This expansion includes renewing its existing 27,323-square-foot space and adding 81,972 square feet. The landlord, Edge Fund Advisors, and co-owner HSBC Alternative Investments, invested $40 million in property upgrades.

title: The deal that ignited Utah’s tech explosion
summary: The 2009 acquisition of Orem-based web analytics company Omniture by Adobe for $1.8 billion is identified as a pivotal moment that ignited Utah's tech explosion. This deal helped fuel the growth of Utah's homegrown tech sector, leading to significant job creation and wage growth in the state. The article also discusses Adobe's subsequent expansion and the broader impact on the "Silicon Slopes" phenomenon, comparing it to later acquisitions like SAP's purchase of Qualtrics.

title: Adobe Inc. Quadruples Manhattan Lease
summary: Adobe Inc. has significantly expanded its presence in Manhattan by quadrupling its lease at 1540 Broadway. The creative software firm renewed its existing lease and added three more floors, bringing its total space to nearly 110,000 square feet in the Class A office tower owned by EDGE Fund Advisors. This move follows a $40 million renovation of the property, highlighting the ongoing "amenity wars" in the commercial real estate market to attract and retain tenants.

title: (P) Adobe Romania: more than 40% of our interns are offered jobs within the company after their internship
summary: Adobe Romania recently concluded its second internship program of the year, where interns showcased their projects at an Interns’ Tech Fair. Over 40% of the interns were offered jobs within the company, highlighting the success of their programs which involve working on real tech challenges with global impact. Adobe Romania offers two main internship programs – a four-month Graduate Diploma Internship and a three-month Summer Internship, both designed to integrate students into engineering teams and develop their skills with mentorship.

title: CRM Watchlist Winners 2019: Once Again, Pegasystems
summary: Pegasystems won the CRM Watchlist 2019 for its strong narrative, product offerings, and overall market impact. The article details Pegasystems' evolution from BPM to customer engagement, highlighting its Pega Infinity platform, its "Empathetic AI" initiative, and suggesting areas for continued impact such as e-commerce integration, refining its vision, and enhancing thought leadership.

title: Adobe exposed data of more than 7 million software users
summary: Adobe Inc. exposed data belonging to 7.5 million of its creative-software customers due to a misconfigured database. The vulnerability, which exposed email addresses, member IDs, and subscription statuses but no financial information or passwords, was addressed on the same day it was discovered. This incident is smaller than a 2013 breach where 38 million user names and encrypted passwords were stolen.

title: Unsecured Adobe Server Exposes Data for 7.5 Million Creative Cloud Users
summary: An unsecured Elasticsearch database belonging to Adobe Creative Cloud exposed personal information for 7.5 million users. The exposed data included email addresses, subscription details, and payment status, but no passwords or credit card numbers. While the vulnerability was swiftly addressed by Adobe, users are advised to be vigilant against targeted phishing attacks.

title: Unsecured ElasticSearch DB exposed data for 7.5M Adobe Creative Cloud Users
summary: An unsecured Elasticsearch database exposed personal data for 7.5 million Adobe Creative Cloud users, including email addresses, account information, and product subscriptions. The leak, discovered by Bob Diachenko and Comparitech, did not include financial data or passwords but could lead to sophisticated phishing attacks. Adobe secured the vulnerability on the same day it was reported but urged users to be vigilant and enable two-factor authentication.

title: Value investing: A deep dive into performance
summary: This article analyzes the significant underperformance of value investing compared to growth investing over the past decade, especially through 2019, contrasting it with the dot-com bubble's aftermath. It examines historical returns, allocation data, and sector weightings (overweighting financials and energy for value; underweighting technology and healthcare), highlighting how these factors contributed to the disparity. The report is part one of a three-part series dissecting value funds' underperformance.

title: VFX Software Market key players - Adobe, Autodesk Inc., SideFX,
summary: The global VFX software market was valued at $559.53 million in 2018 and is projected to reach $1.44 billion by 2026, growing at a CAGR of 12.7%. Key drivers include increased spending on visual effects by the film industry, demand for high-quality video content, and innovations in VFX techniques. The market is also seeing a trend towards incorporating AI and machine learning into VFX software to enhance productivity, with cloud deployment expected to grow significantly.

title: How to Build a $100+ Billion SaaS Company
summary: This article analyzes Salesforce's journey to becoming a $100B+ SaaS company, highlighting key metrics and strategies that growing SaaS companies can emulate. It emphasizes the importance of Net Revenue Retention, upselling a second or third product, going upmarket to target larger customers, and focusing on customer success for long-term growth. The author suggests that while building a $100B+ SaaS company is challenging, it's no longer an impossible dream given the growing number of companies approaching this milestone.

title: Introducing the Content Authenticity Initiative
summary: Adobe announced the Content Authenticity Initiative (CAI) at Adobe MAX 2019, in collaboration with The New York Times Company and Twitter. The initiative aims to develop an industry standard for digital content attribution to ensure trust and transparency online. The CAI seeks to provide consumers with tools to determine content authenticity and invites other companies to join and contribute to this open and extensible framework.

title: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen: We are a content company at our core
summary: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen discusses the company's core identity as a content company and the future outlook for Creative Cloud. He shared these insights during the Adobe Max conference, where new features for Creative Cloud were also highlighted. The interview took place with CNBC's Jon Fortt.

title: Adobe Revenue and Net Income by Quarter
summary: The article presents Adobe Inc.'s revenue and net income by quarter from Q1 1994, highlighting its record-breaking financial performance in fiscal Q1 2020. It details significant financial milestones, such as reaching $500 million in revenue in Q4 2005 and $1 billion in revenue in Q4 2010, alongside a company overview and historical facts.

title: Adobe Revenue by Quarter: FY Q1 1994 – Q1 2020
summary: This article provides a historical overview of Adobe's quarterly revenue from fiscal Q1 1994 to Q1 2020, highlighting key milestones such as reaching $1 billion and $2 billion quarterly revenue marks. It notes Adobe's record-high revenue of $3,091 million in Q1 2020, along with its consistent growth and the significant contribution of subscription services to its total revenue. The article also includes vital information about Adobe Inc.'s history, business segments, and market standing.

title: Adobe Subscription Revenue by Quarter: FY Q1 2009 – Q1 2020
summary: This article details Adobe's subscription revenue performance from FY Q1 2009 to Q1 2020, highlighting its consistent growth and increasing contribution to the company's total revenue. In Q1 2020, subscription services accounted for 91.39% of Adobe's total revenue, reaching a record $2.825 billion. The article also provides a brief history of Adobe Inc., its transformation into a subscription-first model, and key financial milestones.

title: Adobe Quarterly Revenue by Segment: FY Q1 2011 – Q1 2020
summary: This article analyzes Adobe Inc.'s quarterly revenue by segment from FY Q1 2011 to Q1 2020, focusing on Digital Media, Digital Experience, and Publishing. It highlights that Digital Media and Digital Experience segments achieved their highest-ever revenues in Q1 2020, with Digital Media accounting for 70.2% of total revenue. Conversely, the Publishing segment experienced a notable decline in Q1 2020 after reaching a peak in Q1 2019.

title: Adobe Quarterly Revenue by Region: FY Q1 1999 – Q1 2020
summary: This article analyzes Adobe's quarterly revenue by region from FY Q1 1999 to Q1 2020, highlighting that the company achieved its highest-ever quarterly revenue in Q1 2020 across the Americas, EMEA, and APAC regions. It details the revenue contributions from each region, with the Americas accounting for 58.14% of Q1 2020 global revenue, and provides a brief history of Adobe Inc.

title: Adobe Quarterly Subscription Revenue by Segment
summary: The article analyzes Adobe's quarterly subscription revenue by segment from Q1 2013 to Q1 2020. It highlights record-high subscription revenues from the Digital Media and Digital Experience segments in Q1 2020, reaching $2,058 million and $739 million respectively. The Digital Media segment contributed the largest share of total subscription revenue, while the Publishing segment experienced a decline.

title: Number of Adobe Employees Worldwide: FY 1990 - 2019
summary: This article details the growth of Adobe's global employee count from fiscal year 1990 to 2019, reaching 22,634 employees by November 30, 2019. It highlights key milestones in employee growth and provides background information on Adobe Systems Incorporated, including its founding, products, and financial achievements.

title: Digital Interactive Services for All Channel Engagement
summary: This article from Infosys describes their Digital Interactions services, which aim to digitize core operations and orchestrate robust customer experiences across all engagement channels. It highlights how Infosys helps enterprises stay relevant by leveraging advanced UI technologies, future-proofing technology investments, and enhancing time-to-market. The service focuses on building compelling customer experiences, next-gen digital enterprises, and managing digital disruption.

title: The commercial real estate media source.
summary: Adobe Inc. has expanded its lease at 1540 Broadway in Manhattan, renewing its existing 27,323 s/f space and adding an additional 81,972 s/f. The expansion underscores the success of the building's recent $40 million redevelopment, which includes a new amenity floor and other improvements. CBRE represented the ownership, EDGE Fund Advisors, while Colliers TIG represented Adobe Inc.

title: Can All Online Harms Be Tackled Using the Same Regulatory Approach?
summary: This article examines the UK's proposal for regulating online harms and questions whether illegal and lawful harms can be addressed with the same approach. It analyzes four factors (clear legal definitions, tailored technology, known real-world impact, and business consequences) that have proven effective in tackling online child sexual abuse material (CSAM), an illegal harm, and considers their applicability to lawful but harmful content. The author suggests that regulators need to develop clearer definitions, appropriate technologies, evidence bases for real-world impact, and align commercial interests to effectively manage a broader range of online harms.

title: How Shantanu Narayen Leads Adobe To Success
summary: Shantanu Narayen, the Indian-American CEO of Adobe since 2007, is highlighted for his leadership in transforming the company by moving its software franchises to the Cloud. The article details his career progression, educational background, and strategic vision for Adobe, including its focus on AI, ML, and Cloud technologies. Narayen's numerous accolades and current board positions further underscore his influential role in the tech industry.

title: Missouri S&T renews partnership with Adobe
summary: Missouri S&T has renewed its partnership with Adobe Systems Inc. through Adobe's University Outreach Program, providing students and faculty in the technical communication program access to industry-standard Adobe software licenses. This collaboration, which began in 2015, allows students to gain practical experience with essential tools for their future careers, with many students reporting significant benefits and improved skills from using Adobe products in their coursework and projects. Dr. David Wright, associate professor of English and technical communication, expressed enthusiasm for the continued partnership, highlighting the positive impact on students' learning and career readiness.

title: French authorities raid Adobe office over Irish tax claim
summary: French tax officials raided Adobe's Paris office in April 2018, suspecting the US software company of tax evasion by shifting revenue to Ireland. A Paris judge rejected Adobe's lawsuit challenging the legality of the raid, supporting the French authorities' investigation into tax avoidance. This event highlights French efforts to crack down on digital companies using international tax strategies to minimize their tax burden.

title: To grow, Kroger is diversifying beyond grocery
summary: Kroger is expanding beyond traditional grocery sales by diversifying into prepared meals, including ghost kitchens and meal kits, to boost revenue and profit margins. The company is partnering with Clustertruck for delivery-only kitchens and integrating meal kit services like Home Chef into stores and through partnerships with pharmacies like Walgreens. This strategy aims to address consumer demand for convenient, prepared food while navigating the evolving food industry landscape.

title: Adobe Acquires Facebook’s ‘Oculus Medium’ VR Sculpting Tool
summary: Adobe has acquired Oculus Medium, Facebook's VR sculpting tool, for an undisclosed price. Adobe plans to integrate Medium with its Substance team to develop the next generation of 3D tools, ensuring continuity for existing users and making the tool free for new Oculus Touch activations. This move is seen as a strategic win for Adobe's 3D offerings and allows Facebook to divest from a non-core business while preserving the tool's future.

title: Adobe is buying the Oculus Medium VR sculpting app
summary: Adobe is acquiring Oculus Medium, a virtual reality sculpting app originally developed by Facebook-owned Oculus. The app will remain free for Oculus VR system users, with Adobe planning to add more features and improvements in 2020. This acquisition will complement Adobe's existing 3D tools and allows Oculus to focus on other VR art applications like Quill.

title: Microsoft again recognized as a Leader in the 2019 Gartner Content Services Platforms Magic Quadrant Report
summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in Gartner's Content Services Platforms Magic Quadrant for the third consecutive year, placing highest in Ability to Execute. The article attributes this success to innovations in SharePoint and Microsoft 365, focusing on cloud scale, protection, fast time to value, user-centricity, and intelligence. It highlights customer success stories and features like support for 320+ file formats, robust security, AI integration, and the upcoming Project Cortex.

title: SAP best tech workplace in India, followed by Adobe, VMware: Report
summary: SAP has been recognized as the top-rated technology workplace in India for 2019, based on employee ratings and reviews on job site Indeed. Adobe, VMware, and Microsoft followed, with ISRO ranking fifth, moving up from tenth place in the previous year. The survey also highlighted that a significant majority of job-seekers prioritize employer reputation and online company reviews when considering new job opportunities.

title: Gone in a Flash: Adobe to discontinue the plugin by end of 2020
summary: Adobe Flash, a once-ubiquitous software platform for multimedia content, is set to be discontinued at the end of 2020. This decision comes after years of vulnerability to cyber-attacks and the rise of HTML 5, which incorporates multimedia display technology, rendering Flash obsolete. Microsoft will automatically remove Flash via Windows updates, eliminating the need for users to take action.

title: Former Informatica CEO tapped to lead Adobe unit a year after reorganization
summary: Anil Chakravarthy, former CEO of Informatica, has been appointed as the new general manager of Adobe Inc.'s Digital Experience business. This appointment comes a year after the previous general manager, Brad Rencher, left amid a reorganization. Chakravarthy's leadership marks a significant development for Adobe's Digital Experience division.

title: Why Did Gartner Kill the Web Content Management Magic Quadrant?
summary: Gartner announced the retirement of its Web Content Management (WCM) Magic Quadrant due to market maturity and a shift towards Digital Experience Platforms (DXPs). While WCM itself is not dead, the focus has moved to broader content management systems that cater to multiple channels and modalities. The move highlights the evolving landscape of digital experiences, with DXPs emerging as the new focus for integrated solutions.

title: A Perennial Millennial Bet: The Largest Generation, Now in ETF Form
summary: The article discusses the Principal Millennials Index ETF (GENY) as a potentially rewarding investment, especially after the 2020 holiday shopping season, which saw significant online retail growth. GENY tracks companies benefiting from millennial spending habits, including e-commerce, social media, and healthy lifestyles. The pandemic accelerated the shift to online shopping, making GENY a compelling option for investors looking to capitalize on changing consumer trends.

title: IBM’s Arvind Krishna latest among Indians heading global companies
summary: IBM appointed Arvind Krishna as its new CEO, adding him to a growing list of Indian-origin executives leading major global corporations. The article highlights several prominent Indian-origin CEOs, including Sundararajan Pichai of Google, Satya Nadella of Microsoft, Shantanu Narayen of Adobe, and Ajaypal Singh Banga of MasterCard, detailing their backgrounds and career achievements. Krishna's appointment underscores the significant influence of Indian-origin talent in the global business landscape.

title: Target CIO leads retailer's digital customer experience tech makeover
summary: Target's CIO, Michael McNamara, discusses how the company rebuilt its technology stack after a 2013 data breach to refine its digital customer experience. He highlights Target's in-house tech development strategy, its approach to utilizing customer data, and the merging of physical and digital shopping experiences, such as the popular drive-up service. McNamara also touches on the challenges of creating inspiring online experiences for different product categories and the slow maturation of new technologies like RFID and autonomous vehicles.

title: In Pictures: Indian-origin CEOs of top tech companies
summary: This article showcases prominent Indian-origin CEOs leading top technology companies globally. It highlights individuals like Arvind Krishna (IBM), Sundar Pichai (Google, Alphabet), Satya Nadella (Microsoft), Shantanu Narayen (Adobe), Jayshree Ullal (Arista Networks), Sanjay Mehrotra (Micron), George Kurian (NetApp), and Sandeep Mathrani (WeWork), detailing their backgrounds and roles within their respective companies as of early 2020. The article emphasizes the growing presence and influence of Indian-origin leaders in the tech industry.

title: Tech pioneer to speak at 2020 general convocation
summary: John Warnock, co-founder of Adobe Inc. and a University of Utah alumnus, is scheduled to deliver the commencement address at the university's 2020 general convocation. Warnock, who holds multiple degrees from the U, revolutionized graphics, desktop publishing, and web technologies through his work at Adobe. He is celebrated for his visionary intellect, entrepreneurial spirit, and significant contributions to digital document and graphics technologies, including the creation of PostScript and PDF.

title: Odisha Tourism's digital transformsation becomes a case study for Adobe
summary: Odisha Tourism's digital transformation has been selected as a case study by software giant Adobe Systems. This recognition highlights the implementation of the Adobe stack in the government sector and follows Odisha Tourism's revamped website and leveraging of the 5T governance framework to enhance its digital presence. Vishal Dev, secretary (tourism & sports affairs), led this transition.

title: Adobe Celebrates 20 Years on Fortune’s List of 100 Best Companies to Work For
summary: Adobe celebrates its 20th consecutive year on Fortune Magazine's list of 100 Best Companies to Work For. This year, Adobe ranked 35th, attributing its success to its employees and a culture focused on people, purpose, and pride. The article highlights 20 employee-contributed reasons for loving Adobe, covering aspects like diversity, work-life balance, benefits, and innovation.

title: PugetBench for Adobe Creative Cloud: a complete benchmark for Adobe CC Suite by Jose Antunes - ProVideo Coalition
summary: Puget Systems has launched "PugetBench for Adobe Creative Cloud," a new initiative offering comprehensive and consistent benchmark testing for popular Adobe applications such as Photoshop, Lightroom Classic, After Effects, and Premiere Pro. These benchmarks are designed to assess hardware performance using real-world projects and workflows. While free versions are available for personal use, a commercial license for the full suite, including advanced features, is priced at $2,000 USD.

title: Coronavirus Forces $600 Billion Higher Education Industry Online
summary: The COVID-19 pandemic is forcing the $600 billion higher education industry online as campuses shutter. Universities are scrambling to shift to distance learning, with professors like Analisa Packham at Vanderbilt University quickly adapting to new technologies for teaching and holding virtual office hours. This abrupt transition highlights the challenges and rapid adjustments required for both educators and students in the new remote learning environment.

title: 17 Stocks With Fortress Balance Sheets
summary: This article identifies 17 stocks with strong balance sheets, dubbed "fortress balance sheets," that are well-positioned to withstand economic downturns and are currently undervalued. It highlights three specific companies—Coca-Cola, Honeywell, and Public Service Enterprise Group—providing detailed analyst commentary on their financial resilience and market positioning amidst the COVID-19 pandemic. The piece serves as a guide for investors looking for sturdy companies during market uncertainty.

title: Silicon Valley Salesforce rival hires CFO who helped take Zuora public
summary: Freshworks, a Salesforce rival based in San Mateo, has hired Tyler Sloat as its new CFO. Sloat previously served as CFO at Zuora and helped take the company public. Freshworks aims to increase its revenue from over $200 million to $1 billion in the next few years with Sloat's financial leadership.

title: Adobe Copyright Suit Over ColdFusion
summary: Adobe, Inc. has filed a copyright infringement and breach of software license agreement lawsuit against Silk Road Technology, Inc. The suit alleges that Silk Road used Adobe's ColdFusion product, a website-building software, to offer services to third parties, which is a violation of the licensing terms. Adobe is seeking actual damages from Silk Road Technology.

title: 2020 ESG trends to watch
summary: This article outlines five key ESG trends anticipated to unfold in 2020, signaling a shift towards more impactful sustainable investing. These trends include identifying climate change innovators among established companies, integrating ESG criteria into capital terms, re-evaluating real estate based on climate risks and regulations, managing human capital through layoffs and talent shortages, and increasing stakeholder accountability in corporate governance.

title: Adobe announces AI toolbox for Experience Platform
summary: Adobe has launched five pre-packaged AI solutions for its Experience Platform, designed to help marketers leverage vast amounts of data for better customer insights and predictive optimization. These tools, including Customer AI and Attribution AI, aim to simplify the process of understanding customer behavior and optimizing marketing strategies. The company plans to expand this AI toolbox based on customer needs for improved marketing outcomes.

title: Adobe Named a LEADER in The Forrester Wave™: B2C and B2B Commerce Suites
summary: Adobe has been recognized as a Leader in both "The Forrester Wave™: B2C Commerce Suites, Q2 2020" and "The Forrester Wave™: B2B Commerce Suites, Q2 2020" reports, following its acquisition of the Magento platform. Adobe was the only vendor to achieve a rating of 3 or higher out of 5 in all criteria across both Waves, highlighting its strong strategy, current offering, and market presence in the commerce suite space. The company's Magento Commerce platform, part of Adobe Experience Cloud, is praised for its flexibility, rich functionality, and ability to deliver differentiated customer experiences for both B2C and B2B businesses.

title: Adobe Product Revenue by Quarter: FY Q1 2002 – Q1 2020
summary: The article analyzes Adobe's product revenue by quarter from FY Q1 2002 to Q1 2020, highlighting a significant decline in product revenue and its share of total revenue. Adobe's product revenue hit an all-time low of $143 million in Q1 2020, a 16.16% YoY decline, while the share of product revenue fell drastically from 81.98% in Q1 2010 to just 4.63% in Q1 2020, largely due to the rise of subscription revenue. The article also provides a brief overview of Adobe Inc., its history, key products, and financial milestones.

title: Adobe Quarterly Digital Media Revenue by Offerings
summary: Adobe's Digital Media segment achieved an all-time high revenue in fiscal Q1 2020, with Creative Cloud reaching $1,818 million and Document Cloud hitting $351 million. The Creative Cloud services accounted for 83.8% of the Digital Media revenue, showing significant year-over-year growth. The article details Adobe's revenue streams, company history, and market standing.

title: Software piracy: HC awards Rs 30 lakh damages to software giants Microsoft, Adobe
summary: The Delhi High Court has awarded Rs 30 lakh in damages to Microsoft Corporation, Adobe Systems, and Quest Software, restraining a Delhi-based company, Chetu, from using or distributing their pirated software. The court's decision came after an investigation revealed Chetu was using unlicensed software despite claiming to be a Microsoft certified partner. The awarded damages are to be equally shared among the three software giants.

title: Evolving Our Brand Identity
summary: Adobe is updating its brand identity, including a new single-color, all-red logo for the corporate brand with a warmer hue. The company is also refining the Creative Cloud, Document Cloud, and Experience Cloud logos to improve legibility, accessibility, and consistency across its extensive product portfolio. These changes aim to make it easier for customers to navigate and understand Adobe's offerings while maintaining a fresh look and feel.

title: Adobe Sign enhances e-signatures for healthcare and life sciences
summary: Adobe Sign has introduced new enhancements specifically for the healthcare and life sciences industries, focusing on compliance with regulations like the US FDA's 21 CFR Part 11. These updates simplify the e-signature process for critical documents, helping remote teams maintain productivity and meet stringent industry standards. The enhancements include improved Fill & Sign features and expanded IT admin settings, supported by a collaboration with Montrium for compliance documentation.

title: Adobe’s CEO on how the company is posting record results amid economic chaos
summary: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen discusses how the company is achieving record results despite economic challenges, attributing success to the rapid acceleration of digital transformation trends during the pandemic. Adobe reported record quarterly revenue of $3.13 billion, a 14% year-over-year increase, and has not laid off any employees. Narayen highlights the shift to remote work, the increasing demand for digital services like Adobe Sign, and a substantial total addressable market across its core businesses.

title: Adobe stock closes just shy of record as coronavirus work-from-home trend boosts subscriptions
summary: Adobe Inc. shares reached an all-time high during Friday's trading session, closing just shy of their record. The increase is attributed to the company's ability to capitalize on the work-from-home trend during the COVID-19 pandemic, leading most analysts to raise their price targets for the stock.

title: Adobe stock closes just shy of record as coronavirus work-from-home trend boosts subscriptions
summary: Adobe Inc. shares reached their highest price ever during Friday's trading session, closing just shy of a record. This surge is attributed to the company's ability to capitalize on the work-from-home trend amid the COVID-19 pandemic, leading most analysts to raise their price targets for the stock. Adobe shares closed up 4.9% at $406.54, nearly matching its record high closing price.

title: Juneteenth emerges as company holiday as Nike, Citigroup and others commemorate black history
summary: An increasing number of U.S. companies, including Nike, Citigroup, Twitter, and Uber, are recognizing Juneteenth as a paid company holiday to commemorate the liberation of slaves after the Civil War. This move follows the death of George Floyd and a growing call for racial justice, with experts suggesting observing Juneteenth can improve perception among black workers and consumers. While it presents a risk of becoming just another day off, companies are encouraged to use the day to learn about their historical connections to slavery.

title: Adobe Flash Player ends officially on December 31, 2020
summary: Adobe announced that support for its Flash Player will officially end on December 31, 2020. Users are advised to uninstall the software by then, as Flash Player downloads will be removed from Adobe's website, and Flash-based content will be blocked from running after the end-of-life date. Major browser vendors like Google and Mozilla are also removing Flash support from their products.

title: Monday.com rolls out app framework to connect work across the enterprise
summary: Monday.com has launched a new low-code app framework and integrations as part of its mission to become the "Work OS for enterprise." This framework aims to connect various teams and tools across an organization, transcending traditional project and task management. It allows for the creation of customized workflows and integrations with third-party applications, providing a centralized platform for communication and collaboration.

title: Marc Benioff's Salesforce has eclipsed Larry Ellison's Oracle in market cap
summary: Salesforce's market capitalization has surpassed Oracle's, marking a significant milestone in the long-standing rivalry between Marc Benioff and Larry Ellison. Salesforce, under Benioff, has grown by diversifying its offerings and validating its internet-delivered software model, achieving profitability and expanding its market share even as some analysts suggest avenues for greater profit margins. This development highlights Salesforce's success despite its younger age compared to Oracle, a company that Ellison acknowledged missing the initial cloud trend.

title: Kalyan Kumar B., HCL Technologies
summary: Kalyan Kumar B. is the Corporate Vice President and Global CTO of HCL Technologies, leading their Global Cloud Native Services and DRYiCE product divisions. He drives the company's product and technology strategy in emerging technologies, focusing on thought leadership, incubation initiatives with startups, and new technology alliances, particularly in autonomics/AI and service-orchestration. Beyond his corporate role, Kumar is an author, a member of the Wall Street Journal CIO Network, and actively promotes an inclusive world through his rock band, 'Contraband,' and by supporting cricket through Capital Kids Cricket.

title: Adobe Issues July 2020 Critical Security Patches for Multiple Software
summary: Adobe has released security updates for five of its widely used applications, patching a total of 13 vulnerabilities, including four critical and nine important ones. These patches address issues like arbitrary file system write attacks, arbitrary code execution, and privilege escalation in products such as Creative Cloud, Media Encoder, Genuine Service, ColdFusion, and Download Manager. Adobe recommends users install these updates immediately, despite no public disclosure or in-the-wild exploitation of the vulnerabilities.

title: CIOs Face Their Next Biggest Challenge
summary: The COVID-19 pandemic has created significant challenges and opportunities for CIOs, accelerating digital transformation across industries. Key challenges identified include security issues, keeping up with changing customer expectations, and modernizing legacy systems. The article emphasizes the crucial role of data privacy, AI, and enhanced collaboration between IT and other departments for businesses to adapt and thrive in this evolving landscape.

title: Adobe Australia a Best Place to Work 8th Year in a Row
summary: Adobe Australia has been recognized as a Best Place to Work for the eighth consecutive year by the Great Place to Work Institute, ranking 13th in the 100-999 employee category. The company, with over 400 employees, attributes its sustained recognition to a strong, people-first culture and core values that have enabled it to support employees, customers, and the community, even during challenging times. Initiatives like a 'Work From Home' fund and COVID time-off, alongside employee networks, underscore Adobe's commitment to employee well-being and engagement.

title: New in July 2020: Playback Improvements for H.264 and HEVC on Windows
summary: Adobe has released updates for Premiere Pro and After Effects, significantly improving H.264 and HEVC playback performance on Windows Intel systems, offering up to a 2X speed increase due to collaboration with Intel. The update also introduces ProRes 4444 XQ export for macOS, previously Windows-only, and features the Roto Brush 2 in After Effects Beta, which uses Adobe Sensei for faster rotoscoping. These enhancements are part of Adobe's ongoing efforts to boost application quality and performance through strategic partnerships.

title: How Quality Engineering Intern, Michell Brito, is Making Products Accessible to Everyone
summary: Michell Brito, a Quality Engineering Intern at Adobe from Texas A&M University, is working to enhance product accessibility for Behance. Her project involves developing infrastructure for automated accessibility testing, aiming to ensure new features are accessible to all users. Despite the virtual nature of her internship, Brito has found the experience positive, praising Adobe's virtual events and supportive team communication.

title: Meeting The Moment: HPE CEO Antonio Neri’s Year Of Extraordinary Leadership
summary: This article highlights HPE CEO Antonio Neri's leadership during a tumultuous year marked by the COVID-19 pandemic and social unrest. It details his commitment to partners, the acceleration of HPE's edge-to-cloud Platform-as-a-Service strategy with GreenLake, and his focus on the company's culture. Partners commend Neri's authentic leadership, his financial support for the channel, and his vision for the future of hybrid cloud.

title: Photo Editing App Market to See Booming Growth | Adobe, Google LLC, Lens Distortions, LLC.
summary: The global Photo Editing App Market is projected for booming growth, driven by the increasing professional photography career paths, rising digital transformation, and demand for special effects in media. Key players analyzed in the market study include Adobe Inc., Google LLC, and Corel Corporation. The report provides an overview of market insights, trends, growth factors, and the impact of COVID-19 on the industry.

title: Communist China puts our technology and safety at risk every day
summary: The article highlights the severe threat posed by the Chinese Communist Party (CCP) to U.S. technology and national security through cyberattacks, data theft, and intellectual property infringement. Authors Rep. Adam Kinzinger and Rep. John Joyce detail instances like "Operation Aurora" and the OPM breach, emphasize the dangers of applications like TikTok, and underscore the CCP's efforts to steal COVID-19 vaccine research. They advocate for congressional action, including proposals from the China Task Force, to bolster defenses, protect American research, and secure telecommunications against Chinese technological overreach to safeguard national security and human rights.

title: Adobe Experience Manager review
summary: Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) is a high-end platform designed to streamline digital content management for large organizations, offering advanced features for websites, forms, and digital assets. While its significant licensing and setup costs might deter midsize businesses, its intuitive interface, AI-powered tools, and robust functionality make it an attractive solution for enterprise-level operations. The platform comprises AEM Sites, Forms, and Assets, all running on Adobe Cloud Service, providing comprehensive solutions for content creation, data collection, and asset management.

title: The Best Free Photoshop Alternatives
summary: This article reviews several free alternatives to Adobe Photoshop, catering to users who want powerful image editing without a subscription fee. It covers options like Adobe Photoshop Express for basic edits, GIMP for a comprehensive desktop experience, browser-based Pixlr for a Photoshop-like interface, Polarr for general edits and mobile use, and Paint.NET for Windows users seeking a simpler interface than GIMP.

title: CAI Achieves Milestone: White Paper Sets the Standard for Content Attribution
summary: The Content Authenticity Initiative (CAI) has released a white paper titled "Setting the Standard for Content Attribution," addressing the growing problem of inauthentic media. The paper proposes an industry-standard solution for content attribution, enabling creators to securely attach their identity and information to their work, thereby increasing transparency and combating misleading content. This system aims to provide cryptographic verification of media provenance from creation to consumption, ensuring trust and crediting creators.
authors: Andy Parsons|Leonard Rosenthol|Eric Scouten|Jatin Aythora|Bruce MacCormack|Paul England|Marc Levallee|Jonathan Dotan|Sherif Hanna|Hany Farid|Sam Gregory

title: Adobe CTO Abhay Parasnis makes Fast Company’s “Most Creative People in Business” list
summary: Adobe CTO Abhay Parasnis has been recognized by Fast Company on its "Most Creative People in Business" list for his innovative contributions, particularly in applying AI to new challenges and expanding Adobe's creative product offerings. The article highlights his role in developing products like Adobe Aero and Adobe Photoshop Camera, emphasizing his vision for democratizing creativity. It also mentions the recognition of Adobe Research as an "Innovative Team of the Year" for fostering breakthroughs and incorporating cutting-edge technology into Adobe's tools.

title: Adobe announces next milestone on vision for digital modernization in government
summary: Adobe plans to pursue FedRAMP Moderate authorization for Adobe Sign, which will enable government agencies to use e-signatures for processes involving personal information. This initiative aligns with the 21st Century IDEA to modernize IT systems and improve citizen services, especially pertinent as the COVID-19 pandemic accelerates the need for digital transformation in government operations. Adobe aims to help agencies maintain continuity, enable remote work, and deliver services efficiently through its FedRAMP authorized solutions.

title: Leading Hong Kong and Global Companies Selected SAP Procurement Solutions to Transition to New Normal
summary: SAP announced that numerous leading companies globally and in Hong Kong have adopted SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass solutions to streamline their procurement processes, manage costs, reduce risk, and maintain supply chain integrity amidst economic challenges. Companies like Techtronic Industries, Carlsberg Group, and Vestas Wind Systems implemented these solutions to enhance digital transformation, improve spend visibility, and ensure business continuity. The increasing adoption underscores the critical role of digital procurement in navigating supply chain disruptions and fostering efficiency.

title: Is it Finally the End of Adobe Flash Player?
summary: Adobe Flash Player, once vital for interactive web content, has become a major security risk with over a thousand vulnerabilities identified. Despite its End of Life announcement for December 31, 2020, threat actors continue to exploit Flash-related weaknesses and scams. Organizations are strongly advised to completely remove Flash Player from their systems, rather than just patching it, to mitigate significant security risks, especially with remote workforces.

title: Innovation unwrapped: Joy Kim
summary: This article features an interview with Joy Kim, a Research Scientist/Engineer at Adobe, discussing her work in social systems and creativity support tools.
