She shares insights into human-computer interaction, her "Mechanical Novel" project, and her perspective on innovation as balancing newness with usefulness. Joy also talks about her personal creative outlet through her Etsy store, JojostoryArt, and offers advice on fostering creativity in daily life.

title: CAFC Issues Writ of Mandamus Directing Judge Albright to Transfer Case to the Northern District of California
summary: The CAFC issued a writ of mandamus, for the first time, overturning Judge Alan Albright’s denial of a transfer motion in In re Adobe Inc. The CAFC found that Judge Albright committed several errors in his decision, including failing to give proper weight to convenience factors and overlooking the willing witness factor. This decision marks the first transfer of a case out of Waco since Judge Albright's confirmation in 2018.

title: How Allegion turns users into fans with its unique approach to UX design
summary: Allegion, a global security company, has redefined its UX team as the Digital Product Design team to clarify roles and standardize design delivery across its 30+ brands. The article highlights their user-centric approach, focusing on context-first design, and their implementation of a company-wide design system called Phoenix using Adobe XD. This system involves close collaboration between designers and developers to ensure consistent, high-quality digital products.

title: August NVIDIA Studio Driver Now Ready for Download — Brings Support for Adobe Premiere Rush, Premiere Pro and More Creative App Updates
summary: NVIDIA has released its August Studio Driver, which offers support for new updates across a range of creative applications including Adobe Premiere Rush, Premiere Pro, Blender, V-Ray 5 for Maya, Flicker Free, and BorisFX Optics. Key features include AI-powered tools in Adobe apps like Auto Reframe and Scene Edit Detection, OptiX acceleration for all NVIDIA GPUs in Blender, and performance enhancements in V-Ray 5 and Flicker Free. The update aims to improve productivity for creators, especially those working remotely, with increased speed and new functionalities.

title: Wix Introduces Editor X, Offers Designers Advanced Tools for Building Custom Websites
summary: Wix has launched Editor X, a new platform specifically designed for web designers and digital agencies, offering advanced tools for building custom websites with a low-code experience. Editor X aims to address the limitations of Wix's previous offerings for advanced designers by providing cutting-edge responsive design, flexible grids, and advanced CSS editing capabilities. While the platform boasts numerous features for design, development, business solutions, and marketing, its performance (page speed) is noted as a significant drawback.

title: Energy company NRG drives 10x lift in leads with better personalization
summary: NRG Energy, an energy company serving over 3 million customers, has significantly boosted its lead generation and customer engagement through a modernized digital strategy. By leveraging Adobe Experience Cloud, NRG implemented a personalization strategy that resulted in a 5x increase in engagement, a 2x bump in conversion, and a 10x lift in leads. The company moved from a static website to a dynamic platform built on Adobe Experience Manager, and uses Adobe Analytics and Adobe Target to understand customer needs and deliver tailored content across various channels.

title: Wintrust funds $3.3B and approximately 11,000 loans with the help of Adobe
summary: Wintrust Financial Corp. utilized Adobe Experience Manager (AEM) and Adobe Campaign to quickly build an online portal for the Small Business Association’s (SBA) Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) during the COVID-19 pandemic. This rapid deployment allowed Wintrust to process 11,000 loans totaling $3.3 billion, significantly aiding small to midsize businesses. The efficiency and scalability provided by Adobe's platforms were crucial in handling the surge in loan applications and ensuring timely communication with customers.

title: Strengthening the marketing-IT partnership for greater digital success
summary: This article emphasizes the growing importance of collaboration between marketing and IT departments, particularly in the financial services industry, to achieve digital success. It highlights that meeting customer expectations and launching new digital offerings are top priorities for both functions, necessitating a unified approach to data and technology. The piece uses TD Ameritrade as a case study to illustrate how aligning technology initiatives with business use cases and fostering organizational change can lead to a stronger partnership and successful digital transformation.

title: The State of Exploit Development: 80% of Exploits Publish Faster than CVEs
summary: Unit 42 research analyzed 45,450 public exploits and found that 80% are published before their corresponding CVEs, with an average of 23 days prior. This study highlights the critical need for timely patching, as the risk of exploitation significantly increases after vendors release patches, and many exploits are available even before vulnerabilities are officially disclosed, often due to delays in CVE publication. The research underscores that timely patching is crucial for continuous business operation and security.

title: Salesforce Beats SAP, Oracle, Microsoft, Adobe—Combined!—in 2019 CRM Share
summary: Salesforce's strong Q2 performance continued its dominance in the CRM market, with its 2019 market share surpassing that of SAP, Oracle, Microsoft, and Adobe combined, according to IDC data. This achievement is particularly notable as Microsoft was included in the comparison for the first time. The company's "Platform and Other" business unit emerged as its largest revenue driver, highlighting a significant shift in its internal hierarchy, and its market capitalization now exceeds Oracle's and is nearly as large as SAP's.

title: Genesys Announces Partnership with Adobe to Deliver Contextual Customer Experiences
summary: Genesys has partnered with Adobe to integrate Genesys Cloud with Adobe Experience Platform, aiming to improve customer experiences by breaking down data silos between marketing, commerce, sales, and service departments. This collaboration will enrich customer profiles in real-time, enabling more personalized engagements, augmenting customer journeys with AI and automation, and driving revenue through better campaign conversion and attribution. The goal is to provide a more complete view of customers, fostering stronger empathic connections and brand loyalty.

title: Releases Recap: August 24 – 28
summary: This article provides a recap of new releases and updates on the Oculus Platform for the week of August 24-28, 2020. It highlights key titles like Facebook Horizon (invite-only beta), Shooty Fruity, HouseFlipper VR, Medium by Adobe, and Yupitergrad, detailing their features, target audience, developers, and availability. Additionally, it lists upcoming live events in Oculus Venues.

title: What Enabled Adobe to Raise Its Digital Media ARR?
summary: Adobe's Digital Media ARR increased due to the continued migration of its Creative Suite and the addition of new Creative Cloud subscriptions. The company saw significant growth in its Digital Media segment, with Creative Cloud revenues rising 44% year-over-year, leading Adobe to raise its fiscal 2016 Digital Media ARR guidance to $4.0 billion. Fiscal 1Q16 was the last quarter Adobe provided specific Creative Cloud subscriber figures.

title: Pullback Brings Stocks Closer to Fair Value
summary: The market experienced its largest single-day decline since June 11, primarily driven by a sell-off in the technology sector, which was deemed overvalued. While the overall market remains overvalued, this pullback has brought stock valuations closer to fair value. The energy sector, in contrast, appears significantly undervalued, offering potential opportunities for long-term investors.

title: Network Security Firm Qualys Floats to Top of Cloud Computing
summary: Qualys, a network security firm founded in 1999, is positioned for significant growth in the cloud computing sector, serving nearly 40 Fortune 100 companies among its 4,000 customers. The company, known for its vulnerability assessment and credit card database security, offers the advantage of instantly adding new services and scaling based on customer needs without major capital expenditure. Qualys reinvests 25% of its revenue into R&D and has successfully navigated market challenges as a software-as-a-service pioneer.

title: QualysGuard Patch Report Aligns IT Security with Operations
summary: Qualys announced a new Patch Report feature within QualysGuard Vulnerability Management, designed to provide actionable patching information for non-security IT staff. This report offers prioritized lists of necessary patches to reduce risk efficiently. Joe Bennett of CredAbility praised the new feature for improving vulnerability remediation and tracking patch effectiveness, with support for Microsoft and Adobe patches already included.

title: Adobe X Keith Haring Sparks a new wave of creativity with Digitized Keith’s Brushes
summary: Adobe has launched a new "Creativity For All" campaign, in collaboration with Isobar - Dentsu McGarryBowen Amsterdam and the Keith Haring Studio, to democratize art by digitizing Keith Haring's tools. These digitized brushes are now available in Photoshop, Illustrator, and Fresco, inviting creatives to use art to raise awareness for social issues, continuing Haring's legacy of using art for social commentary. The campaign features a hero film by artist Dima Maseykin and music by Antfood, and encourages global participation from both renowned and emerging artists.

title: Inside Adobe’s employee-focused COVID-19 response roadmap
summary: Adobe successfully transitioned its 22,000+ global workforce to remote work within a single weekend during the COVID-19 crisis, leveraging previous contingency planning experience and existing cloud infrastructure. The company prioritized employee experience by enhancing collaboration tools, implementing a Zero-Trust Enterprise Network (ZEN) for secure access, and providing "desktop as a service" solutions. This focus on employee well-being and productivity ensured business continuity and a smooth transition to long-term remote work.

title: Introducing the Adobe Certified Service Partner for Video and Audio program
summary: Adobe has launched the Adobe Certified Service Partner for Video and Audio program to help systems integrators provide expert support, workflow design, and software integrations for Adobe Creative Cloud users. This program offers training and certification, enabling partners to better serve clients and gain new business opportunities through Adobe's promotional efforts. Initial partners like MoovIT and Support Partners, along with new additions Jigsaw24 and Qvest Media, are already leveraging this certification to deliver more comprehensive services to media companies worldwide.

title: CMO Of The Week: American Express’ Elizabeth Rutledge
summary: Elizabeth Rutledge, CMO at American Express for nearly 30 years, discusses how her deep customer knowledge guides the brand, especially during the COVID-19 crisis. She highlights initiatives like "Stand for Small" and stresses the importance of empathy, virtual events, small business support, and the ongoing fight for racial equality.

title: Major John Deere acquisition includes 21 dealerships in Texas
summary: Tellus Equipment Solutions, LLC, a newly formed company, has acquired Ag-Pro Texas, LLC, including all 21 of its full-service John Deere dealerships in central and south Texas. This acquisition makes Tellus Equipment one of the largest John Deere agriculture and turf dealers in North America. Jeff Donaldson has joined Tellus Equipment as chief operating officer to lead sales, customer service, marketing, and operations.

title: Global Audio and Video Editing Software Market- Featuring Acon AS, Adobe Inc., Animoto Inc., and Apple Inc. Among Others
summary: The global audio and video editing software market is projected to grow by USD 2.5 billion between 2020 and 2024, with a CAGR exceeding 11%. This growth is primarily driven by the increasing rate of content generation and a shift towards cloud-based delivery models. The report provides a comprehensive analysis including market sizing, forecast, trends, growth drivers, challenges, and vendor analysis, covering companies such as Adobe Inc., Apple Inc., and Microsoft Corp.

title: Epic is not EXACTly the Microsoft of Madison
summary: This article argues that while Epic Systems Corp. is often compared to Microsoft in terms of its regional economic impact, the comparison is flawed. The author, through Erik Iverson of WARF, suggests that Exact Sciences, due to its publicly traded nature and broad employee stock options, is more akin to Madison's Microsoft, creating localized wealth. The article also details Epic's private stock plan for employees, noting its differences from Microsoft's early model.

title: COVID-19 Recovery Analysis: Automotive Software
summary: Technavio reports that the automotive software market is expected to grow by $10.70 billion between 2020 and 2024, with a CAGR exceeding 9%, despite the COVID-19 pandemic. This growth is primarily driven by the increased adoption of Advanced Driver-Assistance Systems (ADAS) in mass-produced vehicles. The report provides an in-depth analysis of market trends, drivers, and the competitive landscape, highlighting key market participants and potential challenges like increasing software complexity.

title: Exclusive: Behind the scenes of Unity’s unique IPO in the middle of a pandemic
summary: This article provides a behind-the-scenes look at Unity Software Inc.'s initial public offering during the COVID-19 pandemic. CEO John Riccitiello recounts the chaotic 24 hours leading up to the IPO, which included a virtual bell-ringing and overcoming a crucial WiFi outage that threatened their carefully planned webcast. Despite the challenges, Unity shares opened more than 30% higher, marking a significant first-day pop.

title: Adobe finally figured out how to make PDFs make sense on a phone
summary: Adobe has introduced "Liquid Mode" in its Acrobat Reader for iOS and Android, a new feature designed to intelligently reformat PDFs for small screens. This innovation addresses the long-standing problem of reading fixed-layout documents on smartphones by making them scrollable, much like web pages, leveraging AI to deconstruct and reassemble document content. While still in its early stages and not supporting all PDF types, Liquid Mode aims to make reading PDFs on mobile devices a much more pleasant experience and represents a significant rethinking of Acrobat's core value proposition.

title: Future of Sales 2025: Why B2B Sales Needs a Digital-First Approach
summary: The article discusses the accelerated shift towards digital-first B2B sales, predicting that by 2025, 80% of sales interactions will occur in digital channels. It emphasizes the need for sales leaders to adopt a digital mindset, diversify digital selling tools, and rethink sales enablement to cater to evolving customer buying preferences. The transformation necessitates adaptive systems to optimize digital interactions and maintain relevance.

title: Bring your motion design & video visions to life at Adobe MAX
summary: Adobe MAX is offering a dedicated Video track for those interested in motion design, video editing, and animation. The track features sessions tailored for various skill levels, from shooting videos on smartphones to advanced After Effects animation and Premiere Pro workflows. Attendees can learn to create engaging content, refine post-production techniques, and streamline their creative processes.

title: Delivering 3D Creators' Visions to Customers the Way They Intended
summary: Sony has developed the Eye-sensing Light Field Display (ELFD) to overcome the limitations of conventional 3D displays by offering a glasses-free, high-definition 3D experience with real-time eye-tracking and rendering. This technology aims to accurately reproduce creators' 3D visions and is being explored for applications in product design, entertainment, and various B2B fields, with ongoing efforts to expand its capabilities for larger systems and multiple viewers.
authors: Kazuki Yokoyama|Takayuki Kurihara|Takaomi Kimura|Ban Kawamura|Thaisa Yamamura

title: Tractor Supply Co. shows resilience during COVID-19, taps Adobe Experience Cloud to drive growth
summary: Tractor Supply Company, an essential business operating over 1,900 stores, experienced significant growth during COVID-19, with net sales up 35% and triple-digit e-commerce growth in Q2 2020. The company's prior investments in e-commerce, mobile, and BOPIS (buy online, pickup in store) proved crucial, allowing for a quick pivot to curbside pickup. To further drive growth and enhance its blended shopping experience, Tractor Supply has partnered with Adobe, utilizing Adobe Experience Cloud to deliver, personalize, and measure customer interactions online and integrate with physical stores.

title: Infosys to acquire U.S.-based Blue Acorn iCi for $125 mn
summary: Infosys has announced its acquisition of Blue Acorn iCi, a U.S.-based digital customer experience company, for $125 million. This acquisition aims to enhance Infosys' capabilities in digital customer experience, commerce, and analytics. The deal is anticipated to close during the third quarter of fiscal 2021, strengthening Infosys' commitment to the Adobe ecosystem.

title: Infosys to acquire US-based Blue Acorn iCi for up to $125 million
summary: Infosys announced it will acquire US-based Blue Acorn iCi, a provider of digital customer experience, commerce, and analytics solutions, for up to $125 million. This acquisition is set to strengthen Infosys’ end-to-end customer experience offerings and capabilities, particularly in the Adobe ecosystem, and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2021. Blue Acorn iCi reported $43.6 million in revenue in 2019.

title: Infosys to acquire US-based Blue Acorn iCi for around $125 million
summary: Infosys has announced its acquisition of US-based Blue Acorn iCi for up to $125 million. This strategic move aims to strengthen Infosys's end-to-end customer experience offerings, particularly in digital commerce, and enhance its capabilities within ecosystems like Adobe, Magento, Salesforce Commerce, and Shopify. The acquisition is expected to close in Q3 FY2021.

title: Why is Adobe Called Adobe? The Story of Garage Startup to Empire
summary: This article explores the origins of Adobe, from its founding by John Warnock and Charles Geschke after leaving Xerox Parc, to its growth into a software empire. It explains that Adobe was named after a creek behind Warnock's house and highlights the founders' risk-taking in embracing PostScript technology, which revolutionized graphic arts. The piece also touches on significant moments in their history, including refusing a buyout from Steve Jobs and dealing with a hostage situation.

title: Office/Retail/Mixed-Use, Best Project, Project of the Year Finalist: Adobe Corporate Campus - Phase 2 | 2020-10-13
summary: This article highlights phase two of the Adobe Corporate Campus project in Lehi, Utah, which was a Project of the Year Finalist and Best Project in the Office/Retail/Mixed-Use category. The project included a four-story office building, a parking garage, a state-of-the-art fitness center, and various sustainability features and overcome challenges like wet weather and the COVID-19 pandemic. Key firms involved included Okland Construction Co., WRNS Studio, and Ensign Engineering.

title: Adobe Photoshop Gets New NVIDIA AI-Powered Neural Filters That Can Automatically Age You
summary: Adobe Photoshop has introduced new AI-powered neural filters, developed in collaboration with NVIDIA, to enhance image editing. One notable feature is the Smart Portrait Filter, which utilizes NVIDIA StyleGAN2 technology to allow users to easily adjust facial characteristics like gaze, lighting, and age with simple sliders. This technology dramatically speeds up complex adjustments that would typically require extensive manual work.

title: Your creative system starts with Creative Cloud
summary: This article highlights how Adobe Creative Cloud, especially Creative Cloud Libraries, helps brands maintain consistent experiences across various marketing touchpoints. It announces new features for Libraries, including deeper integration with Adobe XD for design systems, expanded integration with Adobe Spark for visual storytelling, and the availability of Libraries APIs for third-party integrations like Google Workspace. The goal is to streamline collaboration, enhance productivity, and ensure creative consistency for all teams.

title: UCF Partners with Adobe to Personalize Reading Experiences for Students, Adults
summary: The University of Central Florida (UCF) is collaborating with Adobe on a digital reading project aimed at personalizing reading experiences and reducing information overload. This partnership utilizes Adobe's Liquid Mode technology, allowing users to adjust text settings in PDFs, while UCF researchers develop tools to match readers with optimal reading formats. Early studies show promising results, with some adults accelerating their reading by over 25%.

title: Adobe Illustrator launches on iPad and Adobe Fresco comes to the iPhone
summary: Adobe has released the first public version of its Illustrator vector graphics app on the iPad, a highly anticipated development among users. Additionally, the company made its Fresco drawing and painting app available on Apple's iPhone, expanding its accessibility from previous Windows or iPad limitations. Both apps feature tailored user interfaces for their respective devices, with Illustrator supporting Apple Pencil and Fresco offering consistent features and brushes across platforms, along with a new version 2.0 update.

title: Adobe Unveils Authentication Tool in Battle Against Deepfakes
summary: Adobe Inc. has launched a new software tool to help content creators authenticate their digital images, aiming to combat the spread of deepfakes and increase trust in online content. This attribution tool for Photoshop and Behance will allow creators to tag images with their names, editing history, and location, with testing beginning soon and a general release slated for 2021. The initiative responds to growing public skepticism about manipulated digital images.

title: The Content Authenticity Initiative unveils content attribution tool within Photoshop and Behance
summary: The Content Authenticity Initiative (CAI) has unveiled a preview of its new content attribution tool within Adobe Creative Cloud, specifically Photoshop and Behance. This tool, built on an open standard, will provide secure, tamper-evident attribution data such as author's name, location, and edit history to photos, aiming to increase trust and transparency online. The initiative, a collaboration with several leading organizations, also emphasizes the importance of crediting creators for their work, fostering a virtuous cycle of proper attribution and consumer expectation.

title: October 2020 release of Adobe XD: Get to final faster with 3D transforms, Creative Cloud Libraries, Visual Studio Code, and more
summary: Adobe announced its October 2020 release for Adobe XD, timed with Adobe MAX. This update introduces significant features like 3D Transforms for creating depth and perspective in designs, and enhanced Creative Cloud Libraries for scaling design systems with improved collaboration and organization. Additionally, a new Adobe XD extension for Microsoft Visual Studio Code aims to bridge the gap between designers and developers by enabling them to work with design systems without leaving their code editor.

title: Adobe’s new AI experiment syncs your dance moves perfectly to the beat
summary: Adobe showcased a new AI experiment at its Adobe Max conference that can perfectly sync off-beat dance movements to music. The technology uses computer vision to analyze body movements and music beats, then adjusts the video to match them. This experiment could be a significant tool for creators and may eventually be integrated into platforms like TikTok or Instagram Reels.

title: Nvidia deal for Arm will drive computing power growth, says SoftBank’s CEO
summary: SoftBank Group Corp. CEO Masayoshi Son stated that the sale of chip designer Arm to Nvidia Corp. will boost computing power growth, marking his first public comments since the $40 billion deal announcement. Son made these remarks at an AI summit hosted by Saudi Arabia, emphasizing his belief in AI's societal transformation. The deal, part of SoftBank's asset sales, has raised concerns about Arm's neutrality as a supplier, but Son remains optimistic about its implications for future investment and AI acceleration.

title: C3.ai, Microsoft, and Adobe combine forces to re-invent CRM with AI
summary: C3.ai, Microsoft, and Adobe announced the launch of C3 AI® CRM, an AI-first, industry-specific customer relationship management solution powered by Microsoft Dynamics 365 and integrated with Adobe Experience Cloud. This collaboration aims to redefine the CRM market by offering AI-enabled revenue forecasting, product forecasting, and customer churn prediction tailored for various industries. The C3 AI CRM is unique for its modern AI-first architecture, enabling real-time predictive insights across the entire revenue supply chain.

title: C3 AI, Microsoft & Adobe: Reinventing CRM with AI
summary: C3.ai, Microsoft Corp., and Adobe Inc. announced the launch of C3 AI CRM, an enterprise-class, AI-first customer relationship management solution. This new offering, powered by Microsoft Dynamics 365 and integrating Adobe Experience Cloud, is purpose-built for industries and designed to drive customer-facing operations with predictive business insights. The partners aim to reinvent the CRM market by providing industry-specific, AI-enabled solutions for various sectors globally.

title: Monday.Com launches apps marketplace
summary: Monday.com has launched an Apps marketplace built on its Apps Framework, providing customers with over a hundred applications to extend their Work OS solution. The marketplace aims to empower users to build and share functionalities, though it is currently in beta and lacks features like monetization and app reviews that are expected to be added later. This move is a significant step for monday.com, demonstrating the extensibility of its platform and centralizing integration options for its growing user base.

title: Windows 10 Update Attempts (and Fails) to Remove Adobe Flash Forever
summary: Microsoft released an optional Windows Update intended to remove Adobe Flash Player from Windows 10 machines ahead of Flash's official end-of-life at the end of 2020. However, the update only removes the version of Flash bundled with Windows 10, failing to delete standalone installations or browser components of Flash. Users are advised to manually check and remove any remaining Flash installations for improved security.

title: Walgreens Boots Alliance Renews and Extends Relationship with WPP to Accelerate Digital Transformation and Deep Focus on Communications
summary: Walgreens Boots Alliance (WBA) has renewed and extended its partnership with WPP as its global marketing and communications agency until at least the end of 2022, with an option to extend further. This collaboration aims to accelerate WBA's digital transformation, focusing on implementing an integrated mass-personalization marketing strategy and reputation-management communications. The partnership will leverage data and technology solutions, including WBA's strategic alliance with Microsoft and Adobe, to create personalized omnichannel experiences.

title: A Look Behind the Vimeo Brand
summary: This article explores Vimeo's unique position in the crowded video platform market, highlighting its focus on community, storytelling tools, and value proposition compared to competitors like YouTube. Author Bryan Elliott shares insights from his professional experience and an interview with Vimeo CMO Harris Beber, discussing Vimeo's features, OTT capabilities, and a new service connecting creators with video professionals. The piece also delves into the author's personal journey in content creation, including his early experiences in the entertainment industry and the inspiration behind his career path, concluding that now is an optimal time for video storytelling.

title: ITSMA study: Earning B2B buyer trust matters even more now
summary: A recent ITSMA study, co-sponsored by Adobe and SAP, highlights that earning B2B buyer trust is more crucial than ever, especially in light of the COVID-19 pandemic. The survey of nearly 300 B2B senior executives reveals that technology providers, professional services firms, and systems integrators are top sources of trusted information. Digital transformation has become a primary driver for technology investments, with cloud, security, and collaboration software being key priorities, while content quality and practical insights are preferred over format.

title: PepsiCo introduces direct-to-consumer e-commerce sites
summary: PepsiCo has launched two direct-to-consumer e-commerce platforms, PantryShop.com and Snacks.com, in response to increased online shopping due to the coronavirus pandemic. PantryShop.com offers bundled product collections, while Snacks.com allows consumers to curate snack packages from Frito-Lay brands. This initiative aims to provide consumers with easy and fast access to their favorite PepsiCo products delivered directly to their homes.

title: Adobe acquires marketing workflow startup Workfront for $1.5B
summary: Adobe has acquired the marketing workflow management startup Workfront for $1.5 billion. This acquisition aims to strengthen Adobe's Experience Cloud by providing robust tools for managing complex marketing projects, especially in a distributed work environment. Workfront's CEO Alex Shootman will continue to lead the company, which will operate within Adobe Experience Cloud.

title: South Australia Department for Education reshapes the future with forms
summary: The South Australian Department for Education is using Adobe Experience Manager Forms to streamline administrative processes and achieve its goal of world-class public education. By digitizing forms like the School Card program and secondary school transition forms, the department has significantly reduced manual data entry, improved accuracy, and saved costs. This move also enhances privacy, simplifies workflows for internal employees, and aims to provide a more user-friendly experience for families.

title: Adobe Acquires Workfront For $1.5 Billion
summary: Adobe announced its acquisition of Workfront Inc. for $1.5 billion, aiming to integrate a collaboration tool into its Experience Cloud marketing software. The deal, expected to close between December and February 2021, will bring Workfront's 3,000 corporate customers and 1 million users to Adobe, enhancing its offerings for distributed marketing teams and digital content management. This acquisition is Adobe's fifth-largest and reflects a growing need for efficient work management in digital marketing.

title: Shutterstock acquires AI-driven music platform Amper Music
summary: Shutterstock has acquired AI-driven music platform Amper Music for an undisclosed sum, furthering the trend of stock image sites getting involved in rights-free music. This acquisition enhances Shutterstock's AI capabilities and R&D efforts, allowing users to create and customize original music while strengthening its existing portfolio of licensed content. Amper Music's employees will join Shutterstock, bringing their expertise in AI-powered music composition to a global customer base.

title: The University of New Mexico builds a bridge to the future as an Adobe Creative Campus
summary: The University of New Mexico (UNM) has become an Adobe Creative Campus to equip its diverse student body and faculty with essential digital literacy skills. This initiative provides widespread access to Adobe Creative Cloud tools, supporting creative expression, critical thinking, and storytelling across various disciplines. UNM also leverages Adobe Document Cloud to streamline administrative processes, demonstrating a comprehensive commitment to digital transformation for its students and the state of New Mexico.

title: The Snowflake Story
summary: Snowflake Inc., founded in 2012, is a cloud-based data-warehousing company that came out of stealth mode in 2014. The company offers a data warehouse-as-a-service, running on major cloud platforms and enabling data storage and analytics for corporate users. Snowflake had a historic IPO on September 16, 2020, with shares more than doubling in value, leading to a $70.4 billion market valuation on its first day of trading.

title: Adobe and Nike announce student winners of analytics competition
summary: Adobe and Nike announced the winners of their 2020 Analytics Challenge, a virtual competition focused on reimagining the Nike customer experience using Adobe Analytics. The top prize of $35,000 went to "The Alphas" from the Indian Institute of Technology (Indian School of Mines) Dhanbad for their three-pronged strategy involving website redesign, social community building, and refined marketing efforts. The event aimed to equip students with career-ready digital literacy skills, highlighting the growing importance of digital services during the pandemic.

title: CSM petitions funding of Adobe licenses, college district eyes possible alternatives
summary: A student senator at the College of San Mateo (CSM) has launched a petition to urge the college district to fund Adobe Creative Cloud licenses for students after Adobe discontinued free access. The district acknowledges the cost challenge of providing licenses for all students but is exploring solutions such as licensing for specific courses, loaning computers with pre-installed software, or offering remote desktop access for late spring 2021. Students and faculty express concerns about the cost and accessibility issues, with some digital media students having difficulty affording the discounted licenses and experiencing problems with CollegeBuys.Org.

title: Compass elects LinkedIn CFO Steve Sordello to board of directors
summary: Real estate technology firm Compass has appointed Steve Sordello, LinkedIn's CFO, to its board of directors. Sordello brings extensive experience from his tenure at LinkedIn, where he oversaw the company's IPO and its $26.2 billion merger with Microsoft. His expertise in guiding fast-growing businesses will be instrumental as Compass continues to expand and empower its real estate agents.

title: Forget FAANG—it’s time to scale MT SAAS
summary: Bessemer Venture Partners introduces "MT SAAS" (Microsoft, Twilio, Salesforce, Amazon, Adobe, Shopify) as the new basket of top public technology companies, succeeding the traditional "FAANG" stocks. This new group represents leaders in cloud computing, identified as the mega-trend of the next decade, and has shown significant outperformance compared to FAANG stocks over recent years. The article details the characteristics and growth trajectories of each MT SAAS company, highlighting their cloud-first strategies and substantial market capitalization gains.

title: Send Joy During a Stressful Year With a Holiday Card
summary: During a stressful year marked by distance and disconnection due to the pandemic, many people are turning to holiday cards to connect with loved ones. This year, holiday card messages often reflect the realities of the ongoing situation, with a focus on sincerity over overly cheerful sentiments. The trend shows a significant increase in holiday card sending, with many individuals and families incorporating pandemic-related themes into their cards.

title: Salesforce invests in Israel's AppsFlyer at $2 billion valuation
summary: Salesforce Ventures has invested in Israeli startup AppsFlyer as part of its $210 million Series D round, valuing the company at $2 billion. AppsFlyer, which develops tools for analyzing and measuring digital campaigns, plans to recruit 350 additional employees after doubling its annual recurring revenue to over $200 million. Co-founder Oren Kaniel stated the company intends to remain private for now, focusing on long-term growth and leveraging Salesforce's operational knowledge.

title: Amazon Cloud Outage Hits Customers Including Roku, Adobe
summary: Amazon's cloud-computing division, Amazon Web Services (AWS), experienced an outage affecting its Kinesis data streaming service in the U.S. East 1 region. This incident impacted several customers, including Roku Inc. and Adobe Inc., and also hindered Amazon's ability to provide timely updates on its status page. The article was published on November 25, 2020.

title: With Slack, Salesforce Would Put Heat on Microsoft
summary: Salesforce.com Inc.'s potential acquisition of Slack Technologies Inc., valued at $17 billion, positions Salesforce to directly challenge Microsoft Corp. in the business-software market. This strategic move by CEO Marc Benioff, leveraging the work-from-home trend, aims to make Salesforce a dominant provider and intensify competition with its long-time rival. The Wall Street Journal reported on Wednesday that Salesforce is in advanced talks to buy Slack.

title: Online sales reach $10.8 billion on Cyber Monday, the biggest U.S. e-commerce day ever, Adobe says
summary: Online sales on Cyber Monday reached a record $10.8 billion, a 15.1% increase from the previous year, according to Adobe Analytics. Despite setting a new record for U.S. online shopping, this figure fell short of Adobe's original forecast of $12.7 billion due to retailers starting deals earlier in the holiday season. The shift to online shopping and earlier promotions has also lessened the importance of traditional Black Friday metrics and boosted curbside pickup.

title: This chart shows how the Salesforce acquisition of Slack for $27.7 billion stacks up against tech's largest deals ever
summary: Salesforce announced its intent to acquire Slack for $27.7 billion, making it one of the largest tech acquisitions in history, surpassing Microsoft's LinkedIn purchase. This move highlights Salesforce's strategy to expand its enterprise software offerings and compete directly with Microsoft Teams. The article provides a detailed list of the 28 most expensive tech acquisitions, showcasing how Salesforce's Slack deal compares to other major transactions, from Apple's Beats acquisition to Dell's purchase of EMC.

title: XPO Logistics CEO says company is in the middle of the 'e-comm boom'
summary: XPO Logistics CEO Brad Jacobs stated the company is squarely in the middle of the e-commerce boom, particularly within its logistics business. The company plans to spin off its warehousing business from transportation to unlock "trapped value," expecting the separation to occur in the second half of 2021. This move aims to simplify the business and make it more attractive to investors, focusing the new entity on e-commerce fulfillment.

title: 3 Best Practices for Using Third-Party B2B Buyer Intent Data in ABM
summary: This article outlines three best practices for utilizing third-party B2B buyer intent data in Account-Based Marketing (ABM) campaigns. It emphasizes the importance of researching multiple data sources, using intent data to inform content decisions, and preparing for privacy and regulatory concerns. The goal is to help software providers identify and engage prospective buyers more effectively.

title: Adobe completes Workfront acquisition, brings leading work management platform to Adobe Experience Cloud
summary: Adobe has completed its acquisition of Workfront, a leading work management platform for marketers. This acquisition aims to simplify digital transformation by integrating Workfront's robust workflow orchestration with Adobe Creative Cloud and Adobe Experience Cloud. The combined offerings will provide customers with a single system for planning, collaboration, and governance, enhancing productivity and enabling seamless management of marketing workflows.

title: Adobe Stock Looks Like a Buy Before Its Earnings Release
summary: Adobe stock has gained over 47% YTD and is considered a potential buy before its Q4 earnings release on Dec. 9. Despite a recent cool-down from its 52-week highs, the company's consistent revenue growth and attractive PEG ratio suggest it could be a long-term investment, especially if positive earnings and guidance are provided. Analysts anticipate a median target price of $560, indicating a 15.2% premium over current prices.

title: Content Marketing Market 2020-2024- Featuring Adobe Inc., Contently Inc., CoSchedule LLC, among others to contribute to the market growth | Industry Analysis, Market Trends, Opportunities, and Forecast 2024 Technavio
summary: The content marketing market is projected to grow by USD 269.24 billion during 2020-2024, at a CAGR of over 13%, driven by increasing social media users and demand for digital magazines. Technavio's report provides a comprehensive analysis including market size, forecast, trends, growth drivers, challenges, and vendor analysis featuring companies such as Adobe Inc., Contently Inc., and CoSchedule LLC. The study also covers the impact of COVID-19 and offers multiple forecast scenarios.

title: How AbbVie delivers exceptional patient experiences
summary: AbbVie, a biopharmaceutical company, has leveraged digital transformation and Adobe platforms to enhance patient experiences and agile development. They have implemented a multichannel marketing strategy and personalization through data management to provide customer-centric experiences, especially in response to challenges like COVID-19. This has created a strong foundation for future digital capabilities and reduced the time for delivering new experiences.

title: Adobe Lightroom CC Adds Native Support For Apple M1 And Windows 10 On Arm Devices
summary: Adobe has released native Arm64 versions of Lightroom CC (version 4.1) for systems running Apple M1 (macOS Big Sur) and Qualcomm Snapdragon 8cx (Windows 10 on Arm) processors. This update aims to deliver optimized performance on these platforms, as Adobe continues to retool its Creative Suite for Arm-based architecture. Existing x86-64 versions of Lightroom will still function via Rosetta 2 emulation on Apple M1 Macs.

title: Adobe Flash Player Receives Final Software Update As It Walks Death Row
summary: Adobe has released its final software update for Flash Player, marking the end of its support after December 31, 2020. All Flash content will be blocked from running starting January 12, 2021, and Adobe strongly recommends users uninstall the player due to its long history of security vulnerabilities. This phase-out comes over a decade after Steve Jobs famously criticized Flash for its proprietary nature, security flaws, and performance issues.

title: A new chapter for Pantone’s color of the year in 2021
summary: Pantone has announced two Colors of the Year for 2021: Ultimate Gray (PANTONE 17-5104) and Illuminating (PANTONE 13-0647). This dual choice symbolizes strength, optimism, resilience, and the importance of relationships in challenging times. The combination of these practical and vivacious colors offers a message of hope for the new year.

title: State Street invests in a digital future with Adobe
summary: State Street Global Advisors has undertaken a comprehensive digital transformation initiative in partnership with Adobe, rebuilding nearly 150 websites to offer personalized digital experiences. Using Adobe Experience Manager, Marketo, and Adobe Analytics, the company has unified its online presence, improved customer engagement by 40%, and is focusing on data-driven insights and employee training. This multi-phase project aims to enhance customer service, streamline operations, and prepare for future technological advancements in the financial services sector.

title: NerdWallet taps eBay CFO
summary: Personal finance app and website NerdWallet has appointed Lauren St. Clair, formerly North American CFO of online marketplace giant eBay, as its new CFO. St. Clair's immediate priorities include integrating recently acquired companies Know Your Money and Fundera, as NerdWallet continues its rapid growth and international expansion. She brings nearly two decades of finance experience from both startup and multinational corporations to her new role.

title: How to find photos on your phone? Here's how I do it.
summary: This article provides practical advice on organizing and finding photos on a smartphone, addressing a common issue for many users. The author shares his personal method, which involves using professional tools like Adobe Lightroom and various cloud storage services including Amazon Photos and SmugMug. It also highlights how others, like Kristin Metcalfe, leverage built-in features such as Apple's "Places" tool, and discusses the evolving landscape of photo storage services with Google Photos' upcoming changes.

title: Now in Beta: Premiere Pro, Premiere Rush, and Audition for Apple M1 Systems
summary: Adobe has released public Beta versions of Premiere Pro, Premiere Rush, and Audition with native support for Apple's new M1 chipsets, aiming for improved performance and energy efficiency. These initial Beta builds include core editing functions and support for widely used codecs, with a phased approach planned for full M1 integration due to the large codebase. Full native support is expected in the first half of 2021, while users can currently run existing versions via Rosetta 2 emulation.

title: Adobe Flash is about to die, but classic Flash games will live on
summary: Adobe Flash Player is officially ending on January 12, 2021, marking the demise of the once essential but problematic browser plug-in. While Flash was known for security issues and performance problems, it played a crucial role in enabling online multimedia and gaming for many years. Enthusiasts are working on workarounds to preserve classic Flash games and content, ensuring they remain playable despite Adobe's termination of support.

title: WisdomTree Computing Portfolio Has Investors on Cloud Nine
summary: Cloud computing is emerging as a premier disruptive technology, and advisors can leverage this trend for client portfolios using WisdomTree's new Disruptive Growth Model Portfolio. This portfolio targets structural growth themes, including the "MT SAAS" basket of tech companies like Microsoft and Amazon, which are seen as potential successors to FAANG stocks. The WisdomTree Cloud Computing ETF (WCLD), a top performer, is a core component, tracking an index of emerging cloud-based software companies.

title: Adobe Flash Player is finally laid to rest
summary: Adobe Flash Player has officially reached its end-of-life on December 31, 2020, with Adobe ceasing security updates and advising users to uninstall it. Once a dominant platform for online videos and games since its 1996 release, Flash struggled with security issues and failed to adapt to the mobile era. Its demise means Adobe will block content from running in Flash Player starting January 12, 2021, raising concerns about the preservation of historical animations and interactive content.

title: Adobe Officially Ends Support of Its Era-Defining Flash Player Today
summary: Adobe has officially ended support for its Flash Player on December 31, 2020, after more than two decades, with content blocking scheduled to begin on January 12, 2021. The decision, made in 2017, recommends users uninstall the software due to security concerns, though Flash Player was pivotal in evolving web content. The Internet Archive plans to preserve popular Flash animations, games, and videos using an emulator called Ruffle.

title: Today Adobe Flash Player reached the end of life (EOL)
summary: Adobe Flash Player officially reached its end of life (EOL) on January 1, 2021, ceasing to receive updates and support. Adobe strongly recommended users uninstall the software due to past vulnerabilities and the company blocking Flash content from running after January 12, 2021. The move was a culmination of a 2017 announcement by major tech companies to phase out Flash in favor of more secure and performant alternatives like HTML5.

title: Adobe Flash Player is officially dead. Here’s how to uninstall it
summary: Adobe Flash Player has officially reached its "end-of-life" on December 31, after years of declining usage due to security vulnerabilities and the rise of open web standards like HTML5. Adobe is urging users to uninstall the software immediately to protect their systems, as it will no longer receive security updates. The article provides brief instructions on how to remove Flash Player from both Windows and Mac operating systems.

title: F5 to acquire multi-cloud security software maker Volterra for $500 million, raises financial outlook
summary: F5 announced its intent to acquire Volterra, a privately held multi-cloud application security and load-balancing software maker, for $500 million. This acquisition is expected to enhance F5's capabilities in distributed multi-cloud application security, with Volterra's CEO Ankur Singla remaining in a key management role. F5 also raised its financial outlook for the December quarter and its fiscal year growth forecasts, signaling stronger-than-expected software demand.

title: As of today, Adobe is blocking all Flash content from running
summary: Adobe has officially begun blocking all Flash content from running as of January 12, 2021, following its end-of-life support on December 31, 2020. Users are strongly advised to uninstall Flash Player immediately to protect their systems. Although Flash content is rare, Adobe is ensuring its complete deprecation by actively blocking it.

title: How to fully remove Adobe Flash from your Mac
summary: Adobe has officially ended support for Flash and is strongly recommending users uninstall it from their systems to protect against security vulnerabilities, as no further updates or security patches will be issued. This guide provides instructions on how to completely remove Adobe Flash from a Mac, including deauthorizing Flash and using the official Adobe Flash Player Install Manager. Users are warned that any requests to update Flash player now are likely malicious.

title: Americans spent a record online over 2020 holidays, and more e-commerce gains are expected
summary: Online shopping in the U.S. surged by 32.2% to a record $188.2 billion during the 2020 holiday season, driven by the COVID-19 pandemic and increased consumer reliance on e-commerce. November alone saw $100 billion in online sales for the first time, with popular options like curbside pickup contributing to stronger sales for retailers offering them. This unprecedented online activity is also expected to result in a record volume of returns, posing a new challenge for retailers in 2021.

title: Watch out: Purchase order email scam links to phishing page employing Adobe & Microsoft branding
summary: MailGuard has intercepted a phishing email scam that leverages purchase order notifications and the branding of Adobe and Microsoft to trick users. The scam involves a compromised email account sending a fake purchase order with a link to a phishing page designed to steal email credentials. MailGuard warns users to be vigilant against such scams, especially those originating from legitimate-looking senders, and to always check for red flags like generic salutations and unusual URLs.

title: Top Analyst Upgrades and Downgrades: AMD, Adobe, Chevron, Chipotle, DuPont, Intel, Penn National Gaming and More
summary: This article details top analyst upgrades, downgrades, and initiations announced on January 14, 2021, for various major companies. It provides specific ratings, target prices, and consensus figures for stocks like AMD, Adobe, Chevron, Chipotle, DuPont, and Intel. The report also notes the market context of the announcements, including impending Q4 earnings reports and Intel's CEO change.

title: How Adobe is planning its employees’ return to the office
summary: Adobe is carefully planning its employees' return to the office, adopting a flexible, digital-first hybrid model that empowers teams to decide how and when they work together in person. The company emphasizes employee safety and mental well-being, having implemented initiatives like company-wide shutdowns to combat burnout. This approach reflects a broader industry shift towards hybrid work, especially within the information technology sector, where a significant portion of employees are expected to continue working remotely post-pandemic.

title: Infosys Acquires Digital Design Agency Carter Digital
summary: Infosys has acquired Carter Digital, an Australian digital design agency, to bolster its digital experience offerings, particularly within WONGDOODY, an existing Infosys brand. This acquisition is part of Infosys's strategy to enhance its end-to-end digital transformation capabilities for clients in a critical market like Australia. The financial terms of the deal were not disclosed.

title: Pixar Veteran Guido Quaroni Appointed To Key Role On Adobe’s 3D & Immersive Team
summary: Guido Quaroni, a Pixar veteran of 24 years, has left the studio to join Adobe as the senior director of engineering on their 3D and immersive team. Quaroni was instrumental in Pixar's technological advancements, including supervising technical direction for "Toy Story 3" and "Monsters, Inc.," and overseeing their software R&D, including the Universal Scene Description (USD) project. He expressed excitement about Adobe's renewed focus on the 3D market and aims to help accelerate their offerings for creatives.

title: Google Defends Against Allegations Of Collusion With Facebook On Ads
summary: Adam Cohen, Google's director of economic policy, has refuted claims of collusion with Facebook regarding ad-sharing agreements, calling allegations by Texas attorney general Ken Paxton "misleading." The defense comes after a New York Times expose detailed an antitrust lawsuit suggesting Google provided special advantages to Facebook. Cohen clarified that Facebook competes in a crowded online advertising space and that their Open Bidding program is not manipulated in Facebook's favor, constituting less than 4% of Google's display ads.

title: Berkshire Hathaway HomeServices makes it easy to buy a home with Adobe Acrobat Sign
summary: Berkshire Hathaway HomeServices' San Diego Downtown office adopted Adobe Acrobat Sign to streamline the home buying process, particularly for second-stage documents like offers and disclosures. The solution, praised for its ease of use and security, eliminates the need for buyers and sellers to create accounts, simplifying the signing experience. This adoption significantly improved efficiency, especially during the COVID-19 pandemic for processing essential forms, and allows agents to manage documents from any device.

title: How ELEMIS London is utilizing a technology-forward approach to skincare and e-commerce
summary: ELEMIS London has adopted a technology-forward approach to e-commerce, mirroring its innovative product development. The brand invested in a progressive web app (PWA) storefront built on Magento Commerce, becoming a mobile-first company. This strategic move has significantly improved website performance, mobile transactions, and conversion rates, with further enhancements planned to improve the customer experience.

title: Customer analytics firms merge to take on Salesforce, Adobe
summary: Customer analytics vendors Manthan Systems and RichRelevance merged on January 19, 2021, to form Algonomy, aiming to compete with CRM giants like Salesforce and Adobe. Algonomy combines AI-fueled insights with global retail expertise, offering a customer data platform built on augmented intelligence and machine learning for real-time customer experiences. Forrester Research principal analyst Brandon Purcell views this merger as a "big win" due to the companies' complementary strengths and shared focus on personalized retail experiences.

title: IBM iX Forms Accelerator for Adobe Experience Manager aids customer experience transformation
summary: The IBM iX Forms Accelerator for Adobe Experience Manager, a collaboration between IBM and Adobe, is designed to streamline the development of complex digital forms, improving customer experience for large enterprises. This solution helps highly regulated industries create, manage, and publish secure forms, integrating them with back-end systems and offering compliance benefits. It aims to reduce development effort, enhance customer and citizen experiences with dynamic forms, and provide technical flexibility with built-in security features.

title: Adobe Flash Shutdown Halts Chinese Railroad for Over 16 Hours Before Pirated Copy Restores Ops
summary: The Adobe Flash shutdown in January 2021 caused significant disruption for China Railway Shenyang, halting its operations for over 16 hours. The railway's timetables, reliant on Flash, became inaccessible, leading to widespread outages. Service was eventually restored using a pirated, "time bomb"-free version of Flash, highlighting a lack of awareness about the shutdown within China's insular internet and the country's unique stance on copyright.

title: How Discount Tire became a market leader through customer experience
summary: Discount Tire transformed from a small startup to a market leader by prioritizing customer experience in a commodity market. The company successfully integrated online and in-store shopping experiences, using technology like Adobe Experience Cloud to personalize interactions and build resilience, particularly during the COVID-19 pandemic. This strategy has led to significant growth and a sustained focus on individual customer needs.

title: Shutterstock makes a $75 million bet that the future of photography won't always involve cameras
summary: Shutterstock has acquired TurboSquid, a 3D model company, for $75 million, signaling a shift in the photography industry towards CGI and 3D assets. This acquisition will make Shutterstock the world's largest 3D marketplace by revenue and is expected to add 2% to 3% to its overall revenue. The move highlights the increasing use of 3D models in content creation, raising questions about distinguishing real images from computer-generated ones, and the need for future regulation.

title: Breaking into sales? Women in sales share their best career advice
summary: This article features women in sales at Adobe sharing their career advice and what they love about working in the field. They highlight that sales offers opportunities for growth, problem-solving, and valuable transferable skills. The women encourage others, particularly women, to pursue software sales, emphasizing the supportive environment and the shift away from stereotypical aggressive sales traits.

title: Splashtop Announces $50M in New Funding, Achieving Coveted $1 Billion Valuation
summary: Splashtop Inc., a remote access and remote support provider, has secured $50 million in new funding, pushing its valuation past $1 billion. The company, which has been profitable since 2015 and saw accelerated growth during the COVID-19 pandemic, plans to use the funding to attract talent, expand its enterprise presence, and accelerate global expansion. Recognized for its efficient and agile approach to remote access, Splashtop serves a significant portion of Fortune 500 companies and educational institutions.

title: HP Spectre x360 14 Launched in Malaysia, Price Starts At RM6,099
summary: The HP Spectre x360 14 has been launched in Malaysia with a starting price of RM6,099. Hailed as HP's most intelligent PC, it features the 11th Gen Intel Core Processor, a 3:2 aspect ratio display, and up to 17 hours of battery life. HP is also hosting a giveaway in conjunction with the launch.

title: XM Stock Price, Forecast & Analysis | QUALTRICS INTERNATIONAL-CL A (NASDAQ:XM)
summary: This article provides an analysis of Qualtrics International (NASDAQ: XM) stock, detailing its current price, performance metrics, and forecast. It includes technical and fundamental ratings from ChartMill, key financial highlights, and analyst price targets, alongside a company profile. The stock was trading at $18.14, with analysts expecting a 1.67% price increase in the next year.

title: 'I wanted to build something': How a 1990s bike tour led Adobe exec Ashley Still to tech
summary: The article details how Ashley Still, a senior vice president and general manager at Adobe, was drawn to the tech industry and Silicon Valley after leading a bike tour down the West Coast as a Yale undergraduate in the 1990s. This initial experience motivated her to move west and start her career in technology.

title: Box Buys SignRequest, Taking on DocuSign and Adobe in e-Signature Market
summary: Box has acquired SignRequest for $55 million in cash and stock, signaling its entry into the e-signature market. This move positions Box to compete with established players like DocuSign and Adobe. The acquisition aims to expand Box's offerings and capitalize on the growing demand for digital signature solutions.

title: These Places Were Not Ready for Flash to Die
summary: Despite ample warning of Adobe Flash's discontinuation on December 31, 2020, various government and corporate systems worldwide were caught unprepared. This led to significant disruptions, including a Chinese railway system crash, issues with the South African Revenue Service's e-filing, delays in death certificate filings in South Carolina, and technical problems with SEC financial reporting. The article highlights how critical infrastructure and educational systems continued to rely on Flash until its abrupt end, leading to chaos and emergency workarounds.

title: As the role of design in healthcare shifts, Sanofi turns to design system thinking for solutions
summary: Sanofi is implementing an internal design system called "Syntax" to streamline design processes and enhance employee experience across its globally distributed teams. This initiative, inspired by a successful external design system that reduced costs, aims to bring consistency to internal applications and improve efficiency. The company leveraged Adobe Creative Cloud tools like Coediting in Adobe XD and Creative Cloud Libraries, which proved instrumental during the pandemic-induced shift to virtual collaboration and contributed to the project's positive initial results and high Net Promoter Score.

title: Adobe’s 2020 Founders’ Award recipients share their proudest career moments
summary: Adobe's 2020 Founders' Award recipients share their most memorable and proudest career moments at the company. The award recognizes employees who embody Adobe's core values: genuine, exceptional, innovative, and involved. Recipients highlighted various achievements, from advancing diversity and inclusion initiatives to launching major products and solving complex technical challenges.

title: Sonoma county transforms resident services with automated, highly secure digital workflows
summary: Sonoma County is undergoing a digital transformation, replacing manual, paper-based processes with automated digital workflows using Adobe Acrobat DC and Adobe Acrobat Sign. This initiative, driven by the increasing demand for digital government services, aims to improve efficiency, security, and accessibility for its half-million residents. The county has seen significant reductions in workflow times, enhanced compliance, and broad adoption across various departments.

title: Adobe earns top accolade for its people-centric approach to business
summary: Adobe has been named to Forbes' "America’s Best Employers" list, ranking No. 27, in recognition of its long-standing people-centric approach to business. The company highlights its dedication to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace through progressive policies, a thoughtful COVID-19 response, and the "Adobe For All" vision aimed at advancing diversity and inclusion. These initiatives include investing in employee well-being, pay and opportunity parity, and addressing racial injustice.

title: Introducing your 2021 Marketo Engage Champions!
summary: Adobe has announced its 2021 Marketo Engage Champions, marking the 10th anniversary of the program. This elite group comprises Marketo Engage experts and advocates who share their knowledge, support others, and provide feedback to Adobe's product teams. Champions benefit from career development, speaking opportunities, and connections within the Marketo Engage community.

title: Citrix Systems Inc. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors despite daily gains
summary: On Thursday, Citrix Systems Inc. (CTXS) saw a 0.63% increase in its stock price, closing at $137.79. Despite this daily gain, the stock underperformed compared to some competitors like Microsoft Corp. and Adobe Inc., which rose 0.69% and 0.80% respectively, although it outperformed Oracle Corp. The stock is $35.77 below its 52-week high, and trading volume was below its 50-day average.

title: Adobe unveils new AI ethics principles as part of commitment to responsible digital citizenship
summary: Adobe has announced new AI ethics principles—responsibility, accountability, and transparency—to guide its development and deployment of artificial intelligence. Recognizing that AI, like its human creators, can contain biases, Adobe is committed to mitigating such harms through careful design, user feedback, and review processes. The company formed an AI Ethics Committee and Review Board, which has already reviewed features like Neural Filters, and plans to scale this review process to all new AI-powered products.

title: Microsoft Starts Automatically Removing Flash From Windows
summary: Microsoft is automatically removing Adobe Flash from Windows operating systems via an update, following Adobe's official end of support and "time bomb" implementation that disabled Flash content. While this update, KB4577586, will significantly reduce Flash installations, it won't remove manually installed standalone versions, requiring users to follow Adobe's uninstallation instructions for those instances.

title: Amplifying Black Creatives: How Adobe’s Black Employee Network creates a different kind of change
summary: Adobe's Black Employee Network (BEN) plays a crucial role in fostering diversity and inclusion within the company and the broader tech industry. Through various initiatives, BEN supports Black employees' professional growth, addresses racial injustice, and promotes diverse voices. The article highlights the experiences of three Black Adobe employees—Caitlin Crews, Ronell Hugh, and Ransford Hyman—who share how BEN has positively impacted their careers and provided a supportive community.

title: Microsoft users targeted by fake FedEx and DHL emails
summary: A new phishing scam is targeting Microsoft email users with fake emails purporting to be from FedEx and DHL. These emails contain malicious links that, if clicked, lead to lookalike login pages designed to steal user credentials for Microsoft, Adobe, or work email accounts. Security researchers at Armorblox Inc. have identified at least 10,000 Microsoft email users targeted by this attack, which leverages the familiarity of delivery services to trick victims.

title: The Role of Parametric 3D Materials in Color and Material Design | Nicolas Paulhac
summary: This article explores how parametric 3D materials, particularly those from Adobe's Substance toolset, are transforming color and material design (CMF) in product development. It highlights the benefits of using 3D visualization for faster, more collaborative, and cost-effective design processes, emphasizing the importance of photorealism and detail for effective communication and client engagement. The piece details a project undertaken by Nicolas Paulhac and his team to demonstrate the practical application and advantages of this technology within a modern product design workflow.

title: Introducing the 2021 Adobe Analytics Champions
summary: Adobe announced its 2021 Adobe Analytics Champions program, recognizing 40 individuals for their expertise and dedication to Adobe Analytics. These Champions will participate in career development, knowledge sharing, and provide feedback to Adobe product teams, while also helping other users improve their skills. The program celebrates passionate and knowledgeable Adobe Analytics users globally.

title: Time to shine — announcing the 2021 Adobe Experience Maker Awards
summary: Adobe has announced the 2021 Adobe Experience Maker Awards, which will spotlight companies and individuals who delivered exceptional customer experiences. New this year is the "Illuminator" category for resilience, and three categories (Experience Maker of the Year, Team of the Year, and Executive of the Year) will have regional winners. The awards span 15 categories, recognizing achievements using Adobe Experience Cloud products.

title: Austin Community College prepares students for success in a digital future
summary: Austin Community College (ACC) is distinguishing itself as the country's first community college to become an Adobe Creative Campus, focusing on integrating digital fluency across all academic disciplines. Through initiatives like the Digital Literacy Equity and Inclusion for All (DLEI) and faculty fellowship programs, ACC aims to equip students and staff with essential digital skills using Adobe Creative Cloud tools. This commitment extends beyond the classroom, with ACC using Adobe solutions for administrative efficiency and offering students comprehensive support for their online learning endeavors.

title: Discover the future of work management at Adobe Summit
summary: The Collaborative Work Management Track at Adobe Summit will showcase the integration of Workfront with Adobe enterprise solutions, emphasizing their combined power for marketing lifecycle management. Attendees will hear from Adobe and Workfront leadership, customers like Stanley Black & Decker and The Walt Disney Company, and explore various sessions on work visibility, agile management, and technology adoption. The article highlights the increased productivity and creativity expected from this integration, providing a single system for planning, collaboration, and governance.

title: Autodesk Inc. stock underperforms Monday when compared to competitors
summary: Autodesk Inc. (ADSK) shares fell 5.34% on Monday, closing at $253.10, despite a mixed trading session for the broader market. The stock underperformed against key competitors like Apple Inc. (AAPL), Adobe Inc. (ADBE), and Synopsys Inc. (SNPS), all of which also experienced declines. Trading volume for Autodesk surpassed its 50-day average.

title: FDA calls for an advisory meeting for anemia drug from FibroGen
summary: The FDA has called for an advisory meeting to review the new drug application for roxadustat, an anemia drug from FibroGen and AstraZeneca, ahead of its planned approval date. This decision led to a significant drop in FibroGen's stock price and follows a citizen's petition raising concerns about the drug's cardiovascular safety and mortality data. FibroGen has refuted these claims, asserting the petition lacks scientific and legal basis and noting the drug's prior approvals in other countries.

title: Adobe named to Fast Company’s list of the World’s Most Innovative Companies for 2021
summary: Adobe has been recognized by Fast Company on its list of the World’s Most Innovative Companies for 2021, specifically for its disruptive work in Artificial Intelligence (AI). The company was highlighted for bringing computing intelligence to the camera viewfinder with Adobe Photoshop Camera, an app built on Adobe Sensei. Adobe emphasized its continuous innovation, driven by AI and machine learning, to enhance digital experiences across its product portfolio.

title: ServiceNow Introduces New Version of the Now Platform, Giving Businesses Must-Have Digital Tools to Workflow Disruptive Change
summary: ServiceNow has unveiled the Now Platform Quebec release, featuring expanded native AI capabilities and new low-code app development tools like Creator Workflows, App Engine Studio, and App Engine Templates. This update aims to accelerate digital transformation, improve productivity, and deliver enhanced experiences for customers and employees. It enables businesses to build applications quickly and efficiently, supporting distributed workforces and optimizing operations with advanced AI and machine learning.

title: Epidemic Sound valued at $1.4bn as Blackstone joins $450m investment round in Swedish company
summary: Swedish music company Epidemic Sound has secured a $450 million funding round, including investment from Blackstone Growth and EQT Growth, valuing the company at $1.4 billion. The new capital will be used for continued international expansion, customer acquisition, enhancing user experience, and investing in music and digital offerings. Epidemic Sound's business model involves licensing rights-cleared production music and generating royalties from streaming services, with a commitment to pay 50% of streaming revenue to composers.

title: ServiceNow enhances its Now Platform
summary: ServiceNow has launched its Now Platform Quebec release, introducing enhanced native AI capabilities and new low-code app development tools. This update aims to enable faster innovation, improved productivity, and enhanced experiences for customers by empowering users to build applications with varying technical skill levels. The platform further introduces Creator Workflows and new solutions for process optimization, workforce optimization, and enhanced employee experiences, helping organizations adapt to new ways of working and digital transformation challenges.

title: Adobe Digital Economy Index: COVID-19 Report
summary: The Adobe Digital Economy Index report analyzes the lasting impact of COVID-19 on eCommerce, revealing an extra boost of $183 billion to online spending and projecting 2022 to be the first trillion-dollar year for eCommerce. It highlights significant changes in consumer behavior, such as the boom in "Buy Now, Pay Later" options, surging online grocery sales, and persistent out-of-stock messages. The report also notes a slump in digital purchasing power and atypical pricing trends, with digital inflation observed across major online categories.

title: An Accidental Astrophysicist | The Current
summary: Bill Paxton, a veteran software designer previously involved with Adobe Systems and PDF development, has been honored with the 2021 Beatrice M. Tinsley Prize by the American Astronomical Society. Despite not being an astrophysicist, Paxton revolutionized the field by developing MESA, an open-source stellar modeling software at UC Santa Barbara's Kavli Institute for Theoretical Physics. His work has significantly democratized access to computational stellar astrophysics, enabling widespread research and education.

title: NetApp Overhauls Global Sales Organization to Accelerate Cloud-Led Growth
summary: NetApp is overhauling its global sales organization and go-to-market model to accelerate cloud-led growth and better meet customer needs. These changes, announced by President César Cernuda, aim for deeper specialization and hybrid cloud expertise, driven by digitization. The restructuring also involves key leadership appointments, including Rick Scurfield as Chief Commercial Officer, Max Long as SVP, North America, and Alex Wallner as SVP, International, to enhance customer engagement and expand market reach.

title: An Oregon utility's unique model for supporting clean energy goals
summary: Portland General Electric (PGE) has developed a unique Green Future Impact (GFI) program in Oregon, acting as a model for how utilities can help large companies and cities access renewable energy. The program streamlines the procurement of clean energy through aggregated power purchase agreements and customer-identified projects, accelerating the deployment of renewable resources in the state. By handling regulatory and legal hurdles, PGE makes it easier for customers like Intel to meet their clean energy goals, demonstrating a successful model for other utilities to adopt.

title: The start of something new
summary: Adi Muraru, Principal Scientist for Adobe Romania, reflects on his over 10-year career at Adobe, emphasizing the company's commitment to innovation and its appealing work culture. He highlights his experience working on pioneering technologies like Primetime and Experience Manager, and the transformative impact of early big data projects. Muraru finds inspiration in Adobe's values and its people, who constantly strive to solve complex problems and drive continuous improvement.

title: World-class experience makers headline Adobe Summit keynotes
summary: The article highlights the upcoming Adobe Summit keynotes featuring influential speakers like Serena Williams, Dan Levy, and Malcolm Gladwell. These luminaries will share insights on diverse topics such as navigating post-pandemic opportunities, customer experience, disruption, trust, and leadership. Attendees are encouraged to register for the virtual event to gain transformative perspectives for their careers and businesses.

title: Introducing the first Adobe International Advisory Board
summary: Adobe has announced the formation of its first International Advisory Board, comprising six highly respected industry leaders. The board members bring extensive experience from various sectors, and will collaborate with Adobe's leadership to guide customers in adapting to new digital experiences. The initiative aims to leverage diverse global perspectives to address industry-specific challenges and support digital transformation efforts worldwide.

title: John Murphy to Retire as Adobe CFO
summary: Adobe's CFO, John Murphy, announced his retirement after three years with the company to focus on philanthropy. During his tenure, Adobe's revenue significantly increased, and the company made key acquisitions like Marketo and Workfront. Adobe's shares rose following his retirement announcement and improved financial projections.

title: Scott Belsky: What It Takes to Keep Your Business Ahead of the Pace of Technology
summary: Scott Belsky, Adobe's chief product officer and co-founder of Behance, shares insights on how businesses can stay ahead of technological advancements. He emphasizes building incubation labs, focusing hiring on creative problem-solvers rather than those skilled at mundane tasks, and leveraging technology to enhance customer experience. Belsky's advice centers on integrating new tech tools and continuously improving digital interactions to foster innovation and productivity.

title: How API-everything, developer creativity, and low code is the apex of IT agility
summary: This article discusses how an agile IT infrastructure, powered by APIs, developer creativity, and low-code solutions, is crucial for businesses to quickly adapt and innovate, especially during times of disruption.
