It highlights Adobe's approach with offerings like Adobe Experience Platform and Project Firefly, which enable developers to build custom applications efficiently. The piece also emphasizes the role of low-code development in empowering citizen developers and streamlining digital transformation.

title: The building blocks of Microsoft’s responsible AI program
summary: Microsoft emphasizes that for AI to benefit everyone, it must be developed and used in ways that earn trust. The company has established a robust governance model, including the Aether Committee and an Office of Responsible AI, to proactively set guardrails for AI systems. Microsoft also implements a "Responsible AI Standard" and a sensitive uses review process, which has led to drawing "red lines" for certain AI applications like real-time facial recognition for body cameras, underlining the importance of ethical considerations and industry-wide regulation.

title: What Is Predictive Modeling in Marketing?
summary: Predictive modeling in marketing utilizes historical data and statistical techniques to forecast future outcomes, helping businesses understand performance, mitigate risks, and enhance customer experiences. It involves a seven-step process, ranging from defining objectives and gathering data to developing and validating models. While distinct from AI and machine learning, these technologies can significantly enhance the efficiency and evaluation capabilities of predictive modeling, enabling rapid, real-time, and personalized customer interactions.

title: Sony achieved 800% faster web production time | Adobe Australia
summary: Sony Interactive Entertainment Japan Asia (SIEJA) significantly improved its web production time by 800% after implementing Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Analytics, and Adobe Target. This integration allowed them to update websites and content much faster, optimize website performance, and personalize user experiences, leading to increased user satisfaction and engagement. The shift to in-house content management also enabled more flexible and creative marketing strategies.

title: What’s Changed: 2021 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Experience Platforms
summary: This article analyzes the 2021 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Digital Experience Platforms (DXP), highlighting changes from the previous year. It details the vendors categorized as Leaders, Challengers, Niche Players, and Visionaries, explaining the strengths and recent updates for each, including new entrants and those no longer listed. The report emphasizes how DXPs support organizations in digitizing customer experiences across various business contexts.

title: How CDOT transformed infrastructure planning with Adobe Acrobat Sign and ProjectWise
summary: The Colorado Department of Transportation (CDOT) has revolutionized its infrastructure planning by implementing Adobe Acrobat Sign in conjunction with ProjectWise. This digital transformation has allowed CDOT to move away from paper-intensive processes, significantly reducing time, costs, and labor associated with reviewing and sealing massive project plans. Furthermore, the agency successfully lobbied for regulatory changes, simplifying the sealing process and further enhancing efficiency for engineers.

title: What sold, and for how much, in Ocala/Marion County
summary: This article lists warranty deed transfers in Ocala/Marion County, Florida, for prices exceeding $60,000, recorded from February 22-26, 2021. It details numerous property sales, including the names of sellers, buyers, and the sale prices across various subdivisions and estates within the county. Some noteworthy transactions include properties in Golden Ocala and Jumbolair Aviation Estates with high sale values.

title: HighRadius Raises $300M Series C at $3.1 Billion Valuation to Accelerate Growth in Order-to-Cash and Treasury Markets
summary: HighRadius, an AI-powered Order-to-Cash and Treasury Management software leader, has raised $300 million in a Series C funding round, bringing its valuation to $3.1 billion. The investment was led by D1 Capital and Tiger Global, with participation from existing investors and tech industry leaders. This capital will fuel product innovation and expand global market reach for HighRadius's platforms, which help over 600 clients optimize working capital.

title: PDFTron lands fifth acquisition target since US$71m capital raise
summary: PDFTron Systems Inc., a Vancouver-based tech company specializing in document processing tools, has made its fifth acquisition since securing a US$71 million investment in 2019. The company acquired Colorado's Windward Studios Inc., which specializes in document automation software. This move aligns with PDFTron's strategy to use its capital for global acquisitions and expanding its local research and development team, having already seen a 300% revenue growth since the capital raise.

title: Lessons learned after 21 years on Fortune’s list of 100 Best Companies to Work For
summary: Adobe celebrates its 21st year on Fortune's 100 Best Companies to Work For list, ranking 18th in 2021. The article outlines five key lessons learned over these years, emphasizing the importance of employees, unchanging core values, fostering innovation, building a diverse workplace, and being a purpose-driven company. This recognition, especially during 2020, underscores Adobe's commitment to its people and culture.

title: PlanGrid Announces Powerful New Integration Capabilities and Expanded Partner Ecosystem
summary: PlanGrid has announced PlanGrid Connect, an expansion of its API and a suite of new direct pre-built integrations, significantly bolstering its commitment to its partner ecosystem. This development allows construction teams to easily customize integrations between PlanGrid and over 100 other software solutions, eliminating the need for IT resources and preventing manual data entry errors. The new capabilities streamline information sharing, enabling real-time data access and improved collaboration across complex construction projects.

title: NVIDIA Launches Omniverse Design Collaboration and Simulation Platform for Enterprises
summary: NVIDIA has announced the general availability of NVIDIA Omniverse Enterprise, a platform enabling global 3D design teams to collaborate in real-time within a shared virtual space. This platform facilitates seamless work on complex projects for geographically dispersed teams, eliminating the need for in-person meetings or file exchanges. Major companies like BMW Group, Foster + Partners, and WPP are already leveraging Omniverse Enterprise for applications ranging from factory digital twins to virtual ad production, highlighting its transformative potential across various industries.

title: Adobe Cancelation Fee Sees Twitter Users Backing Pirated Software
summary: A $291 Adobe cancelation fee has caused widespread criticism of the company among Twitter users, with many sharing similar experiences of steep cancellation penalties. The incident has sparked a debate about the "moral correctness" of pirating Adobe software, with some users suggesting alternatives to Adobe products. Adobe's inconsistent cancellation terms for various subscriptions and contract types are also highlighted.

title: C++ at Adobe with Sean Parent, senior principal scientist
summary: Sean Parent, a senior principal scientist at Adobe, discusses the extensive use of C++ in Adobe's products, including Photoshop, and its benefits in terms of reach and performance across diverse platforms. He highlights innovations such as enhanced concurrency, unified computational environments, and the development of tools like Hyde and collaboration on Halide for image processing. Parent emphasizes the ongoing opportunities and challenges in reinventing Photoshop and invites C++ developers to join Adobe.

title: Google, Adobe and Samsung top best company culture list for 2021
summary: Google, Adobe, and Samsung have topped Comparably's 2021 Best Global Company Culture list. Google led with an 85% employee approval rating, while Adobe followed closely with 96%, and Samsung was the only Asian company in the top 50. The rankings were based on anonymous employee feedback across 20 workplace culture categories, factoring in companies' handling of the pandemic and employee well-being.

title: Founder of Adobe and developer of PDFs dies at age 81
summary: Charles "Chuck" Geschke, co-founder of Adobe Inc. and a key developer of Portable Document Format (PDF) technology, has died at age 81. Alongside John Warnock, Geschke also developed Adobe PostScript, revolutionizing desktop publishing. The company and the wider technology industry mourn his passing, remembering his relentless drive for innovation.

title: Adobe co-founder, Los Altos resident Charles ‘Chuck’ Geschke dies
summary: Charles M. “Chuck” Geschke, co-founder of Adobe Systems and Los Altos resident, has died at age 81. Geschke co-founded Adobe with John Warnock in 1982, creating PostScript which revolutionized desktop publishing, and the company later expanded significantly into various software areas. He was also notable for his kidnapping in 1992 and his extensive philanthropic work and awards recognizing his contributions to technology and education.

title: Adobe co-founder Charles Geschke dies at 81
summary: Charles "Chuck" Geschke, co-founder of Adobe Inc. and co-developer of PDF technology, passed away at 81. He co-founded Adobe in 1982 with John Warnock, playing a pivotal role in the desktop publishing revolution and receiving the National Medal of Technology and Innovation. Geschke was also known for his contributions to the San Jose community and his commitment to family, enduring personal traumas including a kidnapping.

title: Charles Geschke, founder of Adobe and developer of PDFs, dies at age 81
summary: Charles "Chuck" Geschke, co-founder of Adobe Inc. and a key developer of Portable Document Format (PDF) technology, has died at age 81. Geschke, along with John Warnock, revolutionized how people create and communicate through groundbreaking software like Adobe PostScript, Acrobat, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and Photoshop. He was also awarded the National Medal of Technology by President Barack Obama in 2009 and survived a kidnapping attempt in 1992.

title: Adobe co-founder Charles Geschke dead at 81
summary: Charles "Chuck" Geschke, co-founder of Adobe Inc. and co-developer of the Portable Document Format (PDF), died on Friday, April 16, 2021, at the age of 81. Geschke, who founded Adobe in 1982 with John Warnock, was recognized with the National Medal of Technology in 2009 by President Barack Obama. He is remembered by his company and family for his significant contributions to the technology industry and his dedication to his loved ones.
authors: Bob D'Angelo|Cox Media Group National Content Desk

title: Charles “Chuck” Geschke Dies: Co-Founder of Adobe Inc., Which Developed The PDF, Was 81
summary: Charles "Chuck" Geschke, co-founder of Adobe Inc. and instrumental in the development of the PDF, has died at the age of 81. Geschke, along with John Warnock, created groundbreaking software like Adobe PostScript, revolutionizing desktop publishing and producing products such as Photoshop and Illustrator. He was awarded the National Medal of Technology by President Barack Obama in 2009.

title: Adobe's John Warnock recalls his partnership with co-founder Chuck Geschke: 'We got along famously'
summary: John Warnock, co-founder of Adobe, reflected on his four-decade-long friendship and working partnership with Charles Geschke, who passed away on Friday at 81. Their collaboration was instrumental in changing professional workstyles. Warnock fondly recalled their famously good relationship.

title: Charles Geschke: Adobe co-founder who helped develop the PDF dies
summary: Charles Geschke, the co-founder of Adobe and a key figure in the development of the PDF, has died at 81. Geschke co-founded Adobe in 1982 and was instrumental in its success, though he also made headlines for a kidnapping incident in 1992. His contributions to software were recognized with the National Medal of Technology in 2009.

title: Founder of Adobe and developer of PDFs dies at age 81
summary: Charles "Chuck" Geschke, co-founder of Adobe Inc. and a key developer of Portable Document Format (PDF) technology, has died at the age of 81. Geschke, who also received the National Medal of Technology alongside John Warnock, left Xerox in 1982 to establish Adobe.

title: Inventor of PDFs passes away
summary: Charles "Chuck" Geschke, co-founder of Adobe Inc. and instrumental in developing PDF technology, passed away at age 81. He co-founded Adobe with John Warnock in 1982, revolutionizing desktop publishing with software like PostScript, PDF, Acrobat, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and Photoshop. Geschke was recognized with the National Medal of Technology by President Barack Obama in 2009.

title: Co-founder of Adobe, who helped develop PDF technology, dies aged 81
summary: Charles 'Chuck' Geschke, the co-founder of Adobe Inc. and co-developer of the Portable Document Format (PDF), has died at the age of 81. Geschke, along with John Warnock, created groundbreaking software like Adobe PostScript and spearheaded the desktop publishing revolution. He was also known for surviving a kidnapping in 1992 and was awarded the National Medal of Technology in 2009.

title: Charles "Chuck" Geschke, Co-Founder of Adobe Inc Dies at 81
source: The Sentinel - of this Land, for its People
source_domain: The Sentinel - of this Land, for its People
summary: Charles "Chuck" Geschke, co-founder of Adobe Inc., passed away at the age of 81. Geschke was instrumental in developing groundbreaking software, including the ubiquitous PDF, Acrobat, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, and Photoshop. Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen lauded his contributions, stating that Chuck instilled a relentless drive for innovation in the company and was a guide and hero to the technology industry.

title: Charles Geschke, the co-founder of Adobe, has passed away at 81: Contributions to Technology & Innovation
summary: Charles Geschke, co-founder of Adobe and co-inventor of the PDF, has passed away at 81. He is celebrated for his profound contributions to technology and innovation, revolutionizing how people create and communicate through software like Photoshop, Illustrator, and Premiere Pro. Geschke's dedication led to numerous accolades, including the IEEE Computer Society’s Computer Entrepreneur Award and the National Medal of Technology and Innovation.

title: Chuck Geschke, Father of Desktop Publishing, Dies at 81 (Published 2021)
summary: Chuck Geschke, co-founder of Adobe and a pioneer in desktop publishing, has died at 81. He and John Warnock developed PostScript, a crucial technology that enabled digital documents to be sent to printers, revolutionizing how businesses and individuals could print their own materials. Their innovation at Adobe, including the PDF, significantly influenced the development of personal computing and digital document sharing.

title: Charles Geschke, Adobe co-founder who helped spark desktop publishing, dies at 81
summary: Charles Geschke, co-founder of Adobe, has passed away at the age of 81. Geschke was instrumental in sparking the desktop publishing revolution, having been fascinated by the science of letterpress printing from a young age due to his family's background in photoengraving. He retired as president of Adobe in 2000.

title: Charles Geschke (1939–2021), co-founder of Adobe
summary: Charles Geschke, co-founder of Adobe and a driving force behind desktop publishing and popular software like Photoshop and PDFs, passed away at 81. Geschke, who started Adobe with John Warnock after their work at Xerox, served as COO and president and was awarded the National Medal of Technology and Innovation. Tributes highlight his entrepreneurial spirit and focus on people as key to Adobe's success.

title: Charles Geschke (1939–2021), co-founder of Adobe
summary: Charles Geschke, co-founder of Adobe, passed away on April 16, 2021, at the age of 81. He was instrumental in developing PostScript, which led to the first desktop publishing software and revolutionized the printing industry. Geschke, who served as Adobe's COO and president, was also known for his focus on the creativity of individuals within the company.

title: Adobe shareholders sign off on exec raises, with CEO Shantanu Narayen winning a plush $7m pay rise
summary: Adobe shareholders approved an executive compensation package for fiscal 2020, resulting in CEO Shantanu Narayen receiving a $7 million pay bump, bringing his total earnings to $45.9 million. Other executives also saw significant pay increases, largely due to performance-based equity awards and cash bonuses. This decision follows Adobe's strong financial performance in FY2020, with revenues up 15 percent to $12.87 billion and profits reaching $5.2 billion.

title: How Thermo Fisher Scientific became the hub for life sciences products
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific, a key manufacturer of life sciences equipment, leveraged Adobe Experience Manager to create a more harmonized and accessible website experience. This digital transformation, accelerated by the COVID-19 pandemic, enabled scientists and researchers to quickly find essential products and information, significantly boosting web traffic and lead generation. The company's cohesive digital strategy helped break down internal silos and improve customer access to critical supplies during the pandemic.

title: FedEx is the engine of e-commerce during COVID-19
summary: FedEx has served as a crucial engine for e-commerce during the COVID-19 pandemic, leveraging Adobe Experience Cloud solutions to meet surging customer demands. The company rapidly expanded capacity and adapted its digital platforms to handle increased online shopping, personalizing experiences for businesses and consumers alike. FedEx's long-term planning and agility, along with tools like FedEx Delivery Manager, enabled them to navigate unprecedented growth and a challenging holiday season.

title: Lovesac builds a customer experience platform for life with Adobe Commerce
summary: Lovesac, recognized as the fastest-growing furniture retailer, has partnered with Adobe Commerce to build a flexible and scalable customer experience platform. This move supports their "designed for life" philosophy and sustainable products, enabling them to adapt to changing customer behaviors and thrive, especially during the COVID-19 pandemic. The partnership has led to double-digit growth in conversion rates and average order values and positions Lovesac for continuous innovation in its online presence.

title: Panera Bread turns to Adobe Experience Platform as it accelerates frictionless dining and real-time personalization
summary: Panera Bread is partnering with Adobe to create a data-driven, real-time personalized experience for its millions of customers across both digital and physical channels. This collaboration, leveraging Adobe Experience Platform services, aims to deliver a frictionless dining experience, from order placement to guest recovery. The initiative reflects Panera's strategic focus on digital services and technology to meet evolving consumer expectations and enhance customer engagement.

title: Microsoft will permanently remove Flash from Windows PCs by July 2021
source: The Record from Recorded Future News
source_domain: The Record from Recorded Future News
summary: Microsoft announced its plan to permanently remove Adobe Flash Player from Windows PCs by July 2021, addressing a significant security risk left by Flash's end-of-life status. This removal process, which began with optional updates in September 2020 and expanded to silent deployments in February 2021, aims to eliminate the defunct software that no longer receives updates and is vulnerable to exploitation. The move is largely uncontroversial given that Adobe itself embedded a "time bomb" rendering Flash unusable after January 2021, making its removal a secure and practical solution.

title: Adobe CEO: We’re in the ‘sweet spot’ of the world’s reliance on tech
summary: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen discusses how the company transitioned from Flash software to a successful Creative Cloud subscription service, benefiting from the global reliance on technology. He highlights Adobe's three businesses being in the "sweet spot" of technological need and his strategies for continued growth, along with addressing the future of women and people of color in the workplace post-pandemic with Catalyst's Lauren Pasquarella Daley.

title: Microsoft July Update To Remove Flash from Windows Systems
summary: Microsoft is planning to remove Adobe Flash Player from most Windows systems in July via a Windows update, KB4577586. This update will be delivered through the "Latest Cumulative Update" for Windows 10 and via Monthly Rollup for older Windows systems, likely on July 13. Users can also manually remove Flash now by downloading KB4577586 from the Microsoft Update Catalog.

title: Mitsubishi Motors increases digital sales with Adobe
summary: Mitsubishi Motors Australia partnered with Isobar and Adobe to implement a digital transformation strategy focused on customer-first online experiences. By leveraging Adobe Experience Cloud applications like Analytics, Audience Manager, Experience Manager, Target, Campaign, and Commerce, they achieved a 3,000% increase in online business leads. The initiative aims to connect online customer data with offline dealerships to personalize interactions, improve efficiency, and accelerate sales, ultimately creating a seamless car-buying journey for customers.

title: Malcolm Gladwell Says the Post-Pandemic World Will Be ‘a Much Better Place’
summary: Malcolm Gladwell predicts that the post-pandemic world will be a more hopeful place, characterized by a shift from hierarchical to network-based organizational structures. He argues that the pandemic demonstrated the effectiveness of decentralized approaches, citing remote work and the vaccine rollout as prime examples. Gladwell believes this triumph of the network model will lead to a better future.

title: Valmont acquires crop analytics startup Prospera for $300m
summary: US irrigation equipment company Valmont Industries has acquired Israeli crop analytics startup Prospera for $300 million. This acquisition creates the largest vertically integrated AI company in agriculture, focusing on digitizing center pivot irrigation systems. Prospera chose the M&A route over continuing multiple partnerships due to its expertise in technology rather than direct-to-consumer sales and marketing.

title: Adobe completes Australian IRAP assessment for Adobe sign
summary: Adobe Sign has completed an Australian IRAP assessment, verifying its security standards for government use. This assessment, conducted against the Information Security Manual (ISM), enables Australian Government agencies to confidently adopt Adobe Sign for digital document and signature automation. The move addresses citizen concerns about data security and trustworthiness in digital government services, as highlighted in the Adobe and Deloitte Blueprint for Enhanced Citizen Experiences report.

title: International Relocation Is Not the Only Type of Work Mobility Needed to Attract Great Talent
summary: This article discusses how workplace mobility has evolved beyond traditional international relocation, driven by technology and new work models. Belgin Ertam of GE MENAT highlights that successful companies like GE are creating diverse opportunities for employees regarding their work "place" to attract and retain talent. It suggests that a broader understanding of mobility, beyond just physical relocation, is crucial in the modern workforce.

title: Contingent workforce grows, as does battle over its future
summary: The increasing reliance on contingent workers is driving changes in HR software and sparking political debate. Companies like Utmost Software and Beeline Inc. are developing "extended workforce systems" to manage these workers more effectively, with a focus on talent management rather than solely procurement. However, the growth of this workforce also brings compliance challenges and ongoing discussions about worker classification and benefits, as seen with legislative efforts in California and policy shifts under the Trump and Biden administrations.

title: Best practices to scale design with Design Systems
summary: This article discusses how design systems are crucial for scaling design efforts and improving business outcomes. It highlights that successful design systems comprise five key elements: design principles, foundations, components, guidelines, and resources. The article also emphasizes the importance of dedicated roles for managing these systems and establishing clear adoption and outcome metrics to measure their effectiveness and secure continued investment.

title: Bozeman: The New Executive Headquarters of Snowflake, Inc
summary: Snowflake, Inc., the largest software IPO last year with a nearly $70 billion market cap, has announced that its executive headquarters will be relocated from San Mateo, California, to Bozeman, Montana. This move follows the company's earnings release, with CEO Frank Slootman and CFO Mike Scarpelli already based in Bozeman, reflecting the company's embrace of remote work. The new headquarters will be located at 106 East Babcock Street in downtown Bozeman.

title: Announcing the 2021 Adobe Experience Maker Awards Finalists
summary: Adobe has announced the finalists for its 2021 Experience Maker Awards, recognizing customers who have innovated using Adobe Experience Cloud solutions amid challenging times. The awards also include the Lion Awards for Workfront customers, celebrating advancements in work management. Finalists in 12 global categories and several regional "Experience Maker" categories were selected from a record number of submissions, and winners will be announced at virtual galas in June.

title: SRM Technologies Announces Collaboration With Adobe
summary: SRM Technologies has joined the Adobe Solution Partner Program to enhance its offerings around Adobe Experience Cloud, focusing on Adobe Experience Manager, Adobe Commerce, and Adobe Analytics. This collaboration aims to strengthen SRM Technologies' capabilities in digital marketing, commerce, and online services, particularly for eCommerce customers in Japan, USA, and India. The move is crucial for businesses adapting to changing consumer behavior and relying on digital transformation.

title: Adobe: Creative Cloud app performance surges more than 80% on M1 MacBook Pro vs. Intel-based systems
summary: Adobe's Creative Cloud applications demonstrate over 80% faster performance on Macs equipped with Apple's M1 processor compared to Intel-based systems, according to a study commissioned by Adobe. Native M1 versions of several Creative Cloud apps have been released, with Premiere Pro currently in public beta. Key findings include significant performance gains for Adobe Sensei features and apps like Photoshop and Lightroom Classic on the M1 chip.

title: Premiere Pro Beta on Apple M1: The results are in
summary: The Premiere Pro Beta for Apple M1 has shown significant performance improvements, with testing by Pfeiffer Report revealing it is 77 percent faster on average than comparable Intel-based systems. Key areas like importing, playback, and exports, as well as specific effects and Adobe Sensei features, exhibit substantial speed enhancements. The optimizations also contribute to improved battery life due to efficient use of the M1’s Unified Memory.

title: Zoom and Salesforce make quick $23 million profit after IPO of Israeli software vendor Monday.com
summary: Zoom and Salesforce each invested $75 million into Monday.com's IPO, quickly realizing a combined profit of $23 million as the stock jumped 15% on its debut day. This investment strategy is part of a larger trend for Salesforce Ventures, which has yielded significant gains from other IPOs like Snowflake, while Zoom is also beginning to use investments as a new business venture. Both companies are subject to a 180-day lock-up period before they can sell their shares.

title: Only 4.8% of designers are Black. Herman Miller, Adobe, and Gap unveil a plan to change that
summary: A new collective called the Diversity in Design (DID) Collaborative, launched by nearly two dozen companies including Herman Miller, Adobe, and Gap, aims to increase Black representation in the design profession. The collaborative plans to promote design in high schools, work with historically Black colleges to develop design curricula, and host a design fair to offer mentorship and internships. This initiative seeks to address systemic inequalities by investing in the talent pipeline for Black designers, moving beyond superficial efforts to create tangible change through specific programs and transparent metrics.

title: Adobe Begins a Much-Needed Overhaul of Premiere Pro's UI
summary: Adobe is starting a major overhaul of Premiere Pro's user interface, drawing inspiration from its simpler mobile video editor, Premiere Rush. The initial focus is on improving the project creation, import, and export workflows to make them more intuitive and efficient for users. These changes aim to address the steep learning curve traditionally associated with Premiere Pro, making it more accessible while retaining its professional capabilities.

title: How Octo is Modernizing Government Websites with XD
summary: Octo, an IT consultancy, plays a crucial role in modernizing U.S. federal government websites and digital services, balancing user experience demands with strict regulatory and accessibility requirements. They leverage Adobe Creative Cloud, particularly Adobe XD, to efficiently design and prototype government projects, ensuring compliance with standards like Section 508 and the U.S. Web Design System. This approach helps Octo win contracts, especially through technical challenges, by demonstrating human-centered design and delivering user-friendly, accessible, and responsive government platforms.

title: The Future of work at Adobe
summary: Adobe is transitioning to a hybrid work model, leveraging digital tools and workflows to support flexibility and remote work while prioritizing intentional in-person gatherings. The company surveyed employees to inform this strategy, which includes expanding remote options and utilizing a new digital campus app, Adobe Life, to enhance the employee experience. This long-term transformation aims to foster creativity and innovation regardless of location.

title: A supply-chain breach: Taking over an Atlassian account
summary: Check Point Research (CPR) uncovered and responsibly disclosed chained vulnerabilities in Atlassian's platform that could lead to full account takeover. These vulnerabilities, including XSS, CSRF, and Cookie Fixation, allowed attackers to bypass security measures like SameSite "Strict" cookies and HttpOnly cookies. The attack chain enabled taking over accounts on various Atlassian subdomains such as training.atlassian.com and jira.atlassian.com, posing significant risks to data collaboration and information sharing for Atlassian's millions of users.
authors: Dikla Barda|Yaara Shriki|Roman Zaikin and Oded Vanunu

title: PSA: Adobe Photoshop Sketch, Illustrator Draw leaving the App Store next month
summary: Adobe is removing Photoshop Sketch and Illustrator Draw from the App Store on July 19, 2021, though existing users can continue using them until January 10, 2022. The company strongly recommends users migrate to Adobe Fresco, where assets can be preserved. Adobe Fresco is available for free with premium features unlockable for an additional cost.

title: Salesforce Sells $8 Billion of Bonds to Fund Slack Acquisition
summary: Salesforce.com Inc. has completed an $8 billion bond sale to finance its $27.7 billion acquisition of Slack Technologies Inc. The debt offering, which saw more than $32 billion in orders, aims to expand Slack's reach within the corporate employee sector.

title: Growth, Large-Cap Funds Lead in Second Quarter
summary: The U.S. equity market continued its climb in 2021’s second quarter, with growth and large-cap funds leading the way. The Morningstar US Growth Index gained significantly, surpassing the Morningstar US Value Index, while mega-cap stocks and the energy sector also performed strongly. However, funds with heavy allocations to utilities, consumer defensive, and industrials sectors faced headwinds.

title: Caroline Silver joins the Adobe International Advisory Board
summary: Caroline Silver, an internationally renowned investment banker, has joined the Adobe International Advisory Board. Her extensive experience in financial services and other sectors will help Adobe's customers navigate digital transformation challenges, particularly given the heightened customer expectations and regulatory complexities in the financial industry. Her appointment marks a key step in the board's evolution, aiming to provide strategic guidance for businesses adopting digital strategies.

title: Caroline Silver Joins Adobe International Advisory Board
summary: Caroline Silver, an Advisory Partner at Moelis & Company and a veteran in international investment banking, has been appointed to the Adobe International Advisory Board. This board, chaired by Paul Robson, President International at Adobe, aims to support Adobe's customers in developing and implementing digital transformation strategies. Silver's extensive experience will be valuable in helping clients, including major financial institutions, adapt and thrive in the digital economy.

title: How to Translate Your Website Into Other Languages and Why It’s Important
summary: This article emphasizes the importance of translating websites for software providers expanding into new international markets, noting that nearly 66% of buyers prefer content in their primary language. It discusses how advancements in AI and neural machine translation have improved the speed and quality of website translation, shifting the translator's role more toward editing. The piece also outlines various translation services and stresses the need for thorough editorial review to ensure cultural appropriateness and accuracy before a translated website goes live.

title: Slack Technologies Inc. stock rises Friday, still underperforms market
summary: Slack Technologies Inc. (WORK) shares increased by 0.41% to $44.47 on Friday, ending a three-day losing streak, despite a positive trading session for the broader market. The stock closed just below its 52-week high of $44.99 and outperformed some competitors like Microsoft Corp. (MSFT) but still lagged behind the overall market performance. Trading volume was slightly below its 50-day average.

title: How Adobe Sign and Laserfiche empower the rise of the citizen developer
summary: The article discusses the rise of citizen developers in a new hybrid work environment, highlighting their role in improving operational efficiency and automating tasks. It emphasizes how platforms like Laserfiche (a no-code document workflow solution) and Adobe Sign (an e-signature tool) enable these citizen developers to digitize processes, reduce turnaround times, and free up IT personnel for more complex projects. A state agency's successful implementation of a digital statements-of-work process using these tools serves as a key example.

title: Intercontinental Exchange modernizes documentation for the global market
summary: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) has adopted Adobe Acrobat Sign to streamline its global operations, enhancing efficiency and security for various departments including sales, operations, legal, and HR. The switch from their previous e-signature vendor was driven by competitive cost, high security, and faster onboarding, with key integrations with platforms like Salesforce and Microsoft SharePoint. This transition has significantly reduced paper processes and accelerated document processing, with contracts being completed in minutes, even with global clients.

title: Sika brings innovation and sustainability to global business processes
summary: Sika, a global leader in construction and automotive materials, leverages Adobe Acrobat Sign to digitize and improve its business processes worldwide. The implementation has led to significant efficiency gains, evidenced by 3,000 transactions in the first six months and a 30% monthly increase in usage. Teams in Portugal and Saudi Arabia have seen faster approvals, reduced manual labor, and increased focus on customer value, highlighting the flexibility and ease of use of Acrobat Sign across various functions.

title: BIPOC and women employees ranked their CEOs: These were the top 10 for 2021
summary: A new Comparably survey reveals the top CEOs as ranked by their BIPOC and women employees in 2021, based on 10 million anonymous ratings. Eric Yuan of Zoom Video Communications topped the list for BIPOC employees, while Shantanu Narayen of Adobe ranked highest among women. The rankings also highlight a significant shift compared to previous years and note the limited presence of female CEOs at the very top.

title: Work From (Home) Anywhere. There’s No Turning Back.
summary: The Direxion Work From Home ETF (WFH) has significantly outperformed the S&P 500 and NASDAQ-100 since its inception, defying initial skeptical predictions. This robust performance is attributed to a secular shift towards digitized workplaces and increased IT spending, rather than a temporary pandemic-driven trend. The article highlights the continued growth prospects of companies enabling remote work, despite economic reopenings.

title: From collaborative apps in Microsoft Teams to Windows 365—here’s what’s new in Microsoft 365 at Inspire
summary: Microsoft unveiled significant updates to Microsoft 365 at Inspire, focusing on enhancing hybrid work experiences. Key announcements include new collaborative apps within Microsoft Teams, integrations between Dynamics 365 and Teams without additional licensing costs, and the introduction of Windows 365 Cloud PC for a personalized Windows experience from any device. The company also expanded Microsoft Viva with new partner integrations and development tools, alongside new security features like Microsoft 365 Lighthouse to help manage security postures for small and medium businesses.

title: Why Using Emojis in Marketing Can You Help Drive Sales, and Bond With Customers
summary: A new Adobe survey reveals that emojis are a powerful tool in marketing, customer service, and social media, driving sales and customer engagement. Forty-two percent of emoji users are more likely to purchase products advertised with emojis, and half find brands using them more relatable. Emojis also significantly boost email open rates and are preferred by nearly half of Gen Zers for customer support interactions.

title: Verb Technology's (VERB) Forecast in 2021: Is It a Good Stock to Buy?
summary: Verb Technology (VERB) stock has recently surged due to a new partnership and positive analyst coverage. Ascendiant Capital initiated a "buy" rating with a $4 target price, highlighting increased demand for Verb’s CRM and video marketing software. Despite its potential, analysts suggest the stock is currently overvalued after a rapid price increase, recommending caution.

title: Business Process Management Platform as a Service Market: Ready
summary: Ample Market Research & Consulting Private Limited has published a new study on the Business Process Management Platform as a Service (BPM PaaS) Market, evaluating its trends, opportunities, and risks. The report provides a detailed perspective on the BPM PaaS industry, segmented by applications like BFSI, Manufacturing, and Retail, and by product types such as Cloud BPM and On-premises BPM. It includes market sizes, growth forecasts up to 2025, and profiles key players including OpenText, Adobe Systems, and Oracle.

title: MonkeySports hits a home run with Adobe Commerce
summary: MonkeySports, a leading sporting goods retailer, successfully migrated its various digital storefronts from Magento Commerce 1 to Adobe Commerce (formerly Magento Commerce 2) to enhance scalability, customization, and security. This transition, supported by Perficient, involved integrating performance-accelerating tools and improving security frameworks, leading to significant increases in conversion rates and revenue within months of launch. The upgraded platform provides customers with a mobile-optimized experience, faster performance, and a streamlined checkout process.

title: Box Sign gives business users built-in digital signature tool
summary: Box Inc. has integrated digital signature capabilities, named Box Sign, into its cloud content management platform for Business and Enterprise subscribers, stemming from its $55 million acquisition of SignRequest. This new feature allows users to sign documents digitally at no extra cost, leveraging the growing demand for remote work tools and digital workflows. Despite a market with numerous digital signature vendors, analysts suggest significant growth potential, especially in mid-sized businesses, and Box plans to maintain an open platform for other integrators.

title: Walmart to sell its e-commerce technologies to other retailers
summary: Walmart is making its e-commerce and retail technologies, previously used to transform its own business, available to other retailers through a strategic partnership with Adobe. This collaboration will integrate Walmart's Marketplace, fulfillment, and pickup technologies into the Adobe Commerce Platform, allowing small to mid-sized retailers to utilize Walmart's tools. The initiative aims to provide new revenue streams for Walmart while potentially boosting its Marketplace through increased product listings and offering an expanded set of customers to Adobe's retail clients.

title: The Trade Desk vs. Adobe: Which Software Stock is a Better Buy?
summary: This article compares Adobe (ADBE) and The Trade Desk (TTD), two software companies expected to benefit from the ongoing demand for software solutions due to global digitalization and hybrid work trends. It analyzes their latest developments, financial results, expected performance, profitability, and valuation, concluding that Adobe is a better buy due to higher profitability and lower valuation despite TTD's edge in data management.

title: NVIDIA Advances Instant AI with North American Availability of Base Command Platform
summary: NVIDIA has announced the North American availability of its Base Command Platform, a hosted AI development hub providing instant access to NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD supercomputers. This platform, hosted by Equinix and part of the NVIDIA AI LaunchPad program, is designed for enterprises with large-scale, multi-user AI workflows, enabling them to accelerate AI projects from prototype to production. Adobe is an early adopter, exploring its potential to streamline machine learning development, and the platform is supported by partners like NetApp and Weights & Biases for integrated data management and MLOps tools.

title: Microsoft improves marketing operations efficiency with Microsoft Power Automate
summary: Microsoft's Global Demand Center leveraged Microsoft Power Automate to significantly improve the efficiency of its marketing operations, reduce costs, and free up employee time for higher-level tasks. By automating standardized and repeatable tasks in Adobe Marketo Engage, the GDC achieved a 26% reduction in program build costs and saved thousands of employee hours, while expanding its scope to cover the entire commercial portfolio. The initiative demonstrates how intelligent automation can sustain growth and innovation while managing budget constraints, with future plans to expand these capabilities to field marketers.

title: Spice up your summer designs with these additions to Adobe Fonts
summary: Adobe Fonts has released a variety of new typefaces and font families to enhance summer designs, catering to diverse needs from professional projects to casual use. The update includes contributions from new foundries like DX Korea and Design210, additions to existing families, and new individual fonts with signature styles. These new fonts offer a wide range of aesthetics, from vintage and art-deco inspired designs to modern sans serifs and expressive scripts, all available with an Adobe Creative Cloud subscription.

title: At home, at work, and in the community — Adobe Stock is helping visualize veterans beyond the battlefield
summary: Adobe Stock has launched the "Veterans Return" creative brief as part of its Advocates program, aiming to diversify the representation of U.S. military veterans and active service members in stock imagery. The initiative seeks to move beyond traditional "warrior" or "traumatized veteran" stereotypes, encouraging creators to depict veterans in varied civilian roles and reflecting the diverse nature of military service. Adobe Stock also supports veteran artists through its Artist Development Fund to empower them in telling their own stories.

title: Digital Market May See a Big Move | Adobe Systems Incorporated , Oracle , SAP SE , Salesforce , IBM , Marketo
summary: Ample Market Research has added a detailed report on the Digital Market, outlining current and future growth trends, supply and demand analysis, and key player participation. The report forecasts the market from 2020 to 2025, covering segmentation by type, application, and region, along with drivers, limitations, and opportunities. It also provides a competitive analysis of major industry players like Adobe Systems, Oracle, SAP SE, Salesforce, IBM, and Marketo.

title: Adobe congratulates award-winning changemakers
summary: Adobe is celebrating two of its public sector partners, the U.S. Census Bureau and the Los Angeles Department of Public Social Services (LA DPSS), for receiving 2021 Service to the Citizen Awards. The Census Bureau also earned the 2021 Adobe Experience Maker Award for successfully delivering the first online census amidst the pandemic, while the LA DPSS was recognized for fast-tracking digital initiatives and improving content efficiency. Adobe extends congratulations to the award recipients and their teams for their innovation and leadership.

title: Intuit Inc. stock falls Wednesday, still outperforms market
summary: Intuit Inc. (INTU) stock declined 0.54% on Wednesday, closing at $532.98, despite an overall market downturn that saw the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average fall more than 1%. The stock still outperformed several competitors, including Adobe Inc., SAP SE ADR, and Oracle Corp., all of which experienced larger percentage drops. Intuit Inc. was just $9.98 shy of its 52-week high reached two days prior.

title: Adobe buying Frame.io in $1.28B deal
summary: Adobe announced its acquisition of Frame.io, a video review and collaboration platform, for $1.275 billion in cash. Frame.io aims to streamline video creation workflows for creative professionals by centralizing media assets and enabling real-time feedback. This acquisition is expected to enhance Adobe's video editing products by integrating Frame.io's collaboration features.

title: Adobe acquires collaborative video editing platform ‘Frame․io’ for $1.3 billion
summary: Adobe has announced the acquisition of Frame.io, a cloud-based collaborative video editing platform, for approximately $1.3 billion. Frame.io, created in 2015, allows video editors to work in groups with comments and approvals, and was already frequently used by professionals working with Adobe software like Premiere Pro and After Effects. The acquisition aims to integrate Frame.io's features into Adobe's software to enhance collaborative video workflows, particularly with the growing demand from streaming services and social networks.

title: Adobe to Buy Video-Production Platform Frame.io for About $1.3 Billion
summary: Adobe announced its acquisition of Frame.io, a cloud-based video collaboration platform, for approximately $1.275 billion. The deal is expected to close in Adobe's fourth fiscal quarter of 2021, subject to regulatory approval. Frame.io's co-founders will join Adobe, and the platform's features will be integrated with Adobe's creative software products to enhance collaborative video workflows.

title: Adobe says it will acquire video-collaboration software company Frame.io for $1.275 billion
summary: Adobe announced its acquisition of Frame.io, a video-collaboration software company, for $1.275 billion to enhance its Creative Cloud video-editing applications. Frame.io's technology allows multiple people to provide input on videos and will integrate with Adobe's Premiere Pro and After Effects, strengthening its video editing capabilities. The deal is expected to close in Adobe's fourth fiscal quarter.

title: Adobe buying Frame.io in $1.28B deal
summary: Adobe announced its acquisition of Frame.io, a video review and collaboration platform, for $1.275 billion in cash. Frame.io, founded in 2014, helps creative professionals streamline video creation. The acquisition aims to enhance Adobe's creative software, particularly Premiere Pro and After Effects, by integrating Frame.io's collaborative functionalities.

title: Adobe buys collaborative video editing software firm Frame.io for $1.275B
summary: Adobe Inc. has acquired Frame.io Inc., a video review and collaboration platform provider, for $1.275 billion in cash. This acquisition aims to enhance Adobe's Creative Cloud, particularly its Premiere Pro and After Effects applications, by integrating Frame.io's tools for streamlined video creation and collaboration. The move responds to the growing demand for efficient video editing tools driven by the explosion of video content on social media and across various industries.

title: Adobe to Buy Frame.io for $1.3 billion in Cash in Video Push
summary: Adobe Inc. is acquiring video collaboration software startup Frame.io for approximately $1.3 billion in cash. This acquisition will integrate Frame.io's capabilities into Adobe's Creative Cloud, enhancing features alongside Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator. The move is part of Adobe's strategy to bolster its offerings in the video sector.
authors: Liana Baker|Isabella Simonetti and Crystal Tse

title: Adobe to buy Frame.io in $1.27 billion deal
summary: Adobe Inc. announced its plan to acquire Frame.io, a cloud-based video collaboration platform, for $1.27 billion. This acquisition aims to integrate Frame.io's workflow functionality with Adobe Creative Cloud's video capabilities to create an end-to-end video collaboration platform. The deal is expected to close in the fourth quarter, with Frame.io's co-founders joining Adobe.

title: Adobe to Acquire Frame.io for $1.28 billion, Soon to Feature Video Review Collaboration with Tools
summary: Adobe is set to acquire Frame.io for $1.28 billion, a move that will expand its "Creative Cloud" platform and enhance its video collaboration services. Frame.io, a video collaboration service founded in 2014, will help Adobe's Premiere Pro, After Effects, and other video editing tools reach a wider audience. This acquisition underscores Adobe's commitment to developing its products for the rapidly changing digital content landscape, particularly in video.

title: Adobe is acquiring collaborative video software maker Frame.io for $1.275 billion
summary: Adobe is acquiring Frame.io, a collaborative video production software maker, for $1.275 billion. Frame.io provides cloud-based tools for reviewing and giving feedback on video edits, integrating with popular editing software such as Adobe's Premiere Pro, Apple's Final Cut Pro, and Avid Media Composer. The acquisition aims to enhance Adobe's Creative Cloud offerings and will see Frame.io’s founders join Adobe, with Frame.io continuing to support non-Adobe software.

title: Wachtell directs Adobe’s $1.27 bln Frame.io purchase
summary: Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz is advising Adobe on its $1.275 billion acquisition of video review platform Frame.io. This deal allows Adobe to enhance collaboration for users during video creation and adds to Wachtell's growing portfolio of technology sector transactions. The firm has been active in other major tech M&A deals, including Square's purchase of Afterpay and Salesforce's acquisition of Slack.

title: Adobe acquires collaborative video cloud platform Frame.io
summary: Adobe has announced its acquisition of Frame.io, a collaborative video cloud platform, for $1.275 billion, subject to regulatory approval. This acquisition aims to enhance collaboration capabilities within Adobe Creative Cloud, particularly for video editing tools like Premiere Pro and After Effects, by integrating Frame.io's real-time footage upload and online collaboration features for creative teams. Frame.io's co-founders will join Adobe, and Frame.io will continue to serve its existing customer base while integrating with Adobe’s ecosystem.

title: 7 benefits of technology in the classroom
summary: This article highlights seven key benefits of integrating technology into the classroom, ranging from improved adaptability for diverse learners and enhanced collaboration to increased student engagement and cost reduction. It discusses how tools like cloud applications, digital document solutions, and educational games can transform teaching and learning experiences, while also promoting essential tech skills and streamlining administrative tasks. The author emphasizes that technology acts as a multiplier for educators, making learning more enjoyable and achieving better student outcomes.

title: How thinking like an entrepreneur helped Taki Tsaklanos of Bose pivot like a startup
summary: Taki Tsaklanos, lead of Bose's Center of Excellence for Customer Experience, shares how entrepreneurial thinking enabled the company to pivot like a startup, especially during the pandemic. Bose developed a virtual try-on experience for their Frames audio sunglasses, which significantly increased conversion rates. Tsaklanos emphasizes a consumer-first approach, continuous innovation, cross-functional collaboration, and resilience in adapting to new challenges.

title: An investor with a flair for tech could push Fiserv to the next level
summary: ValueAct Capital has invested $1.2 billion in Fiserv (FISV), believing the payment technology company is misunderstood by the market and poised for significant growth through transformation. ValueAct aims to assist Fiserv in leveraging its technology, like Clover, to achieve higher growth rates and better communicate its strategic opportunities to the market, drawing parallels to its successful investments in other perceived "legacy" tech companies like Microsoft and Adobe. This partnership could help Fiserv enhance its valuation by clarifying its position as a "new world company" with substantial growth potential.

title: Amazon Web Services works with Adobe Experience Cloud as it reimagines B2B marketing
summary: This article details how Amazon Web Services (AWS) leveraged Adobe Experience Cloud to transform its B2B marketing strategy, particularly in response to the COVID-19 pandemic. It highlights AWS's successful pivot to virtual events, such as re:Invent, and how Adobe's analytics, personalization, and marketing automation tools enabled a seamless and personalized digital customer journey. The collaboration aims to further enhance personalization and optimize customer engagement in future hybrid events.

title: Gartner Identifies Six Technologies to Drive New Customer Acquisition and Growth for Digital Marketing
summary: Gartner has identified six key technologies that digital marketers should leverage for new customer acquisition and growth in a post-pandemic world. These technologies include AI for Marketing, Multichannel Marketing Hubs, Influence Engineering, Customer Data Ethics, Event-Triggered Marketing, and Mobile Marketing Analytics. The shift from customer retention to acquisition highlights the increasing reliance on data-driven strategies and ethical considerations in marketing.

title: Introducing the 2021 Adobe Community Advisors
summary: Adobe has introduced its 2021 Community Advisors, recognizing individuals who actively strengthen the Adobe Experience League community. These advisors facilitate participation, answer questions, share expertise, and will also contribute through blogs, webinars, and user group meetings. The program aims to spotlight engaged and supportive community members, and active community members are encouraged to apply for future participation.

title: Prativa Mohapatra becomes the first woman to lead Adobe India
summary: Prativa Mohapatra has been appointed as vice president and managing director for Adobe India, making her the first woman to lead the company's operations in the country. She will oversee Adobe’s India business across its three main units and work with engineering leaders. This appointment aligns with Adobe's commitment to diversity, with women also in leadership roles in other Asia Pacific countries.

title: Cleaning up the confusion in marketing ops job postings
summary: The marketing operations field suffers from confusion in job postings, often blending various disciplines. This article advocates for clearer distinctions and a consistent vocabulary for roles like operations specialists, technologists, data experts, and product managers. It provides detailed high-level duties for each role to help create more precise job descriptions within the evolving martech landscape.

title: Chemours increases MQLs with Adobe Marketo Engage
summary: Chemours, a specialty chemicals company, significantly boosted its Marketing Qualified Leads (MQLs) by adopting Adobe Marketo Engage. This led to a 20% conversion rate from MQLs to sales-accepted leads through enhanced personalization, automated lead scoring, and improved integration between marketing and sales. The platform allowed Chemours to better engage with customers, optimize its marketing budget, and gain deeper insights into the buyer journey.

title: Who is Prativa Mohapatra, the new chief of Adobe India?
summary: Prativa Mohapatra has been appointed as the vice president and managing director for Adobe India, becoming the first woman to lead the software giant's operations in the country. With 25 years of experience, including a previous role at IBM, Mohapatra will oversee Adobe's India business across its Experience Cloud, Creative Cloud, and Document Cloud units. She is known for her expertise in building high-growth teams and leading business transformations, as well as mentoring women leaders.

title: E-signature platforms strengthen document management's clout
summary: E-signature platforms are emerging as a critical component of automated document management systems, driven by digital transformation and accelerated by the COVID-19 pandemic. They streamline workflows, reduce costs, improve efficiency, and enhance customer and employee experiences compared to traditional paper-based processes. The article highlights the differences between digital and electronic signatures and discusses the expanding role of e-signatures within enterprise applications, emphasizing the benefits of an enterprise-wide approach and integration with other platforms like Microsoft Teams and Salesforce.

title: Elastic Announces ElasticON Global Virtual Conference, Oct. 5 - 7, 2021
summary: Elastic announced details for its free, virtual ElasticON Global conference, scheduled for October 5-7, 2021. The event, themed "Solve," will feature keynotes from Elastic's CEO Shay Banon and CPO Ashutosh Kulkarni, as well as fireside chats with Microsoft EVP Scott Guthrie, chef José Andrés, and NASA's Diana Trujillo. The conference will also include over 100 technical sessions, a virtual exhibit hall, and opportunities for participants to enhance their skills with live training courses.

title: Adobe Inc. (ADBE) Stock: Should you invest in this recession-proof tech firm?
summary: Adobe Inc. (ADBE) has shown resilience and strong growth, outperforming the S&P 500, with analysts recommending the stock due to its long-term prospects and domination in the design software space. The company's cloud-based applications and consistent demand for digital documents, amplified by the work/learn from home trend, have driven impressive revenue and earnings growth. Adobe's Q2 2021 performance saw revenues climb 23% and adjusted earnings increase by 23%, with expectations for continued growth in Q3.

title: Adobe appoints Prativa Mohapatra as VP & MD for Adobe India
summary: Adobe has appointed Prativa Mohapatra as Vice President and Managing Director for Adobe India, making her the first woman to lead the company's digital experiences business in India. This appointment comes as Adobe celebrates strong financial performance, with Q2 FY2021 sales reaching $3.84 billion, a 23% year-over-year increase, highlighting India's crucial role in the company's growth. Mohapatra will oversee Adobe's India business across its three core units and work closely with engineering leaders.

title: The Market Remains Hot As We Kick Off Our Next Forrester Wave™ Evaluation Of The DAM Landscape
summary: Forrester is launching its next Wave evaluation for Digital Asset Management (DAM) due to significant market growth and increasing customer demand. Many organizations struggle with content creation challenges, such as outdated processes, content volume outpacing production capabilities, and the need for better personalization foundations. DAM solutions offer governance, centralization, metadata enrichment, content atomization, and AI-powered tagging, distinguishing them from basic cloud storage options.

title: PM Modi meets Adobe CEO, discusses ways to provide smart education to youngsters
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi met Adobe Systems President and CEO Shantanu Narayen to discuss smart education, Digital India, and the use of emerging technologies in health, education, and R&D. Narayen expressed interest in contributing to India's 75th anniversary of independence and bringing video and animation to children in India. Both leaders emphasized the importance of smart education and the creation of AI centers of excellence in India.

title: PM Modi holds meeting with First Solar CEO in Washington
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with First Solar CEO Mark R Widmar in Washington DC during his three-day US visit. The discussion focused on India's renewable energy landscape, as part of Modi's meetings with corporate heads who have potential investment interests in India. He also met with CEOs from Qualcomm and Adobe, and was scheduled to meet with leaders from Blackstone and General Atomics.

title: Modi meets Adobe CEO Narayen, discusses company's investment plans in India
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with Adobe President and CEO Shantanu Narayen to discuss the software giant's collaboration and future investment plans in India. The discussions covered India's Digital India initiative, the use of emerging technologies in health, education, and R&D, and emphasized creating AI centers of excellence. Narayen highlighted Adobe's robust and long-standing presence in India, calling it a "secret weapon" for innovation with multiple centers across the country.

title: Modi, Qualcomm CEO discuss semiconductor
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon in the US to discuss investment opportunities in India's telecom and electronics sectors, particularly focusing on the semiconductor supply chain and the government's production-linked incentive schemes. This meeting was Modi's first engagement upon his arrival in the US, highlighting India's push to attract semiconductor companies and build a local innovation ecosystem. Modi also met with top officials from Adobe, First Solar, General Atomics Global Corporation, and Blackstone to discuss various investment and collaboration opportunities in India.

title: PM Modi, Adobe CEO Discuss Company's Investment Plans In India
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen to discuss the company's investment plans in India and its collaboration on the Digital India initiative. The discussions also focused on the use of emerging technologies in health, education, and R&D, with both leaders emphasizing the creation of AI centers of excellence in India. Narayen praised India's COVID-19 vaccination efforts and expressed Adobe's commitment to advancing digital education in the country.

title: Marketing Resource Management Market Size, Share, Growth, Forecast, 2034
summary: The global Marketing Resource Management (MRM) market was valued at USD 5.61 billion in 2025 and is projected to grow to USD 16.28 billion by 2034, exhibiting a CAGR of 12.57% during the forecast period. This growth is driven by the increasing need for brand and regulatory compliance and the adoption of cloud-based MRM services, despite challenges like cyber-attacks. North America currently holds the largest market share due to the presence of major players and rapid cloud adoption, while Asia Pacific is expected to show significant growth due to digitization and the rising adoption of SaaS and cloud solutions by SMEs.

title: Human resources trends and technology
summary: This article discusses the evolving role of HR professionals in a post-pandemic world, highlighting key trends and technological advancements. It emphasizes the need for HR to adapt to remote work, prioritize employee well-being, invest in DEI initiatives, and adjust benefits and sick leave policies. The article also underlines the importance of leveraging new HR technologies and staying informed about COVID-19 related guidelines.

title: Shell Philippines fuels dealer partnerships with Adobe Acrobat Sign
summary: Shell Philippines, facing logistical challenges in managing contracts across its 1,100 fuel stations, adopted Adobe Acrobat Sign to streamline its partnership agreement process. This digital transformation has significantly reduced contract completion times from months to hours, saving the company approximately 1,000 hours annually in manual tasks and US$90,000 in costs. The successful implementation has also improved document security, archiving, and has encouraged dealers to embrace other technological solutions, leading to its expansion to more departments and even adoption by Shell Indonesia.

title: CORSAIR Debuts their new XENEON 32QHD165 Gaming Monitor, featuring an ultra-slim 32-inch QHD screen
summary: CORSAIR has launched its new XENEON 32QHD165 Gaming Monitor, featuring a 32-inch QHD IPS LED screen with a 165Hz refresh rate and compatibility with AMD FreeSync Premium and NVIDIA G-SYNC. The monitor is designed for gamers and creators, offering excellent color accuracy, HDR400 support, and integration with CORSAIR iCUE and Elgato Stream Deck software. It also boasts a versatile design with a unique stand featuring a 1/4" threading for mounting accessories and comprehensive connectivity options.

title: Adobe gets new CFO as it manages 22% revenue growth
summary: Adobe Inc. has appointed Dan Durn as its new CFO, effective October 18, succeeding John Murphy. Durn, previously CFO at Applied Materials and other semiconductor manufacturing companies, brings extensive experience in finance and strategy. His appointment comes as Adobe celebrates record revenue of nearly $4 billion in its most recent quarter, marking a 22% year-over-year increase, driven by its successful shift to a subscription-based model and strong performance in its digital media unit.

title: Adobe appoints Dan Durn Chief Financial Officer
summary: Adobe announced the appointment of Dan Durn as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer (CFO), effective October 18, 2021. He will succeed John Murphy, who is retiring. Durn brings extensive experience from Applied Materials, NXP Semiconductors, and GlobalFoundries, with a background in global strategy, financial planning, and mergers and acquisitions.

title: Western Digital Announces WD Blue SN570 M.2 NVMe SSD
summary: Western Digital has launched the new WD Blue SN570 NVMe SSD, targeting creators such as graphic designers and content creators with its fast and reliable storage solution. This internal flash drive offers read speeds up to 3,500 MB/s, comes in a slim M.2 2280 form factor, and includes a one-month membership to Adobe Creative Cloud. Available in capacities from 250 GB to 1 TB, pricing starts at $49.99, and it comes with a 5-year limited warranty.

title: Adobe Stock Tumbled, and CEO Shantanu Narayen Sold a Large Block of Shares
summary: Adobe stock experienced a downturn following its third-quarter earnings report. In response to this dip, CEO Shantanu Narayen sold a significant block of his shares. The article highlights this sale in the context of the company's recent stock performance.

title: American Express Launches New Enhanced Business Platinum Card
summary: American Express has launched its new enhanced Business Platinum Card, offering significant new benefits in top business spending categories, enhanced services, and travel perks for U.S. customers. The card provides access to over $1,400 in value annually for a $695 fee, along with increased rewards points for various business expenditures and new travel benefits such as CLEAR membership credit and discounts on Wheels Up. This launch reflects American Express's focus on meeting evolving customer needs and supporting B2B spending growth.

title: The U.S. American Express Business Platinum Card Is Now More Powerful than Ever with Enhanced Business and Travel Benefits
summary: American Express has launched a new Business Platinum Card in the U.S. offering over $1,400 in annual value through enhanced business spending categories, services, and travel perks. The card now provides increased rewards points in key business categories, statement credits for services like Dell Technologies, Indeed, Adobe, and wireless providers, and expanded premium travel and dining benefits including airport lounge access and private jet discounts. Existing benefits such as 5x points on flights and hotels, airline fee credits, and cell phone protection continue, though the annual fee will increase to $695 starting January 13, 2022.

title: Apple unveils game-changing MacBook Pro
summary: Apple has unveiled a completely reimagined MacBook Pro powered by the new M1 Pro and M1 Max chips, available in 14- and 16-inch models. These new laptops offer extraordinary performance, unprecedented battery life, and advanced features like a Liquid Retina XDR display, improved camera and audio systems, and extensive connectivity, marking a significant leap for professional users. The MacBooks are designed for various professionals, offer remarkable power efficiency, and feature an environmentally conscious design with recycled materials.

title: IBM Acquires Leading Adobe Workfront Consultancy
summary: IBM has announced its intention to acquire the Adobe Workfront consulting unit and assets from Rego Consulting Corporation. This acquisition is part of IBM's larger hybrid cloud and AI strategy, marking its 17th acquisition since April 2020. The move aims to enhance IBM's expertise in automating marketing workflows with AI and deepen its offerings in digital business transformation, leveraging its strategic partnership with Adobe.

title: Adobe named a World’s Best Workplace for sixth year in a row
summary: For the sixth consecutive year, Adobe has been recognized on Fortune Magazine’s list of 25 World’s Best Workplaces, ranking number 21. This global distinction was achieved by being named a Best Workplace in eight different countries, highlighting the company's commitment to creating an inclusive and supportive work environment. The recognition is based on surveys representing millions of employee opinions worldwide, celebrating Adobe's dedication to employee development, wellbeing, and innovation.

title: Trimble saves $500K in martech spend with Marketo Engage
summary: Trimble successfully consolidated its marketing technology by standardizing on Adobe Marketo Engage, leading to a reduction of over $500,000 in martech spend and a $400,000 annual decrease in external agency costs. This change allowed Trimble's diverse marketing teams to improve efficiency, enhance customer experience, and increase self-reliance in campaign execution. The company plans further integrations and automation to streamline marketing efforts and drive revenue growth.

title: How West Elm Designs Digital Experiences for Luxury Retail|Adobe
summary: West Elm discusses its approach to designing digital experiences for luxury retail, especially in response to the increased demand during the COVID-19 pandemic when customers could not visit showrooms. The company streamlined its workflow and enhanced collaboration using Adobe XD, enabling faster design iterations and a more cohesive customer experience. This shift allowed West Elm to quickly adapt to changing consumer expectations and maintain its distinct brand identity across multiple touchpoints.

title: Photoshop’s AI Is Now So Fast That Hovering Over Objects Instantly Creates Perfect Masks
summary: Adobe Photoshop has received a major update that significantly streamlines the masking process using AI-powered technology. Users can now simply hover their mouse over an object to instantly create a mask, and a new "Mask All Objects" feature automatically generates separate masks for every detected object. Additionally, Adobe announced that Photoshop and Illustrator will be available as limited-functionality beta versions on the web for Chrome and Edge browsers, facilitating easier collaboration.

title: Exclusive: Adobe’s mind-blowing AI turns napkin sketches into cartoons
summary: Adobe's new AI research projects, Project Sunshine and Project Make It Pop, can transform napkin sketches or photos and videos into richly detailed vector cartoons. Project Sunshine converts sketches into colorized, shaded vector images, while Project Make It Pop can turn photos or videos into cartoons with repositionable figures, even allowing characters to copy poses from other images or replace people in videos with cartoon avatars. These "Sneaks" demonstrate Adobe's focus on inventive tools for advanced illustration and aim to automate time-consuming design processes.

title: How AI is shaping Adobe’s product strategy
summary: Adobe is increasingly integrating artificial intelligence, powered by its Sensei platform, into its Creative Cloud products to improve image and video editing. While the core AI technologies are not new, Adobe's strategy focuses on seamless integration, leveraging its large user base and cloud infrastructure to deliver these features effectively and cost-efficiently. This approach allows Adobe to gather data, continuously improve its AI models, and strengthen its market position, demonstrating how combining advanced AI with robust product delivery can lead to profitable business applications.

title: What Does Facebook, Inc.’s Name Change Really Mean?
summary: Facebook, Inc. rebranded to Meta amidst speculation and efforts to pivot to the metaverse, following a trend of companies changing names to reflect expansion or reposition brands. While some view such changes as primarily semantic, branding experts suggest they can make sense from a brand architecture standpoint and signal broader strategic shifts. The article highlights how similar rebrands by companies like Michael Kors (Capri Holdings) and Coach (Tapestry) were accompanied by significant internal changes and strategic pushes upmarket, suggesting Meta's success will depend on similar substantive transformations.

title: How AI is driving powerful new Photoshop features — and shaping Adobe’s product strategy
summary: Adobe's Creative Cloud suite, particularly Photoshop, is increasingly integrating AI-powered features like auto-masking and Neural Filters, which leverage deep learning to enhance image and video editing. While the core AI technologies are not groundbreaking, Adobe's strategic advantage lies in its ability to seamlessly deliver these tools to its vast user base through its existing platform and cloud infrastructure. This approach allows Adobe to overcome common challenges in AI product delivery, ensuring continuous improvement and strengthening its market position.

title: Adobe Advertising Cloud named a Leader in Gartner’s 2021 Magic Quadrant for Ad Tech|Adobe
summary: Adobe Advertising Cloud has been named a Leader in Gartner's 2021 Magic Quadrant for Ad Tech for the fourth consecutive year. This recognition highlights Adobe's expertise in integrations, audience segmentation based on first-party data, and strong omnichannel advertising inventory. The company credits this achievement to its focus on delivering value and helping brands navigate the evolving digital and privacy landscape.

title: Industry Report: Technology’s primary role in 2022 is to increase revenue
summary: A report by WTM London and Travel Forward indicates that industry professionals view technology's main role in 2022 as increasing revenue, with cost reduction being the third most important factor. The research, based on a survey of nearly 700 senior executives, also highlights a preference for technology to help find new clients and source markets over retaining existing customers. This suggests an underlying optimism in the industry for market growth and an expanded customer base in the coming year.

title: Sundance Institute Announces the 2021 Women at Sundance | Adobe Fellows
summary: The Sundance Institute announced the eight artists selected for its 2021 Women at Sundance | Adobe Fellowship. This yearlong program supports women artists in film and media, especially those from underrepresented communities, with mentorship, workshops, grants, and industry connections. The fellowship aims to foster community, develop craft, and advance the careers of these storytellers.

title: Puget Systems updates recommendations for After Effects by Jose Antunes - ProVideo Coalition
summary: Puget Systems has released updated hardware recommendations for Adobe After Effects users, following Adobe's introduction of the Multi-Frame Rendering feature. Their detailed report, based on extensive benchmark testing, analyzes optimal CPU, GPU, and RAM configurations for various user types to maximize performance benefits. Multi-Frame Rendering significantly improves rendering and previewing speeds, potentially offering a 2x to 4x performance increase depending on the hardware.

title: airSlate Launches Workflow Packages to Support Higher Education
summary: airSlate has introduced new workflow packages specifically designed for higher education institutions to help them digitally transform their document management processes. These solutions aim to make academic organizations more efficient and productive by automating workflows for various departments, such as student applications, financial aid, and course scheduling. The company emphasizes its compliance with standards like FERPA, SOC 2 Type II, and GDPR, noting the increased urgency for digital transformation following the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Stratasys 3D Printing Roundup: Open Software Platform, Adobe Collaboration, & Prince
