summary: Melissa Waters, CMO of Upwork, discusses her company's approach to AI, emphasizing a human-centered policy that prioritizes safety and responsible data handling. She details how Upwork's marketing teams are using AI tools like ChatGPT and Regie.ai to boost productivity, customize content, and expand their online presence. Waters also highlights the potential of AI for hyper-personalization in marketing and advises brands to encourage experimentation among employees while addressing concerns about job redundancy.

title: Adobe: Is OpenAI's Sora Changing The Game? (NASDAQ:ADBE)
summary: OpenAI's launch of its new text-to-video model, Sora, caused Adobe Inc.'s stock to drop due to concerns about disruption in the creative industry. This article will discuss both the potential dangers and benefits Sora poses for Adobe. The author maintains a long position in ADBE shares.

title: Top 25 Cloud Computing Companies of 2024
summary: The Software Report has released its list of the Top 25 Cloud Computing Companies of 2024, highlighting the industry's rapid growth and innovation. These companies, ranging from security and compliance solutions to managed services and IaaS, were selected based on market impact, revenue growth, product reliability, strategic vision, and sustainability efforts. Noteworthy awardees include Visma, Qualys, Cloudflare, Samsara, and Mirantis, each contributing significantly to the evolving cloud landscape.

title: Transforming healthcare marketing technology with Chris Vitti of The Cigna Group
summary: Chris Vitti, Senior Director and Head of Marketing Technology at The Cigna Group, discusses how technology can bridge gaps in the healthcare customer journey, personalizing experiences and improving patient outcomes. He emphasizes the importance of modern marketing technology, embracing AI, and continuous learning for those aspiring to a career in the field. Vitti also highlights Cigna's mission and the impact of technology in transforming the healthcare industry.

title: 5 Best Mind Mapping Software & Tools (Free & Paid)
summary: This article reviews the five best mind mapping software and tools for project management, including options for free and paid users. It highlights FigJam for visual workflows, Miro for collaboration, Lucidchart for complex projects, Canva for custom graphics, and WorkCanvas by monday.com for integrations. The review provides detailed information on features, pricing, pros, and cons for each tool, helping businesses choose the best fit for their project visualization and collaboration needs.

title: Omnicom’s AI-driven framework powers brand experiences at scale
summary: Omnicom has developed an AI-driven framework to enhance brand experiences at scale, leveraging Adobe's enterprise technologies to streamline the content supply chain. This framework covers everything from ideation and creative processes to omnichannel deployment and performance measurement, aiming to deliver personalized content that fosters strong customer connections. The approach emphasizes efficiency, precision, and ethical considerations for AI-generated content to meet consumer demand for tailored experiences.

title: Osprey Bitcoin Trust seeks merger or sale with existing bitcoin ETF
summary: The Osprey Bitcoin Trust (OBTC) is exploring strategic options, including a sale or merger with an existing bitcoin ETF, due to its shares trading at a discount to Bitcoin's value. If these talks are unsuccessful, the Trust intends to liquidate within 180 days. This follows Osprey's past attempt to take over Grayscale's bitcoin trust and a lawsuit against Grayscale for alleged deceptive practices.

title: IBM says use of Adobe AI tools in marketing boosted productivity
summary: IBM has reported significant productivity gains from its initial testing of Adobe's generative AI tools in marketing. The use of these AI tools, which can generate images from text, has reduced the time for end-to-end marketing cycles from two weeks to two days, with IBM expecting a 10-fold increase in designer productivity. While short-term impacts suggest existing teams will handle more work, the long-term effect on employment is less clear, even as business leaders view designers as increasingly important.

title: IBM deploying Adobe Firefly generative AI tool for marketing, design teams
summary: IBM has expanded the internal deployment of Adobe Firefly, a generative AI tool, across its marketing and design teams to boost productivity. The company's design teams have been experimenting with Firefly since its beta launch last year and are now using it for ideation, content creation at scale, and assisting external clients with AI adoption and brand consistency. This expansion signifies the "next evolution" of IBM's partnership with Adobe, aiming to achieve personalization at scale in marketing.

title: How Stitch Fix Is Using Generative AI To Help Us Dress Better
summary: Stitch Fix is leveraging generative AI to enhance its personalized styling service by interpreting vast amounts of customer feedback and streamlining internal processes. The company uses AI to create clothing recommendations for human stylists, generate ad copy and product descriptions, and automatically style outfits, demonstrating a balance between automation and human expertise. This approach allows AI to handle repetitive tasks, freeing up human stylists for more creative and relationship-building activities.

title: EPAM reimagines sales with Dynamics 365 and Microsoft Copilot for Sales
summary: EPAM (NYSE: EPAM) has integrated Dynamics 365 and Microsoft Copilot for Sales to centralize data and enhance business enablement for its global sales and marketing teams. This initiative aims to improve productivity, accelerate time-to-market, and optimize business processes through cloud and AI-enabled transformation. The move supports EPAM's goal to achieve data-driven sales management and grow from $5 billion to $10 billion in revenue.

title: CoinShares closes buy of US bitcoin ETF issuer’s fund division
summary: CoinShares, a Europe-focused investment firm, has completed its acquisition of Valkyrie's fund business, including its actively managed ETF division. This move is part of CoinShares' strategy to expand its asset management presence in the US, taking over ETFs with approximately $530 million in combined assets, including the Valkyrie Bitcoin Fund (BRRR). CoinShares plans to rebrand these funds by the summer, broadening its product offerings and significantly increasing its total addressable market in the US.

title: LaCie Introduces New Rugged Mini SSD with Up to 4TB Capacity
summary: LaCie, a brand of Seagate, has introduced the new Rugged Mini SSD, offering up to 4TB of durable, fast, and compact storage for content creators. This portable drive boasts read speeds of up to 2000MB/s with USB 3.2 gen 2x2 technology and features an IP54-rated, 3-meter drop-protected, and 1-ton car pressure-resistant design using recycled materials. It is compatible with a wide range of devices including Apple products and PCs, and comes with a three-year warranty, data recovery services, and a one-month Adobe Creative Cloud All Apps plan.

title: Adobe shares fall as downbeat forecast fans worries about competition, AI efforts
summary: Adobe's shares fell by 12% after a disappointing quarterly forecast raised concerns about increasing competition for its creative software and the company's AI efforts. Analysts noted that AI has eroded Adobe's competitive advantages as startups like Stability AI and Midjourney challenge its market dominance. Despite a $25 billion stock buyback announcement, the company's revenue forecast was below estimates, causing a significant drop in market value.

title: After Earnings, Is Adobe Stock a Buy, Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar maintains its fair value estimate of $610 per share for Adobe (ADBE) after its fiscal first-quarter earnings, despite what they describe as "perplexing" and slightly disappointing guidance for the second quarter. While the stock experienced a post-earnings selloff due to convoluted guidance, Morningstar views it as a temporary dislocation, considering factors like recent pricing actions, upcoming product launches, and strong generative AI adoption which make shares attractive. The company's wide economic moat, driven by switching costs and network effects in its Digital Media segment, continues to support its long-term outlook.

title: Adobe Brings Firefly Generative AI Into Substance 3D Workflows
summary: Adobe has integrated its Firefly generative AI capabilities into Substance 3D Sampler and Stager, aiming to enhance productivity for industrial designers, game developers, and VFX professionals. New features include Text to Texture in Sampler, which creates 3D object textures from text prompts, and Generative Background in Stager, which generates background images and composites objects with intelligent lighting. These advancements are designed to accelerate creative workflows and iterative processes, with Content Credentials automatically attached to Firefly-created assets for transparency.

title: Adobe only trained its AI tool, Firefly, on licensed data. Here’s why that’s good for business
summary: Adobe's AI-powered image creation tool, Firefly, was trained exclusively on licensed data, distinguishing it from many competitors and mitigating legal risks. This strategic decision was driven by the needs of their creative professional customer base, who prioritize content provenance and usage rights. Despite the data limitations, Firefly offers robust features, has garnered significant user adoption, and contributed to a pivotal year for Adobe, with over 5 billion images created and $15.17 billion in digital media annualized recurring revenue.

title: Dropbox Sign (Hellosign) vs DocuSign: Pricing, Features, Safety
summary: This article provides a comprehensive comparison between Dropbox Sign (formerly HelloSign) and DocuSign, two leading e-signature solutions, focusing on their pricing, features, and safety in 2024. It highlights DocuSign's greater customization and functionality versus Dropbox Sign's unlimited transactions, while also pointing out their respective limitations and suggesting PandaDoc as a potential alternative for more robust document creation and integration needs. The comparison also delves into user limits, transaction limits, and overall e-signature workflows for both platforms.

title: Accenture and Adobe to Co-Develop Industry-Specific Generative AI Solutions to Accelerate Marketing Transformation
summary: Accenture and Adobe are expanding their partnership to co-develop industry-specific generative AI solutions using Adobe Firefly. This collaboration aims to help organizations, particularly in retail, consumer goods, automotive, financial services, and health industries, create personalized content at scale and transform their content supply chains. Accenture will integrate Firefly Custom Models into its marketing services, and its engineers will specialize in Adobe Firefly to support clients in deploying generative AI campaigns.

title: Canva Acquires Affinity Design Suite in Push to Rival Adobe
summary: Canva Inc. has acquired the Affinity suite of creative software in its largest acquisition to date, aiming to intensify its competition with Adobe Inc. The deal, valued at several hundred million pounds in cash and stock, bolsters Canva's professional tool offerings and marks a significant expansion for the Australian startup.

title: Adobe and Databricks develop a partnership that enables brands to deliver AI-driven personalization at scale
summary: Adobe and Databricks have partnered to deliver AI-driven personalization to brands at scale. This collaboration integrates Adobe Experience Platform with Databricks, enabling seamless data and AI-driven insights for customer experiences. Key innovations include Federated Audience Composition, AI-driven personalization, secure data collaboration, and managed Databricks connectors, all designed to reduce data movement, enhance security, and accelerate time to value for personalization use cases.

title: Adobe’s new AI tool can turn anyone into an infographics wiz
summary: Adobe's new generative AI tool, Project Infograph It, aims to democratize infographic creation by allowing users to build customizable infographics from data sets and text descriptions. The tool, previewed at Adobe's Sneaks event, simplifies the process by requiring only raw data and a prompt, automatically generating various chart types and applying design elements. This innovation seeks to make data visualization accessible beyond specialized teams.

title: Adobe Just Demonstrated How AI Can Create Stellar Marketing Materials for Coca-Cola–or Any Company
summary: Adobe showcased its new GenStudio tool, designed to revolutionize marketing content creation using generative AI. At its recent summit, CEO Shantanu Narayen demonstrated how the tool, through an example with Coca-Cola, can rapidly produce on-brand marketing materials across multiple platforms and analyze their performance to optimize future campaigns. GenStudio is expected to be fully released later this year, promising to significantly speed up content production for marketers.

title: Adobe Hired Shaq to Explain How AI Marketing Works. Here Are the Highlights
summary: Adobe recently showcased several new generative AI tools for marketing at its annual summit, featuring Shaquille O’Neal as a demonstrator. These tools address challenges like campaign playbook generation, hyper-personalization, workflow automation, brand consistency, influencer marketing, and infographic creation. Shaq highlighted the user-friendliness and practical applications of these innovations in the marketing field.

title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Berkshire Hathaway, Procter & Gamble, Adobe, Advanced Micro Devices and AstraZeneca
summary: This Zacks Analyst Blog highlights research reports on major stocks including Berkshire Hathaway, Procter & Gamble, and Adobe, along with Advanced Micro Devices and AstraZeneca PLC. It provides an overview of each company's recent performance, key financial drivers, and potential headwinds. The article emphasizes Zacks Investment Research's stock-picking strategies and their strong historical performance.

title: CISA Highlights Critical Adobe Security Updates for Acrobat, Magento, and More (CVE-2024-20738, CVE-2024-20719, CVE-2024-20720)
summary: CISA has issued an alert regarding critical security updates from Adobe addressing multiple vulnerabilities across products like Adobe Commerce (Magento), Acrobat, Reader, and FrameMaker Publishing Server. Threat actors are already exploiting CVE-2024-20720 in Magento to deploy backdoors and payment skimmers. Organizations are urged to promptly apply updates and review their security posture to prevent exploitation.

title: Capital One’s Discover bid tops biggest Q1 tech-related deals
summary: Capital One's proposed $35.3 billion merger with Discover was the largest tech-related M&A deal globally in Q1, contributing to a significant jump in overall tech-related deal value to $183.7 billion. This increase reflects growing corporate confidence amid improving macroeconomic conditions, despite a decrease in the total number of deals. The article also discusses contributing factors like the drive for growth and AI advancements, as well as headwinds such as high interest rates, geopolitical uncertainty, and increased antitrust scrutiny.

title: Adobe Introduces Next Generation of Frame.io to Accelerate Content Workflow and Collaboration for Every Creative Project
summary: Adobe has launched Frame.io V4, a flexible, fast, and intuitive creative collaboration platform designed to streamline and simplify workflows across content creation and production. This new version aims to meet the escalating demand for video content by centralizing feedback, reducing revisions, and accelerating media asset delivery for creative teams. Frame.io V4 introduces advanced workflow management capabilities, including a dynamic metadata framework and smart folder system called Collections, and extends support beyond video post-production to various content types and creative disciplines.

title: Following Departure of its Founder, E-Discovery Company DISCO Names New CEO
summary: E-discovery company CS Disco has named Eric Friedrichsen as its new President and CEO, filling the vacancy left by co-founder Kiwi Camara. Friedrichsen, previously CEO of Emburse, LLC, will start on April 29, with interim CEO Scott Hill transitioning to board chair. This appointment comes after controversies surrounding Camara's departure and his compensation in 2022.

title: Adobe leverages Microsoft Azure to streamline the customer experience, harnessing the power of connected cloud services.
summary: The partnership between Adobe and Microsoft integrates Adobe’s enterprise solutions with Microsoft’s cloud services, aiming to improve customer experiences and drive AI transformation. This collaboration focuses on streamlining cloud infrastructure, enhancing data management, and accelerating business outcomes through seamless integrations and innovative AI capabilities. The alliance has shown significant economic impact, including a 251% ROI over three years and improved productivity for IT, security, and marketing teams.

title: Adobe Is Buying Videos for $3 Per Minute to Build AI Model
summary: Adobe (ADBE) is actively procuring videos to develop its artificial intelligence text-to-video generator, aiming to compete with technologies like OpenAI's Sora. The company is offering artists $120 to submit videos depicting everyday actions and emotions for AI training purposes. This initiative highlights Adobe's strategy to enhance its AI capabilities in the face of competitive pressure.

title: Creator-Platform Company Spotter Hires Adobe and Microsoft Alum Ivo Manolov as CTO
summary: Spotter, a company providing capital and software to online creators, has appointed Ivo Manolov, an alum of Adobe Systems and Microsoft, as its new Chief Technology Officer. Manolov will lead the development of AI-powered software for YouTubers. This strategic hire underscores Spotter's commitment to enhancing its technology and AI initiatives for creators.

title: Adobe plans generative AI video editing tools for Premiere Pro
summary: Adobe Inc. is introducing three new generative AI tools for its Premiere Pro video editing software later this year, leveraging its Firefly family of models. These tools include generative extend for lengthening clips, object addition and removal within video, and text-to-video generation, aiming to enhance creative workflows and address common editing challenges. Adobe also plans to integrate third-party AI models like OpenAI’s Sora and Runway into Premiere Pro to offer creators more diverse options.

title: Adobe Transforms Digital Document Experiences this Tax Season with General Availability of Acrobat AI Assistant
summary: Adobe has officially launched its AI Assistant for Acrobat and Reader, making generative AI capabilities available to billions of users on desktop, web, and now mobile. This tool, available through an add-on subscription, summarizes documents, answers questions with intelligent citations, and helps generate content. The release aims to enhance productivity across various document types, from tax forms to research papers, with an emphasis on data security and user trust.

title: Adobe’s PDF-Reading AI Assistant Is Here, But It’ll Cost You
summary: Adobe has launched Acrobat AI Assistant, a generative AI tool designed to summarize long documents and answer questions through a chatbot interface. Aimed at helping users engage with documents more efficiently, this assistant was initially released as a free beta in February and is now available for $5 per month. It allows users to quickly extract information, define action items, and find key details within various types of documents, citing sources directly.

title: Adobe Explores OpenAI Partnership as It Adds AI Video Tools
summary: Adobe is exploring partnerships with third-party generative AI tools like OpenAI's Sora for its Premiere Pro video editing software, while also integrating its own Firefly AI model. This move aims to address concerns about AI's impact on its core business and offers users more options for AI-generated video content. Adobe also emphasized its commitment to AI ethics and transparency regarding content created with third-party models.

title: CommBank, Telstra, and Canva: LinkedIn reveals top workplaces for growth
summary: LinkedIn has released its 8th annual Top Companies List for Australia, identifying the leading employers for career growth. Commonwealth Bank, NAB, and Telstra secured the top three spots, with Canva making its debut on the list. The ranking emphasizes the importance for companies to invest in employee development and skills enhancement, particularly given the rapid changes expected in job requirements due to technology like AI.

title: CoStar revenue up 12%, but Homes.com advertising eats into profits
summary: CoStar reported a 12% increase in revenue for Q1 but saw net profits significantly decline due to heavy advertising spending for Homes.com. Despite the profit dip, CEO Andy Florance highlighted the success of Homes.com's agent subscription program and record traffic, asserting the platform's goal to become the top U.S. real estate portal. The company also announced the acquisition of Matterport to enhance its property data and digital listing experiences.

title: Adobe’s new Photoshop turns AI into a practical superpower
summary: Adobe has launched its new Photoshop beta, integrating the updated Firefly 3 AI model, which significantly improves photorealistic output and introduces new features for both amateur and professional users. Key functionalities include Generative Fill with Reference Image, Generate Image for text-to-image creation, Generative Background, Generate Similar, and Enhanced Detail, all designed to streamline image editing and creativity. The article highlights that these advancements address previous criticisms of Adobe's generative AI and are already driving a substantial increase in Photoshop subscriptions.

title: New Adobe Photoshop with Advanced Generative Fill and Generate Image Brings New Superpowers to All
summary: Adobe has unveiled a new beta version of Photoshop featuring significant advancements in Generative Fill, including the new Reference Image and Generate Image capabilities, powered by the Firefly Image 3 Foundation Model. These updates aim to enhance creative workflows, offering users greater control and efficiency in bringing their visions to life. Adobe also emphasizes its commitment to responsible AI innovation through Content Credentials and IP indemnification for Firefly-generated content.

title: Adobe Introduces Firefly Image 3 Foundation Model to Take Creative Exploration and Ideation to New Heights
summary: Adobe has launched Firefly Image 3 Foundation Model, its latest generative AI model, now available in beta for Photoshop and the Firefly web application. This new model promises significant improvements in photorealistic quality, prompt understanding, detail, variety, and speed for creative ideation. It also introduces features like Structure Reference and Style Reference, enhanced rendering of people and complex scenes, and broader styles for illustrations and icons, all while ensuring responsible AI development and transparency with Content Credentials.

title: The Coca-Cola Company announces 5-year AI partnership with Microsoft
summary: The Coca-Cola Company has announced a five-year AI partnership with Microsoft, committing $1.1 billion to Microsoft Cloud and its generative AI capabilities. This collaboration aims to integrate AI across Coca-Cola's core systems to enhance efficiency, scalability, customer experiences, marketing, and supply chain operations. The partnership builds on a previous agreement and will see Coca-Cola leveraging solutions like Azure OpenAI Service and Copilot to drive digital transformation.

title: Adobe to bring full AI image generation to Photoshop this year
summary: Adobe plans to integrate full artificial intelligence image generation into its Photoshop software later this year, using its new Firefly Image 3 system. This move is a response to increasing competition from other AI image generation services and aims to enhance the workflow of creative professionals. The new tool will allow users to generate images from text prompts and even use uploaded images as a reference for composition, speeding up the design process.

title: Adobe Collaborates with Ethical Hackers to Build Safer, More Secure AI Tools
summary: Adobe is expanding its bug bounty program to include security researchers for Content Credentials and Adobe Firefly, aiming to build safer and more secure AI tools. This initiative encourages ethical hackers to identify vulnerabilities, particularly those related to large language models (LLMs) and the potential misuse of Content Credentials, fostering transparency and trust in AI development. Adobe emphasizes collaboration with the security community and industry experts to address AI risks and enhance product security.

title: 7 agency reviews to know about this month
summary: This article highlights several significant agency reviews happening this month, involving major companies like McDonald's, BlackRock, Adobe, and Goodyear. It outlines that these companies are reportedly reviewing various parts or all of their advertising accounts. The piece serves as a quick overview of key marketing account movements in the industry.

title: Adobe's Acrobat AI Assistant Is Available and Could Help Company Compete
summary: Adobe has launched its Acrobat AI Assistant, available to individual and enterprise users via a paid add-on subscription. This move positions Adobe to better compete with other big tech companies like Microsoft and Google in the artificial intelligence market. The AI Assistant aims to enhance document productivity and generate insights, contributing to Adobe's AI monetization strategy.

title: Netflix’s head of design answers every question you ever had about Netflix
summary: In a rare and extended Q&A, Netflix's VP of Design, Steve Johnson, discusses the company's design philosophy, its approach to gaming, the Vision Pro, and generative AI. Despite Netflix's 260 million subscribers and its leading position in the streaming industry, Johnson explains the ongoing challenge of helping users discover what they want to watch. He also shares insights into how the streaming giant designs its system and navigates the crowded entertainment landscape.

title: Adobe Global Chief Cybersecurity Legal And Privacy Officer Nubiaa Shabaka On Trust, Data Security And AI
summary: Nubiaa Shabaka, Adobe's Global Chief Cybersecurity Legal and Privacy Officer, discusses the integrated approach Adobe takes to privacy and security, highlighting their interdependence, especially in the era of AI. She explains how Adobe utilizes AI to enhance privacy-centric tools and security measures against increasingly sophisticated cyber threats. Shabaka also emphasizes Adobe's proactive stance on AI governance and its advocacy for consistent global standards in privacy and security regulations.

title: Design Startup Figma Valued at $12.5 Billion in Secondary Share Sale
summary: Design startup Figma Inc. is conducting a secondary share sale valuing the company at $12.5 billion, following the collapse of its proposed acquisition by Adobe Inc. in December. The sale, expected to be up to $900 million, will allow current and former employees and other equity holders to sell shares. This move aims to leverage strong business momentum and investor interest in Figma's product development vision.

title: Figma Sets $12.5B Valuation in New Equity Offering, Falling Short of Adobe's Acquisition Offer
summary: Figma has announced a tender offer for investors to sell shares at a $12.5 billion valuation, a 25% increase from its 2021 valuation but significantly less than Adobe's terminated $20 billion acquisition offer. The tender offer, expected to range from $600 million to $900 million, is backed by existing and new investors. This development follows the dissolution of the Adobe-Figma acquisition due to regulatory hurdles, highlighting Figma's independent growth despite the failed merger.

title: Adobe Unveils Firefly-Powered Generative Remove in Lightroom for Fast and Easy AI-Editing Across Surfaces
summary: Adobe has unveiled "Generative Remove" in Lightroom, powered by Adobe Firefly, allowing users to easily remove unwanted objects from photos across mobile, web, and desktop. Alongside this, Lightroom introduces Lens Blur with new presets and other AI-powered tools, streamlining photo editing for all skill levels. Adobe also emphasizes its commitment to responsible AI innovation, integrating Content Credentials with Generative Remove to ensure transparency and authenticity.

title: New Adobe Express for Enterprise Makes It Easy to Accelerate On-Brand Content Creation and Production Across Teams
summary: Adobe announced the new Adobe Express for Enterprise, powered by Firefly Image Model 3, to accelerate on-brand content creation for businesses. This platform provides brand controls, bulk creation, and AI capabilities designed for commercial safety, enabling marketing, sales, and communications teams to produce compelling visual content more efficiently. Leading companies like IBM, Dentsu, and Red Hat are already leveraging Adobe Express for Enterprise to streamline content workflows and enhance brand consistency across various channels.

title: Adobe Lightroom gets first Firefly feature — Generative Remove
summary: Adobe has introduced its Firefly Generative Remove feature to Lightroom, marking the first time a Firefly AI capability has been integrated into the photo-editing app. Similar to Photoshop's Generative Fill, this tool allows users to non-destructively remove unwanted elements from photographs. Alongside this, Adobe also made its AI-powered Lens Blur generally available and rolled out other updates, including a streamlined mobile editing experience and HDR display optimization.

title: Autodesk (ADSK) Acquires Wonder Dynamics for 3D Animation & VFX
summary: Autodesk (ADSK) has acquired Wonder Dynamics, creators of the AI-powered 3D animation solution Wonder Studio, to enhance its offerings in 3D animation and VFX. This acquisition will enable easier integration of 3D animated characters into projects and aligns with Autodesk's strategy to incorporate AI ethically. The move pits Autodesk against major players like Microsoft, Adobe, and Meta Platforms in the rapidly growing generative AI market.

title: Randstad Selects Accenture Song to Deliver Generative AI-Powered Creative and Content at Scale
summary: Randstad has appointed Accenture Song as its global creative and content agency of record to accelerate its marketing transformation. Accenture Song will help Randstad leverage generative AI, specifically Adobe Firefly and other tools, to create compelling and on-brand content for its marketing communications. This partnership aims to enhance Randstad's brand relevancy, drive scale and consistency, and connect talent with employers in the evolving world of work.

title: Shutterstock Studios Sweeps Awards Season with 15 Telly Awards a
summary: Shutterstock Studios, a division of Shutterstock, Inc. (NYSE: SSTK), has been recognized with significant industry awards, including 15 Telly Awards, four Webby Honors, and a Webby Nomination. These accolades highlight their innovative commercial storytelling, virtual production, and immersive content creation for diverse clients. The studio also secured five awards at Clube da Criatividade Portugal and is set to host an immersive showcase and participate in Cannes Lions to further demonstrate its technological advancements.

title: 16 Best AI Stocks to Buy in 2025: What Are the Best AI Companies to Invest in 2025?
summary: This article identifies 16 promising AI stocks to consider for investment in 2025, highlighting their innovative approaches, financial performance, and growth potential. It covers how companies utilize AI across various sectors and provides guidance on different investment methods, including direct stock purchases, AI-focused ETFs, venture capital funds, and crowdfunding platforms. The article also discusses key considerations for investing in AI stocks and explores the debate around a potential AI bubble.

title: NVIDIA Brings AI Assistants to Life With GeForce RTX AI PCs
summary: NVIDIA announced new RTX technology to power AI assistants and digital humans on new GeForce RTX AI laptops, including "Project G-Assist" for context-aware help in games and apps. They also introduced PC-based NVIDIA NIM inference microservices for the NVIDIA ACE digital human platform, enabled by the new NVIDIA RTX AI Toolkit. These innovations aim to accelerate over 500 PC applications and games, with new RTX AI PC laptops from ASUS and MSI featuring up to GeForce RTX 4070 GPUs.

title: Ansel Adams’s Estate Rebukes Adobe for Selling A.I. Images in the Style of the Late Photographer
summary: The Ansel Adams estate publicly criticized Adobe for selling AI-generated images on its stock photo service that mimicked Adams's distinctive style. Despite Adobe's guidelines prohibiting the use of artists' names and "in the style of" prompts for AI submissions, such content appeared on its platform. Adobe has since removed the offending listing and communicated with the estate, stating that the contributor violated its terms and conditions.

title: Which AI Stocks Are Turning Hype Into Revenue?
summary: This article identifies AI stocks that have successfully converted hype into tangible revenue and growth during the first fiscal quarter. It highlights how companies like Nvidia, Microsoft, Amazon, Arista Networks, Broadcom, and Marvell Technology have seen increased fair value estimates due to strong demand for their AI-related products and services. While many AI-related valuations are considered high, the author suggests investment opportunities in Nvidia, certain auto industry stocks, and undervalued software and cybersecurity companies such as Adobe, Fortinet, and Palo Alto Networks.

title: Shutterstock Made $104 Million Licensing Assets to AI Devs Last Year
summary: Shutterstock earned $104 million in revenue last year by licensing its extensive library of photos, videos, and audio assets to AI companies for training models. This marks a significant and growing part of the company's business, with a new multi-year partnership recently announced with AI firm Reka. Shutterstock anticipates this revenue stream to reach $138 million for the entire business unit in 2024 and $250 million annually by 2027, as legitimate data sourcing becomes crucial for generative AI developers.

title: People Aren't Happy With Adobe's Spyware-Like Terms of Service Update
summary: Adobe users are expressing significant discontent over recent changes to the company's Terms of Use, which now mandate unlimited access to user projects for "content review" and other purposes, including potential AI model training. The updated terms grant Adobe a broad royalty-free license to user creations and make it difficult to remove software without agreeing to them. Despite backlash, Adobe has not yet responded or revised the terms, leading to speculation of a "wait-it-out" strategy, reminiscent of a similar controversy faced by Microsoft.

title: Adobe Experience Platform gets AI assistant for customer data insights
summary: Adobe has launched a generative AI assistant for its Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) apps, designed to simplify access to customer experience data for a wider range of employees. The AI assistant offers functionalities including product expertise, usage insights without SQL queries, and content creation for marketing campaigns. It also provides predictive insights and recommendations based on historical customer data, powered by a generative experience model combining base models, custom AEP customer data, and selectable LLMs.

title: Ouch! Adobe Just Got Dragged on Social Media Over a Boring Terms of Service Update
summary: Adobe faced significant backlash on social media due to a terms of service update that caused user panic over AI overreach and data privacy. The update, which clarified Adobe's access to user content for service improvement and moderation, led to concerns that the company would use private projects to train AI models. Adobe maintained it does not use customer content for AI training and does not assume ownership of user work, highlighting a growing tension between companies' AI ambitions and user data skepticism.

title: How Does Monday.com Make Money? Monday.com Business Model In A Nutshell
summary: Monday.com is a cloud-based work management platform founded in 2012 by Roy Mann, Eran Kampf, and Eran Zinman to help organizations scale efficiently. The company generates revenue by offering various subscription plans for its platform, catering to teams from small to enterprise-level based on the number of users and desired features. Its business model relies on a freemium approach with tiered paid plans that include features like Gantt charts, time-tracking, and advanced analytics, appealing to a wide range of industries and clients such as Costco and Uber.

title: Adobe Responds to AI Fears With Plans for Updated Legal Terms
summary: Adobe Systems Inc. faced backlash from customers over updated terms of use, prompting a reevaluation of how the company communicates legal issues, especially concerning generative AI. General counsel Dana Rao emphasized that legal terms must evolve with technology. This incident highlights heightened scrutiny on terms of service as technology advances.

title: 5 Ultracheap Stocks to Buy With the Best Returns on Investment
summary: This article identifies five undervalued stocks with strong returns on investment, based on Morningstar's criteria. These companies—Trinet Group, Autodesk, Paycom Software, Adobe, and MarketAxess—have outperformed the Morningstar US Market Index over 10 years, boast average three-year fiscal ROICs exceeding 20%, hold wide or narrow economic moats, and trade at least 20% below Morningstar's fair value estimates. The article provides detailed analysis for each stock, highlighting their competitive advantages and growth prospects.

title: Adobe to adapt terms of service on genAI training after customer backlash
summary: Adobe plans to modify its terms of service regarding generative AI training after customer complaints. Users expressed concern that Adobe's updated terms would allow the company to use their data to train AI models, prompting Adobe to clarify its stance on content ownership and usage. The company has stated it does not train its Firefly genAI model on customer content and will not assume ownership of customer work, with further clarifications expected by June 18.

title: Adobe could be facing an internal revolt amid concerns over its terms of service
summary: Adobe is facing internal criticism from employees regarding its updated terms of service and handling of customer concerns over AI training. This comes after customers already expressed anger about the potential use of personal data for AI, with employees now echoing similar sentiments about the company's communication and public perception. The article highlights the ongoing challenges for Adobe in managing its public image and addressing user and internal staff worries about its AI policies and market dominance.

title: Adobe accused by US government of 'trapping subscribers'
summary: The US government has sued Adobe, alleging the software giant violates consumer protection laws through "hidden" termination fees and a complex cancellation process for its subscriptions. The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) claims Adobe fails to clearly disclose terms, including year-long commitments and early cancellation charges. Adobe disputes these claims, stating transparency in its terms and a simple cancellation process, and vows to fight the lawsuit.

title: ‘Evil’ | Adobe employees hit back at company leadership over user AI crisis
summary: Adobe employees have voiced their frustrations internally regarding the company's recent AI-related controversy and its handling of customer communication. Following a controversial update to Adobe's terms of service that sparked fears of user content being used to train AI models, employees criticized leadership for poor communication and suggested the company is gaining a reputation for being "evil." Despite Adobe promising to roll back the update and clarify its terms, many workers remain unconvinced about the company's preventative measures for similar future incidents.

title: U.S. Sues Adobe Over Hard-to-Cancel Subscriptions (Published 2024)
summary: The Justice Department has sued Adobe, alleging that the company made it difficult for consumers to cancel software subscriptions by hiding expensive cancellation fees in fine print. This lawsuit is part of a broader regulatory effort against "hard-to-cancel" subscriptions and comes after similar actions against other tech giants. The suit names two Adobe executives as defendants and follows an FTC investigation into Adobe's practices.

title: Adobe integrates more generative AI features into Acrobat
summary: Adobe has introduced new generative AI features for its Acrobat PDF editing application, allowing users to generate images and analyze large document collections across various file formats. The update significantly expands upon previous AI capabilities, enabling bulk document analysis, natural language queries with citations, automated meeting transcript summaries, and the creation of formatted content from extracted information. Additionally, new AI tools, powered by the Firefly Image 3 model, facilitate image generation, background removal, and object addition within PDFs.

title: Adobe sued by U.S. regulators for making it too difficult to cancel subscriptions
summary: U.S. regulators have sued Adobe Inc. and two executives, alleging they deceived consumers by obscuring termination fees for a popular subscription plan and making cancellation processes overly difficult. The Justice Department filed the complaint following a referral from the Federal Trade Commission, while Adobe stated it plans to defend itself in court. This legal action comes as Adobe's shares have already fallen 13% this year.

title: The government's lawsuit against Adobe shows deceptive design is good for business—until it's not
summary: The Justice Department has filed a lawsuit against Adobe, alleging that its sign-up process employs deceptive design to lock consumers into hard-to-cancel "annual, paid monthly" subscriptions. The suit claims Adobe hides cancellation terms and imposes hefty early-termination fees, calling out these practices as "dark patterns" that prioritize profit over transparency. This action aligns with a broader government push, including by the FTC, to crack down on manipulative online design practices that trick users.

title: Amazon (AMZN) Strengthens Blink Portfolio With Blink Moments
summary: Amazon recently launched Blink Moments for its Blink Subscription Plus Plan members, enhancing its smart home security offerings by automatically condensing clips from multiple Blink cameras into a single, streamlined video. This initiative, alongside previous launches like the Blink Mini 2 and Blink Outdoor 4 cameras, positions Amazon to capitalize on the growing smart home security market, which is projected to reach $61.97 billion by 2029. The company's strengthened Blink portfolio gives it a competitive edge against rivals like Honeywell, Adobe, and Alphabet in this expanding sector.

title: Adobe Says It Won’t Train AI Using Artists’ Work. Creatives Aren’t Convinced
summary: Adobe has clarified its terms of service, pledging not to train its generative AI, Firefly, on user content after a significant backlash from artists. Despite Adobe's assurances that it won't use user work for AI training and its commitment to "ethically trained" models, many creatives remain skeptical due to past incidents and the company's market dominance. The controversy highlights broader concerns within the artistic community about intellectual property, consent, and the potential impact of generative AI on livelihoods.

title: Game Development Software Market size is set to grow by USD 62.8 million from 2024-2028, growth in demand for pc games and gaming consoles to boost the market growth, Technavio
summary: The global game development software market is projected to grow by USD 62.8 million between 2024 and 2028, driven primarily by the increasing demand for PC games and gaming consoles. This growth is further fueled by the rising popularity and integration of AR/VR games. However, the market faces challenges from the growing availability and adoption of open-source game development software.

summary: Microsoft's Build 2024 event brought significant updates to Azure App Service, focusing on enhancing web development and AI capabilities. Key announcements include automatic scaling for web applications, sidecar support for easier app modification, and tighter integration with Visual Studio for streamlined development and deployment. Furthermore, Microsoft announced strategic partnerships with Nvidia and AMD to bolster AI performance with new GPU offerings, and introduced Cobalt-based VMs for optimized performance and energy efficiency.

title: 3 Stocks to Buy Before This OpenAI Project Shocks Wall Street
summary: This article recommends three stocks poised to benefit from OpenAI's upcoming Sora text-to-video AI system: Microsoft (MSFT), Adobe (ADBE), and Western Digital (WDC). Microsoft is expected to gain significant leverage from its early investments in OpenAI, while Adobe's creative tools are well-positioned for integration with Sora. Western Digital is set to capitalize on the increased data storage demands that advanced AI models like Sora will generate.

title: Media Alert: Adobe Express for Education Fuels Strong Growth in Next-Gen Creativity and Job Skills Reaching Tens of Millions of Students and Teachers Globally
summary: Adobe announced significant growth in the adoption of Adobe Express for Education, reaching tens of millions of students and teachers globally across K-12 and higher education. This expansion is driven by new partnerships and the increasing number of Adobe Creative Campuses, highlighting the company's commitment to equipping students with next-gen creativity and job skills, including responsible generative AI. The platform, praised for its classroom-safe AI features, enables students to develop critical thinking and communication skills through various creative projects.

title: Global E-learning Market Size To Worth USD 1515.97 Billion By 2033 | CAGR of 17.35%
summary: The global E-learning Market is projected to grow from USD 305.97 Billion in 2023 to USD 1515.97 Billion by 2033, exhibiting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 17.35%. This growth is driven by increasing internet connectivity, technological advancements like AI and VR, and the expanding need for online learning solutions across academic, corporate, and government sectors. North America is expected to hold the largest market share, while Asia Pacific anticipates the fastest growth.

title: FTC Brings "Dark Patterns" Complaint Over Online Subscription and Cancellation Practices Against Company and Two Executives
summary: The FTC has filed a complaint against Adobe Inc. and two of its executives for alleged "dark patterns" in its online subscription and cancellation practices, claiming Adobe failed to clearly disclose material terms and made cancellations difficult. This action continues the FTC's trend of targeting both companies and responsible executives for deceptive subscription practices and indicates a sustained focus on individual accountability in consumer protection cases. The complaint seeks civil penalties, monetary relief, and permanent injunctions for all defendants.

title: Adobe CEO sells over $13.6 million in company stock
summary: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen sold over $13.6 million in company stock on June 27, 2024, through The Narayen Family Trust. These transactions, made public via an SEC filing, involved selling 26,000 shares at prices ranging from $543.04 to $545.8218. Following the sale, the trust still indirectly holds 379,660 Adobe shares.

title: Inside The Fall Of Oracle’s Advertising Business
summary: Oracle has announced the shutdown of its advertising business, Oracle Data Cloud (ODC), due to it no longer aligning with the company's strategic vision. This decision impacts acquired entities like Datalogix, BlueKai, Moat, and Grapeshot, and comes despite Oracle's past investments in building a robust ad tech ecosystem. The move is attributed to various factors including regulatory risks, diminishing third-party data marketplaces, and ODC's relatively small revenue contribution compared to Oracle's other ventures.

title: FTC Sues Adobe and Execs for Illegal “Hidden Fees”
summary: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has filed a lawsuit against Adobe, Inc. and two of its executives, alleging that the company deceived consumers through "hidden fees" associated with its "Annual, Paid Monthly" (APM) subscription plan. The complaint asserts that Adobe failed to clearly disclose a significant early termination fee for canceling APM plans, making it difficult for users to stop their subscriptions. The FTC claims these practices violate the Restore Online Shoppers’ Confidence Act (ROSCA) and is seeking a permanent injunction and civil penalties.

title: Adobe Gets E-Signature Patent Claims Erased as Ineligible at CAFC
summary: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit (CAFC) affirmed a district court's ruling that an e-signature patent owned by Esignature Software, LLC is ineligible under Section 101. The CAFC agreed that the patent claims were directed to the abstract idea of a longstanding business practice—applying signatures to documents—using generic computing technology, and that no inventive step was present. Esignature's arguments regarding abstractness and inventive aspects of dependent claims were dismissed or deemed forfeited.

title: Salesforce and other software stocks are expected to bounce back in a big way
summary: Despite a strong rally in the S&P 500 and its information-technology sector this year, some major software companies like Salesforce and Adobe have underperformed. Analysts, however, see significant upside potential for these stocks in the coming months. This presents an opportunity for investors seeking bargains within the tech sector, which has become increasingly concentrated among a few large companies.

title: Salesforce and other software stocks are expected to bounce back in a big way
summary: Despite a strong rally in the S&P 500 and its information technology sector this year, some software companies like Salesforce and Adobe have lagged behind. Analysts foresee a significant rebound for these underperforming software stocks, with Salesforce projected to have a 14% upside and Adobe a 5% upside over the next 12 months. This presents potential buying opportunities for investors looking for bargains in the tech sector.

title: Peloton accused of providing customer chat data to train AI
summary: Peloton is facing a class-action lawsuit in California, accused of using customer chat data to train AI without user permission. The lawsuit, filed by Consumer Advocates, alleges Peloton violated the California Invasion of Privacy Act (CIPA) by sharing chat content with AI-powered marketing firm Drift. The core issue is whether Peloton properly informed users about the data sharing and obtained consent, with the court allowing the case to proceed despite Peloton's attempts to dismiss it.

title: Adobe Unveils Powerful New Innovations in Illustrator and Photoshop Unlocking New Design Possibilities for Creative Pros
summary: Adobe has released significant updates for Illustrator and Photoshop, introducing new AI-powered features and workflow enhancements to boost productivity for creative professionals. Innovations like Generative Shape Fill in Illustrator and the Selection Brush Tool in Photoshop are designed to accelerate design processes, save time, and offer greater creative control, all while adhering to Adobe's "creator-friendly AI" ethics. These tools aim to streamline tasks from ideation to production, allowing designers to focus more on creative execution.

title: Adobe Illustrator’s new tool makes hi-fi product mock-ups as easy as drag and drop
summary: Adobe Illustrator has released a new beta Mockup tool that simplifies the creation of high-fidelity product mock-ups through a drag-and-drop interface. This update, along with others powered by Adobe's Firefly AI engine, aims to accelerate creative workflows for designers by automating complex tasks like placing logos on products and generating patterns. The new tool is expected to significantly reduce the time and effort previously required for such renderings.

title: Apple signs on to voluntary US scheme to manage AI risks, White House says
summary: Apple Inc. has joined President Joe Biden's voluntary commitments for artificial intelligence, becoming the 16th company to agree to manage AI risks. These commitments aim to ensure that AI's power is not used for destructive purposes. Other major firms like Google, Microsoft, Adobe, IBM, and Nvidia had previously signed on to the scheme.

title: NVIDIA Announces Generative AI Models and NIM Microservices for OpenUSD Language, Geometry, Physics and Materials
summary: NVIDIA has unveiled significant advancements to OpenUSD, introducing generative AI models and NIM microservices to expand its adoption across robotics, industrial design, and engineering. These new services aim to accelerate the development of highly accurate virtual worlds and digital twins by enabling AI models to generate OpenUSD language, Python code, apply materials to 3D objects, and understand 3D space and physics. Collaborations with companies like Foxconn, WPP, and Siemens are already leveraging these technologies to enhance manufacturing, marketing, and industrial simulation workflows.

title: Another Minneapolis software business acquired for its AI technology

Pluralytics' AI technology analyzes language to predict appeal to different audiences, securing clients like Great Clips and Best Buy. This acquisition marks the second Minneapolis AI company bought this month, reflecting a growing trend in AI mergers and acquisitions, particularly within the marketing sector.

title: Atlassian Announces Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2024 Results
summary: Atlassian announced its fourth quarter and fiscal year 2024 financial results, reporting strong revenue growth and significant customer expansion. The company highlighted transformative innovations like Rovo, its AI-powered teammate, and its FedRAMP "In Process" status, positioning itself for continued growth in FY25, including a search for a Chief Revenue Officer to accelerate enterprise sales. Both GAAP and non-GAAP financial figures were provided for quarterly and fiscal year performance, along with financial targets for Q1 and FY25.

title: Why Revolve Is Embracing Brick-and-Mortar
summary: Facing declining sales and tumbling shares, Revolve Group Inc. is opening its first permanent brick-and-mortar store in Aspen after a successful pop-up, signaling a strategic shift from its influencer-driven online model to meet growing consumer demand for in-person shopping. Despite significant stock price drops from its pandemic peak, the company remains financially strong and is exploring further physical expansion in luxury destinations, though analysts are mixed on the strategy.

title: PayPal Rolls Out Its Fastlane Checkout Service for Online Sales
summary: PayPal has officially made its Fastlane checkout service available to all U.S. merchants. This one-click guest checkout solution aims to boost e-commerce conversion rates by reducing friction and speeding up purchases. Initial pilots have shown significant improvements in conversion and checkout times for merchants using Fastlane.

title: Adobe Calls Attention to Massive Batch of Code Execution Flaws
summary: Adobe has released fixes for 72 security vulnerabilities across several products as part of its Patch Tuesday rollout. These critical flaws affect Windows and macOS users, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution, memory leaks, and denial-of-service attacks in widely used software like Acrobat, Illustrator, Photoshop, and Adobe Commerce. The company urges users to update their software immediately to mitigate these risks.

title: Best Buy Introduces AI-Powered Delivery Tracking, Signaling Shift in Retail Logistics
summary: Best Buy has launched an AI-powered delivery tracking system to provide minute-by-minute updates, addressing growing consumer demand for transparency and efficiency in online order deliveries. This initiative aims to reduce customer anxiety for large purchases and optimize delivery logistics through real-time data and predictive analytics. Experts believe this move highlights a crucial shift in retail logistics, with AI-driven tracking becoming a key competitive advantage for retailers.

title: Exclusive: Vimeo's new CEO lays out the company's AI strategy
summary: Vimeo's new CEO, Philip Moyer, reveals the company's strong focus on generative AI to compete in the video platform market. Vimeo aims to simplify professional creators' and corporate marketing departments' challenges with AI features for video storage, customization, and distribution, while also addressing opportunities in AI regulation adherence. Despite past layoffs, the company asserts a strong financial position to grow profits without further headcount reductions.

title: The metadata of marketing — what a marketing system of record with Adobe Workfront Planning can do for you
summary: Adobe has launched Workfront Planning, a new solution designed to help marketing teams consolidate data, streamline planning, and orchestrate campaigns more effectively. This platform creates a marketing system of record by providing a common metadata layer, allowing organizations to gain better visibility into operations and improve coordination across teams and with external partners. It enables marketers to define campaigns, connect execution workflows, and leverage AI to transform unstructured data, ultimately leading to faster execution, deeper insights, and improved delivery of marketing outcomes.

Many capabilities of this internal solution will be integrated into SharePoint Premium, making Microsoft Digital "Customer Zero" for external product development.

The tool, first launched in February 2023, is designed to help employees quickly access information and answer customer questions, enhancing customer engagement and service accuracy. This initiative aims to leverage AI to boost associate expertise and streamline in-store operations.

title: 3forge Lands Funding from Morgan Stanley

This funding will be used to accelerate its global go-to-market strategy and expand its development community. 3forge differentiates itself by handling complex data environments, making it a valuable tool for banks.

title: Adobe Inc (ADBE) DCF Valuation: Is The Stock Undervalued?
summary: This article presents a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation for Adobe Inc (ADBE) to determine if its stock is undervalued. The analysis includes forecasted free cash flows, terminal value, and enterprise value calculations.

title: Adobe to Launch Generative AI Video Creation Tool Later This Year
summary: Adobe announced plans to launch a generative AI-powered video creation and editing tool, the Adobe Firefly Video Model, in a limited beta release later this year. This tool will compete with existing AI video generators and is trained on licensed or public domain content from Adobe Stock. Additionally, Adobe is introducing Generative Extend in Premiere Pro, allowing users to extend existing video clips by two seconds.

title: Adobe Reports Record Revenue in Q3 Fiscal 2024
summary: Adobe announced record revenue for its third quarter of fiscal year 2024, which concluded on August 30, 2024. The company has released its financial results, indicating strong performance.

It emphasizes moving beyond generic content variations to deliver highly relevant and responsive experiences, utilizing GenAI to interpret buying signals, engage anonymous visitors, fuel omnichannel interactions, simulate complex journeys, and localize content. The author stresses the importance of careful customization and transparency in GenAI-powered interactions.

title: Adobe Posts Lackluster Growth With Investors Eager for AI Uplift
summary: Adobe Inc. shares experienced a significant drop after the company's revenue outlook disappointed investors who are keen to see its new artificial intelligence tools translate into substantial financial gains. Despite integrating AI features like Firefly into its creative software, investors are looking for concrete evidence that these innovations can generate revenue, especially as emerging startups pose a competitive threat. The company's revenue grew 11% in the reported quarter, and profit topped estimates, but the outlook failed to impress.

title: Adobe shares slump as weak earnings forecast sparks fears of delayed AI gains

Despite the weak forecast, the company expects to surpass its annual net new annual recurring revenue expectations, indicating healthy subscription sign-ups. Analysts suggest a lack of clear short-term catalysts for the stock unless Adobe can assure investors of stronger growth next year.

title: Textron eAviation President and CEO Kriya Shortt Named `Executive in Residence’ by the World’s Premier Aviation and Aerospace University

This initiative aims to prepare the next generation of leaders by exposing them to diverse career opportunities and experiences within the field.

While creators appreciate benefits like time savings and brainstorming support, they also express significant concerns about intellectual property exploitation, lack of transparency, and the need for stronger governmental regulation to protect their rights and work. The study highlights a strong demand for tools that ensure attribution and control over how their work is used by AI models.

summary: NICE Ltd. is presented as a market leader in contact center-as-a-service (CCaaS) and compliance technology, positioned for significant growth driven by AI and business process optimization. Adobe is also partnering with nonprofits like Girl Scouts and DECA to make the coursework widely available and train local program leaders.

summary: Adobe has announced significant updates to Photoshop and Illustrator, introducing new features meant to enhance speed, precision, and power for creative professionals. Photoshop gains new distraction removal technology, a Generative Workspace (beta), and improved 3D workflows, while Illustrator introduces Objects on Path, Enhanced Image Trace, and Generative Shape Fill (beta). These innovations, powered by Adobe Firefly models, aim to streamline creative processes from ideation to production and include strong commitments to responsible AI and content authenticity.

The platform also features expanded partnerships with major advertising platforms like Google, Meta, Microsoft Advertising, Snap, and TikTok, allowing for direct campaign activation and performance insights.

Despite Adobe's efforts to integrate AI tools like Firefly into its products, analysts suggest its "adopt-first, monetize-later" AI strategy is causing frustration due to limited returns on significant capital investment.

The company's revenue and profit forecasts for fiscal year 2025 fell short of analysts' expectations, further fueling fears of AI disruption.

title: Growth ETFs Shaken Up as Tech Takes on Value Blush

The rebalancing also shifts tech's share in the S&P Value Index significantly, with these companies now having their weightings divided between growth and value characteristics based on S&P's multifactor analysis.

title: How AI is being misused in creating deep fakes and 3 countries which top this list of viewers

A majority of these sites were created in 2024, exacerbating the spread of deepfake content, 98% of which was sexually explicit in 2023. Countries worldwide are now working to counter this misuse of AI and deepfake technology.

The series includes products like the SanDisk Creator Phone SSD with MagSafe compatibility, Creator microSD cards, SD UHS-II cards, Phone Drives, USB-C Flash Drives, Pro Portable SSDs, and a Desk Drive, all aimed at providing speed, capacity, durability, and portability for demanding creative workflows. title: Corporate tax departments take the first steps towards a tech-enabled future, new report shows
summary: A new report, the "2025 Corporate Tax Technology Report" by Thomson Reuters Institute and Tax Executives Institute, indicates that corporate tax departments are beginning to embrace technology integration despite a historical slowness to modernize. While widespread GenAI adoption is still limited, departments are increasing budgets and hiring specialists, showing a shift towards a tech-enabled future. The report highlights a positive outlook among tax professionals regarding technology's impact, anticipated growth in tech spend, and evolving job roles.

title: Publishing Market to Grow by USD 18.9 Million (2025-2029), Increase in Demand for Diversity in Content to Boost Revenue, Report on AI Impact on Market Trends - Technavio
summary: The global publishing market is projected to grow by USD 18.9 million between 2025 and 2029, driven by an increased demand for content diversity and a rise in self-published authors. Technavio's report highlights that while AI is redefining the market landscape, challenges include the declining market share of traditional publishing and issues like unauthorized distribution. The industry is adapting to digital transformation with new business models and technologies to enhance reader engagement and content delivery.

title: Adobe and California State University partner to equip students with AI literacy and creative skills
summary: Adobe has joined California State University's (CSU) AI Workforce Acceleration Board as a founding member, building on their existing partnership. This initiative aims to equip CSU students with essential AI literacy and creative skills to succeed in an AI-powered workforce, addressing the growing demand for these competencies across industries. Through access to Adobe Creative Cloud apps and AI tools like Adobe Express and Firefly, students will gain hands-on experience, develop critical digital skills, and prepare for future careers in an AI-driven economy, serving as a model for other educational institutions.

title: VanEck Says SOL Could Hit $520 Soon as SEC Opens Door To Solana ETF
summary: Investment firm VanEck projects that Solana (SOL) could reach US$520 per coin by the end of 2025, driven by a projected 43% growth in the smart contract platform market and its strong correlation with the US M2 money supply. This forecast comes as the SEC acknowledged a Spot Solana ETF filing for the first time, a notable development given SOL's past security labeling. Despite the SEC's acknowledgment not guaranteeing approval, it represents a positive step for Solana's market acceptance.

title: Microsoft and Adobe Patch Tuesday, February 2025 Security Update Review
summary: The February 2025 Patch Tuesday addresses 67 vulnerabilities from Microsoft, including three critical and four zero-day vulnerabilities, two of which are actively exploited. Adobe also released seven advisories for 45 vulnerabilities, with 23 critical. Key vulnerabilities include Elevation of Privilege flaws in Windows Storage and WinSock, and critical Remote Code Execution vulnerabilities in DHCP Client Service, Windows LDAP, and Microsoft Excel.

title: Adobe Expands Generative AI Offerings Delivering New Firefly App with Industry’s First Commercially Safe Video Model
summary: Adobe has launched a new Firefly application, expanding its generative AI offerings to include the industry's first commercially safe video model. The Firefly app provides a comprehensive platform for generating images, vectors, and now videos, with features like Generate Video (beta) powered by the new Firefly Video Model, multi-modal workflows, Scene to Image (beta), and audio/video translation. Adobe is introducing new Firefly Standard and Firefly Pro plans to give users access to these premium video and audio features, all while ensuring IP-friendly and commercially safe content generation.

title: Adobe to charge 50 Cents per AI video in new product launch
summary: Adobe has launched a new subscription product that will charge approximately 50 cents per AI-generated video, aiming to assure investors of its competitiveness against AI startups. These subscriptions, priced at $9.99 or $29.99 per month, allow users to generate five-second videos, alongside unlimited AI photos and illustrations. The company is differentiating its offering through integration with existing creative tools, enhanced control over AI output, and copyright protection, with existing subscribers receiving some free video generation.

title: Amphenol Corporation (APH) Surged due to Rapid Growth In Data Centre Construction
summary: Amphenol Corporation (NYSE: APH) experienced significant growth in 2024, attributed to the rapid expansion of data center construction, which accounts for approximately 20% of its sales. The company specializes in manufacturing electrical, electronic, and fiber optic connectors used across various industries. Despite its strong performance, Aoris Investment Management, while acknowledging Amphenol's potential, believes AI stocks offer higher and quicker returns for investors.

title: Adobe Inc. (ADBE) Launches Firefly Video Model to Compete in AI-Generated Video Market
summary: Adobe Inc. (ADBE) has launched its Firefly Video Model, an AI tool designed for generating video clips, entering a competitive market alongside offerings from OpenAI, Runway, and Meta Platforms. This move positions Adobe to capitalize on the growing AI video market, with economic benefits from generative AI technology expected to boost US labor productivity and GDP in the coming years. The article also touches on significant global investments in AI from Europe and China, highlighting international competition and cooperation in the sector.

title: Why NRG Switched to Adobe Marketing Cloud: The Power of Digital Transformation
summary: This article focuses on why NRG, a leading energy company, decided to switch to Adobe Marketing Cloud. It highlights the power of digital transformation and how this move aimed to enhance their marketing strategies and customer engagement.

title: Adobe Inc. (NASDAQ:ADBE) is a favorite amongst institutional investors who own 84%
summary: This article is a privacy consent page from Yahoo and does not contain any information about Adobe Inc. (NASDAQ:ADBE) or its institutional investors.
