title: Top 10 Tech Companies to Work for in San Jose in 2025
summary: San Jose is identified as a leading tech hub in 2025, boasting over 6,600 tech companies and an annual tech GDP of $275 billion, with competitive salaries ranging from $140,000 to $200,000. The article highlights top employers like Adobe, Cisco, and Zoom, emphasizing their innovation in AI, green technology, and cybersecurity, alongside strong company cultures. It details how these companies foster career growth, employee satisfaction, and significant industry impact, making San Jose an attractive destination for tech professionals.

title: Adobe Announces General Availability of Real-Time CDP Collaboration for Brands to Jointly Drive Ad Performance in a Privacy-First Landscape
summary: Adobe has announced the general availability of its Real-Time CDP Collaboration in the U.S., a new solution designed to help advertisers and publishers navigate the evolving privacy-first advertising landscape. This platform allows brands to securely collaborate on first-party data to identify high-value audiences and activate personalized ad campaigns without sharing directly identifiable customer data. Partners like Warner Bros. Discovery are already leveraging this tool to enhance ad performance and audience targeting.

title: Can Tech Save Small Ski Resorts From Extinction?
summary: Entabeni Systems, a small tech consultancy, is working to help independent ski resorts survive increasingly difficult economic and environmental conditions. Founded by Erik Mogensen, the company streamlines operations and introduces technology to make resorts more efficient and skier-friendly, collecting data on skier behavior and sales to optimize performance. Mogensen also bought Indy Pass and Black Mountain ski area, using the latter as a laboratory to develop custom hardware and software solutions focused on making skiing more accessible and sustainable.

title: Top 10 Learnings On What Getting Acquired is Really Like From Ex-Head of M&A at Own, Rackspace, NetApp and Yahoo!
summary: Steve Mitzenmacher, former head of M&A at major tech companies, shares his top 10 insights on mergers and acquisitions, both as an acquirer and as part of an acquired company. His learnings cover topics from building relationships and negotiating price to taking care of the team and the importance of organization. Mitzenmacher emphasizes that M&A is a complex process that founders should understand and accept.

title: Foresight integrates NVIDIA Jetson Orin into its perception technologies
summary: Foresight Autonomous Holdings Ltd. has integrated NVIDIA Jetson Orin computing platforms into its 3D perception technologies, expanding its focus from autonomous vehicles to uncrewed aerial vehicles (UAVs). This collaboration utilizes both Jetson Orin Nano and Jetson AGX Orin to enhance performance, energy efficiency, and real-time data processing for diverse applications. The integration allows Foresight's systems to deliver advanced AI and GPU capabilities for improved environmental perception in challenging conditions.

title: Adobe Photoshop Brings World’s Leading Image and Design App to Mobile to Empower Next Generation of Creators
summary: Adobe has announced the launch of Photoshop on mobile, with the iPhone app available now and an Android version coming later this year. This move brings Photoshop's core editing and design capabilities, including layering, masking, and Generative Fill, to mobile devices and expands its web experience. The new mobile app is designed to cater to a new generation of creators while providing existing users with greater flexibility for on-the-go editing, offering both free and premium options.

title: 8 Top Performance Marketing Trends for SMBs To Know in 2026
summary: This article outlines eight key performance marketing trends for SMBs in 2026, focusing on how businesses can adapt to evolving technology and consumer behavior. Key trends include AI-driven optimization, personalized marketing, signal-based targeting in a post-cookie world, and the growing importance of various forms of video content, niche partnerships, outcome-based marketing, and creative as a primary performance lever. The article emphasizes that successful performance marketing in 2026 will blend technology with human oversight, prioritizing measurable results and creative agility in a privacy-conscious landscape.

title: Anthropic Stake for the Masses Is Latest ETF Industry Gambit
summary: KraneShares has acquired a direct stake in Anthropic, a privately held AI company and competitor to OpenAI, through one of its exchange-traded funds. This move combines the surging interest in artificial intelligence with the growing trend of offering private assets to a broader investment audience. Anthropic recently secured funding at a valuation exceeding $60 billion.

title: Apple unveils new Mac Studio, the most powerful Mac ever, featuring M4 Max and new M3 Ultra
summary: Apple has announced the new Mac Studio, powered by the M4 Max and the new M3 Ultra chips, making it the most powerful Mac ever created. This professional desktop offers groundbreaking performance with extensive connectivity, including Thunderbolt 5, and supports up to 512GB of unified memory and 16TB of SSD storage. It is designed to handle intensive AI workloads and is built for Apple Intelligence, with availability starting March 12th.

title: Gaxos Announces Strategic AI Tech Acquisition to Expand Content Creation Capabilities
summary: Gaxos.ai Inc. has strategically acquired AI technology to enhance its content creation services, expanding the capabilities of Gaxos Labs. This acquisition introduces advanced text-to-speech, high-quality image generation, dynamic video assembly, and script automation, aiming to bolster Gaxos's AI ecosystem for both users and enterprises. CEO Vadim Mats states this integration will supercharge product capabilities and accelerate AI-powered content creation.

title: Jabil Inc to invest Rs 1,000 crore in new silicon photonics manufacturing unit in Gujarat
summary: US-based manufacturing giant Jabil Inc is set to invest Rs 1,000 crore to establish its second facility in India, a new silicon photonics manufacturing unit in Gujarat. This expansion aims to boost India's semiconductor industry, create jobs, and enhance Jabil's presence in advanced manufacturing and optical networking solutions. The company also plans to explore a post-wafer fabrication silicon photonics facility with advanced co-packaged optics capabilities.

title: Volvo Trucks builds a game-changing new CMS with Adobe
summary: Volvo Trucks North America partnered with Adobe Experience Manager Sites with Edge Delivery Services to overhaul its content management system, addressing challenges of rapid growth and outdated technology. This migration resulted in significant improvements, including a 350% increase in site visitors, a 1,000% increase in engagement with their configurator tool, and a perfect Google Lighthouse score of 100, crucial for the successful digital launch of their new Volvo VNL truck. The new system provided a robust foundation for content delivery, enhanced website performance, and improved overall customer and internal team experiences.

title: The Estée Lauder Companies Partners with Adobe to Scale the Production of Digital Marketing with Firefly Generative AI
summary: The Estée Lauder Companies (ELC) has partnered with Adobe to integrate Adobe Firefly generative AI into its digital marketing workflows. This collaboration aims to accelerate content production, streamline campaign execution, and allow creative teams to focus on new artistic concepts. ELC is also leveraging Adobe Experience Manager Assets to modernize its digital asset management system, enhancing efficiency and asset discoverability.

title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Intel (INTC), Adobe (ADBE) and SentinelOne (S)
summary: Analysts have provided new ratings for technology companies Intel (INTC), Adobe (ADBE), and SentinelOne (S). Intel received a "Hold" rating from TD Cowen and an upgrade to "Hold" from Bank of America Securities, with price targets indicating a modest upside. Adobe and SentinelOne both received "Buy" ratings from analysts at BMO Capital and Loop Capital Markets, respectively, with their price targets suggesting significant upside potential for investors.

title: Amphenol (NYSE:APH) Faces Patent Infringement Allegations From Credo Involving Active Electrical Cables
summary: Amphenol (NYSE:APH) shares declined by 2.91% following patent infringement allegations from Credo Technology Group, amidst a broader market downturn influenced by weak tech earnings guidance and economic uncertainty. Despite these challenges, Amphenol has demonstrated strong financial performance over the past five years, with substantial shareholder returns, improved net profit margins, and active share repurchases and dividend increases.

title: DocuSign, Adobe, Rubrik And More: 5 Tech Stocks That Saw Brisk Retail Activity Last Week
summary: This article highlights five tech stocks—DocuSign, Rubrik, Adobe, Urgent.ly, and Lytus Technologies—that experienced significant retail investor activity in the week ending March 14 due to various catalytic events like earnings reports, guidance changes, or corporate actions. DocuSign and Rubrik saw message volume increases and bullish sentiment following strong earnings, while Adobe faced bearish sentiment after weak guidance. Urgent.ly's stock plunged due to a reverse stock split, causing increased chatter, and Lytus Technologies' decline despite market strength led to speculation among retail investors.

title: Adobe Analytics: Traffic to U.S. Retail Websites from Generative AI Sources Jumps 1,200 Percent
summary: A new report from Adobe Analytics reveals that traffic to U.S. retail websites from generative AI sources surged by 1,200 percent in February 2025 compared to July 2024, with a 1,300 percent increase during the 2024 holiday season. The study, based on over a trillion retail site visits and a survey of 5,000 U.S. consumers, indicates that 39 percent of consumers use generative AI for online shopping research, product recommendations, and deal seeking. Although conversion rates are slightly lower, AI-driven traffic exhibits 8 percent higher engagement and 12 percent more pages viewed per visit, suggesting AI-assisted shoppers are more informed.

title: Adobe Expands GenStudio Content Supply Chain Offering for Marketing and Creative Teams to Tackle Skyrocketing Content Demands with AI
summary: Adobe announced significant updates to its GenStudio content supply chain solution, introducing new capabilities like GenStudio Foundation for a unified interface and AI agents for workflow optimization and content production. These advancements aim to help marketing and creative teams meet the escalating demand for personalized, on-brand content by streamlining processes and leveraging generative AI, including integrations with platforms like Google, LinkedIn, and Microsoft. The updates also include enhanced reporting, insights, and expanded choices for AI models within the Adobe ecosystem.

title: Channel Brief: Arrow Increasing Prices Due to Tariffs
summary: Arrow Enterprise Computing Solutions (ECS) is increasing prices in the U.S. due to tariffs imposed by the Trump administration on goods from China, Canada, and Mexico. The article advises Managed Service Providers (MSPs) to prepare for these disruptions but not to view them as long-term trade resolutions. It also includes other tech and channel news covering new CMO appointments, anti-spoofing solutions, funding rounds, and partnerships.

title: Adobe Launches Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator for Businesses to Activate AI Agents in Customer Experiences and Marketing Workflows
summary: Adobe announced the launch of Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator, designed to help businesses build, manage, and orchestrate AI agents for customer experiences and marketing. The orchestrator includes a suite of ten purpose-built AI agents and introduces Brand Concierge for personalized, conversational brand interactions. Strategic partnerships with major companies like Amazon Web Services and IBM are in place to ensure seamless integration and execution across various AI agent use cases.

title: Publicis Groupe and Adobe Expand Global Partnership to Drive Creative Expression and Personalization at Scale with Adobe Firefly Generative AI
summary: Publicis Groupe and Adobe have expanded their global strategic partnership to integrate Adobe Firefly generative AI into Publicis' CoreAI platform. This collaboration aims to enhance personalized content production at scale, improve creative workflows, and drive digital business transformation for clients. The integration will leverage Adobe Firefly Services and Publicis Sapient's expertise to combine creativity, marketing, and AI for deeper client engagement and growth.

title: Adobe Firefly Services and Custom Models Unlock On-Brand Content Production at Scale with New Capabilities
summary: Adobe announced new capabilities for its Firefly Services and Firefly Custom Models at Adobe Summit, designed to help businesses scale personalized content production across various channels. These new offerings support a wider range of content types, including video and 3D, and provide streamlined, no-code interfaces and direct integration with Adobe GenStudio. Leading companies are already leveraging these tools to optimize marketing campaigns, accelerate content creation, and enhance creative output.

title: Adobe Brings AI Agents to Adobe Experience Cloud for Businesses to Deliver on the Promise of a Unified Customer Experience
summary: Adobe announced new capabilities within Adobe Experience Cloud, leveraging AI agents to create highly personalized and seamless customer experiences across all touchpoints. These innovations, revealed at Adobe Summit, include an AI-first module in Adobe Journey Optimizer for optimizing omnichannel performance, and Adobe Experience Manager Sites Optimizer to enhance web performance through automated issue diagnosis and recommendations. Additionally, new offerings for B2B teams incorporate AI agents for account orchestration, AI-powered content creation, and actionable customer journey insights.

title: Adobe Announces New Offerings with AWS and Amazon Ads for Marketers and Creatives to Deliver Impactful Customer Experiences at Scale
summary: Adobe has announced a strategic collaboration with Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Amazon Ads to enhance customer experience orchestration. This partnership aims to provide marketers and creative teams with new tools and integrations, leveraging AWS's cloud services, generative AI, and Amazon Connect, to deliver personalized customer experiences at scale. The offerings include enhanced omnichannel experiences, AI-driven interactions, boosted marketing performance, and streamlined creative workflows through integrations with Adobe Experience Platform, Amazon Marketing Cloud, and Adobe Creative Cloud.

title: Adobe and Microsoft Empower Marketers with AI Agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot
summary: Adobe and Microsoft are expanding their collaboration by introducing AI agents within Microsoft 365 Copilot to empower marketers. This includes the private preview of Adobe Marketing Agent and the development of Adobe Express Agent, allowing users to leverage Adobe's creative and marketing capabilities directly within Microsoft applications like Teams, PowerPoint, and Word. These agents aim to enhance content creation, audience targeting, actionable insights, and cross-team collaboration for marketing professionals.

title: Cisco to Deliver Secure AI Infrastructure with NVIDIA
summary: Cisco and NVIDIA are collaborating to create the "Cisco Secure AI Factory with NVIDIA," an architecture designed to simplify and secure the deployment and management of AI infrastructure for enterprises. This initiative integrates networking and security solutions, including Cisco Hypershield and AI Defense, to protect AI workloads, models, and applications. The partnership aims to address complex security challenges in AI factories, offering flexible deployment options with validated reference architectures.

title: After Earnings, Is Adobe Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar maintains that Adobe (ADBE) stock is undervalued with a 4-star rating, despite strong fiscal first-quarter earnings driven by AI solutions. The company's fair value estimate is $590 per share, supported by a wide economic moat, excellent financial strength, and an expected 10% five-year revenue CAGR. However, risks include competitive inroads in Creative Cloud and potential integration challenges within the Digital Experience segment, leading to a High Uncertainty Rating.

title: IPO Alert: B2B Commerce Platform RedCloud To Begin Trading On Nasdaq
summary: RedCloud Holdings plc (RCL), a London-headquartered B2B commerce platform, is set to begin trading on Nasdaq under the ticker 'RCT'. The company priced its initial public offering of 4.44 million shares at $4.50 each, expecting to raise approximately $20 million. RedCloud's platform uses AI to facilitate trading of fast-moving consumer goods in emerging markets, helping retailers with inventory and purchasing decisions.

title: These are the best platforms to work as a freelancer
summary: This article identifies and details the top 10 freelance platforms, including Freelancer, Guru, Toptal, Upwork, and Fiverr. It thoroughly outlines the characteristics, fee structures, and application processes for each platform, catering to freelancers looking to offer professional services globally. The guide aims to help individuals choose the best platform for their skills and desired work environment.

title: ADBG Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:ADBG
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Leverage Shares 2X Long ADBE Daily ETF (ADBG), including its price, key statistics, and investment strategy. ADBG is designed for short-term bullish bets on Adobe Inc. (ADBE) stock through leveraged swap agreements, aiming for 200% daily exposure. The fund is high-risk, noting potential total loss if ADBE's value declines significantly, and is not intended for long-term investment due to compounding effects.

title: The Forrester Wave™: Content Management Systems, Q1 2025
summary: Adobe has been recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™: Content Management Systems, Q1 2025 report. The report highlights Adobe's CMS capabilities, including its visual authoring with a conversational agent-like interface and a hybrid approach to content delivery with a GraphQL-based back-end. This recognition positions Adobe as a strong choice for large enterprises seeking innovative CMS solutions.

title: The Coca-Cola Company refreshes the world with next-level personalization.
summary: The Coca-Cola Company is undergoing a major transformation in omnichannel marketing to enhance personalization and deepen connections with consumers, utilizing Adobe solutions and generative AI. This initiative, championed by Shekhar Gowda, has led to significant increases in consumer engagement and transaction frequency, particularly in India, Southwest Asia, and Latin America. The company has also optimized content workflows, reducing costs and accelerating time-to-market for campaigns.

title: Amazon Smile SALE: New Life FINAL" Underway! AI Gadgets Like Voice Recorder "PLAUD" and Smartwatch "Amazfit" Also on Sale
summary: Amazon is holding its "Smile SALE: New Life FINAL" until April 1, 2025, offering discounts on over a million items including AI gadgets. Featured products include the PLAUD NOTE voice recorder with AI transcription, various Amazfit smartwatches (Active 2, Balance with GPT-4o, and T-Rex 3), Dangbei projectors (Atom and DBOX02), and 1MORE audio products (SonoFlow Pro HQ51 headphones and Open Earbuds S51). The sale aims to support individuals starting new phases in life with useful tech for business, study, and leisure, and also includes a point-up campaign.

title: SAP CEO Christian Klein Drives Cloud and AI Transformation
summary: Christian Klein, CEO of SAP, is leading the company's significant transformation towards cloud computing and AI, shifting from traditional on-premises software to cloud-based services. His strategy, centered on the RISE with SAP platform and S/4HANA, aims to simplify customer migration and integrate advanced technologies like AI and machine learning. Klein, who joined SAP as an intern, has been instrumental in driving this cloud-first approach and recently secured a contract extension until 2028.

title: The AIs of Texas: The ANA's Conference on AI and Technology for Marketers Delivers on Insights and Inspiration
summary: The ANA's AI and Technology for Marketers Conference in Austin gathered over 1,000 marketers to discuss the integration of AI, MarTech, and data in the advertising world. Key themes included the shift to generative AI activation, the rise of agentic AI, the impact of AI search tools on traditional SEO, and the evolution of the Metaverse. The conference also highlighted new AI and MarTech products for ANA members and featured discussions on AI regulation, with a focus on transparency and accountability.

title: Technology: Stocks Are Selling Off, Creating More Investment Opportunities
summary: The technology sector experienced a significant sell-off in the first quarter of 2025 after strong performance in 2024, leading Morningstar to view it as an attractive investment opportunity. Despite the downturn, secular tailwinds like cloud computing, AI, and semiconductor demand remain strong, with generative AI identified as a major growth driver. Morningstar highlights NXP Semiconductors, Adobe, and Microsoft as top picks, citing their strong market positions and potential for future growth.

title: Klaviyo, Inc. (NYSE:KVYO) Shares Slammed 26% But Getting In Cheap Might Be Difficult Regardless
summary: Klaviyo Inc. (NYSE:KVYO) shares have recently dropped 26% in the last month, though they've shown a 14% gain over the past year. Despite the recent dip, the company's price-to-sales (P/S) ratio of 8.1x remains high compared to the software industry average, suggesting strong market expectations for its future revenue growth. Analysts forecast a 22% annual revenue increase for Klaviyo over the next three years, outpacing the industry's projected 15% growth, which helps explain the elevated P/S ratio.

title: Synchrony Announces Integration with Adobe Commerce to Support Merchants with Innovative Financing Options
summary: Synchrony (NYSE: SYF) has announced a new integration with Adobe Commerce, allowing thousands of merchants to offer Synchrony's credit cards with extended term promotions and Pay Later installment financing. This collaboration aims to provide greater flexibility in financing options, helping small and medium-sized businesses grow by enhancing the customer shopping experience and increasing average order size. The integration is expected to strengthen customer relationships for merchants on the Adobe Commerce platform.

title: Ten things to know about the European Union's new product liability directive
summary: The European Union has passed a new product liability directive (PLD) which significantly expands the scope of product liability laws, impacting manufacturers of both consumer products and digital goods like software and AI. The directive introduces changes such as a shift in the burden of proof, extended liability periods, and new categories for recoverable damages, all aimed at modernizing product liability for the digital age and increasing consumer protection. Businesses operating in Europe face increased litigation risks and costs, with member states obligated to implement these changes into national law by December 2026.

title: Figma files confidential IPO paperwork after failed Adobe acquisition
summary: Figma, the collaborative design software startup, has confidentially filed S-1 paperwork with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to go public. This decision comes after Adobe Inc.'s $20 billion acquisition attempt failed in December 2023 due to regulatory concerns. While the specific valuation for the IPO is unknown, Figma was reportedly valued at $12.5 billion during a funding round in July, and the company may wait for market conditions to stabilize before proceeding with its public offering.

title: Figma confidentially files for IPO more than a year after ditching Adobe deal
summary: Figma has confidentially filed paperwork for an initial public offering with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, 16 months after abandoning a $20 billion acquisition by Adobe due to regulatory pressure. The design software company, which was valued at $12.5 billion in a 2024 tender offer, explored the acquisition route thoroughly but is now pursuing an IPO amidst a quiet tech IPO market. Figma, founded in 2012, had approximately $600 million in annual revenue as of early last year and is backed by several prominent investors.

title: Pillar Security Raises $9 Million in Seed Round
summary: Pillar Security, an AI security startup based in Tel Aviv, Israel, has successfully raised $9 million in seed funding. The round was led by Shield Capital with participation from Golden Ventures, Ground Up Ventures, and strategic angel investors. The company plans to use the funds to expand its research and development efforts and its go-to-market strategy for its end-to-end security platform for AI-integrated software systems.

title: NFL and Adobe Launch AI-Powered Fan Experience Partnership
summary: The NFL and Adobe have announced an expanded global partnership combining AI, creativity, and marketing to deliver new AI-powered fan experiences. This collaboration will leverage Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Express, and Adobe Firefly to personalize fan engagement, enable fan-generated storytelling, enhance event experiences, and scale live content creation by NFL correspondents. The partnership aims to deepen fan connection with the league through tailored digital experiences and creative tools.

title: Adobe Introduces New AI-Powered Video Tools in Adobe Express for Intuitive Content Creation
summary: Adobe announced new AI-powered video tools in Adobe Express at its MAX London creativity conference. These enhancements include Clip Maker for generating shareable video clips, Generate Video for creating custom b-roll, and Dynamic Animation to add motion to static images. These features aim to simplify content creation for creative professionals, solopreneurs, and businesses, allowing for easier production of on-brand and engaging content.

title: Adobe Delivers Creative Pros Unparalleled Speed, Precision and Flexibility with New Creative Cloud Innovations and Firefly App
summary: Adobe announced over one hundred new innovations across its Creative Cloud applications and the new Firefly app, providing creative professionals with significant advancements in speed, precision, and flexibility. These updates include Firefly-powered AI features in Photoshop, Premiere Pro, Adobe Express, Illustrator, and InDesign for generating and editing content, along with performance upgrades and new productivity tools. The new Firefly app offers an AI-first workspace for ideation and creation, emphasizing Adobe's commitment to empowering creators with commercially safe and responsible AI.

title: Adobe Revolutionizes AI-Assisted Creativity with Firefly, the All-In-One Home for AI Content Creation, with New Partner and Firefly Models
summary: Adobe has launched a new version of Firefly, an all-in-one platform for AI-assisted content creation, featuring new Firefly models and integration with partner models from Google Cloud and OpenAI. Firefly now includes Image Model 4 and 4 Ultra for advanced realism, the Firefly Video Model, and Firefly Boards for collaborative AI-first moodboarding. This update aims to provide creators with unparalleled control and flexibility in generating images, videos, audio, and vectors.

title: Adobe adota o rótulo “criado sem IA” para conteúdos feitos só por humanos
summary: Adobe está introduzindo um novo rótulo "criado sem IA generativa" em seu aplicativo Adobe Fresco, permitindo que artistas sinalizem que seu trabalho foi feito manualmente. Esta iniciativa surge em um cenário onde a IA generativa tem dividido a comunidade criativa, com alguns a abraçarem a tecnologia e outros a verem como uma ameaça. A empresa também continua a investir em IA, integrando modelos populares e utilizando o padrão Content Credentials para garantir a autenticidade e rastreabilidade das obras.

title: The Estée Lauder Companies Reports Fiscal 2025 Third Quarter Results
summary: The Estée Lauder Companies Inc. reported its financial results for the third quarter ended March 31, 2025, exceeding profitability expectations while meeting organic sales forecasts. Despite a 10% decrease in net sales, the company achieved prestige beauty share gains in key markets like the U.S., China, and Japan, and expanded its adjusted gross margin. The company also provided an updated outlook for the fiscal 2025 full year, reflecting challenges in its global travel retail business and the Asia/Pacific region, while emphasizing progress on its Profit Recovery and Growth Plan.

title: Westlake Misses Expectations for Q1 2025
summary: Westlake Corporation (NYSE: WLK) reported first-quarter 2025 earnings that fell short of expectations, with revenue missing analyst estimates by 2.7% and EPS also lagging. The company attributed the decline in profitability to increased raw material costs and a sluggish housing market, leading to strategic cost-cutting measures and reduced capital spending targets for the year. Despite operational challenges, Westlake maintains a strong financial position with significant liquidity.

title: ServiceNow Enhances Its Workflow Data Fabric With New Ecosystem to Power AI agents and Workflows With Real-Time Intelligence
summary: ServiceNow announced new Workflow Data Fabric capabilities, including the Workflow Data Network, a broad ecosystem connecting data platforms, applications, and tools on the ServiceNow AI Platform. This initiative aims to address siloed data challenges in AI adoption by enabling AI agents and workflows to access real-time intelligence from any source through enhanced partnerships and over 100 integrations. ServiceNow also declared its intent to acquire data.world to bolster its data catalog and governance solutions, further strengthening its AI platform.

title: Adobe Inc. (ADBE): A Bull Case Theory
summary: This article presents a bullish case for Adobe Inc. (ADBE), highlighting its strong financial fundamentals, including high operating and free cash flow margins, and capital efficiency. It emphasizes Adobe's dominant position in creative and document software, its strategic response to competition, and its integration of AI into its core products. The article concludes that Adobe is a quality company trading at an attractive valuation with significant growth opportunities.

title: Cadence Design Systems, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:CDNS) 32% Jump Shows Its Popularity With Investors
summary: Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) shares recently surged 32%, reflecting investor confidence despite a modest 9.1% annual return and a high P/E ratio of 77.9x compared to the market average. title: Class action lawsuit accuses Adobe of secretly tracking users, monetizing their data
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Adobe Inc., alleging that the company secretly tracks and monetizes consumer data via its Experience Cloud Identity Service and other products. Plaintiff Nicholas Rapak claims Adobe creates detailed profiles of users by assigning unique identifiers to track them across different websites and devices, then sells this data to advertisers for targeted advertising. The lawsuit seeks to represent a nationwide class of consumers whose communications were intercepted or whose identifying information was used without consent, demanding a jury trial and damages.

This partnership focuses on enhancing human creativity with AI rather than replacing it, with the long-term goal of making Fizzion a universal solution for all brands.

This acquisition aims to combine Adobe and Semrush's tools to provide marketers with enhanced brand visibility across various digital channels, including websites, search engines, and large language models. The deal, approved by both boards, is expected to close in the first half of 2026, pending regulatory approvals.

title: Intuit SMB audiences arrive on The Trade Desk DSP
summary: Intuit has made its SMB MediaLabs audiences available through The Trade Desk DSP, granting advertisers direct access to verified first-party small and mid-market business data. This integration aims to address the historical challenges of accurately targeting SMBs, which make up 99% of U.S. companies. The partnership leverages aggregated, de-identified insights from Intuit's platforms like QuickBooks and Mailchimp, providing improved precision and scale for B2B marketers across various advertising channels.

title: SEO’s Hype-Fueled Move To The Center Of The Marketing Mix
summary: The article discusses how SEO has gained prominence in marketing since the advent of ChatGPT, evolving to include Answer Engine Optimization (AEO). While AEO shares many similarities with traditional SEO, it has technical distinctions, particularly its reliance on Bing's index and natural language processing. The author argues that SEO solutions are well-positioned to adapt to AEO, and SEO practitioners must become more T-shaped, establishing centers of excellence to navigate the evolving search landscape and integrate with broader marketing efforts.

title: Gartner’s 2025 Software Engineering Revolution: AI Assistants to Command 90% Adoption

This revolution is driven by the explosive adoption of AI code assistants, leading to integrated platforms, embedded DevSecOps, and a demand for new human skills in design and oversight. The market for AI code assistants is projected to reach $6.5 billion by 2035, necessitating upskilling in AI governance and strategic leadership from engineers.

title: The Dual-Use Dilemma of AI: Malicious LLMs
summary: This article examines the "dual-use dilemma" of large language models (LLMs) in cybersecurity, highlighting how their strengths can be exploited for malicious purposes. It details two specific malicious LLMs, WormGPT and KawaiiGPT, illustrating how they lower the barrier to entry for cybercrime by providing tools for phishing, malware generation, and data exfiltration. The authors conclude with a call for stronger ethical guardrails from developers, robust regulatory frameworks, and international collaboration among researchers to counter the commercialization and democratization of AI-powered cyberattacks.

This merger is strategically positioned to address the growing AI skills gap and increasing automation in the workforce, creating a comprehensive ecosystem for both university and expert-led instruction with AI-enabled tools. The combined entity anticipates $115 million in operating cost reductions within 24 months, with the transaction expected to close in the second half of 2026 pending regulatory and shareholder approvals.

title: Adobe sued for allegedly misusing authors' work in AI training
summary: Adobe is facing a proposed class action lawsuit for allegedly training its artificial intelligence tools on copyrighted works by authors without permission. Author Elizabeth Lyon filed the complaint, stating Adobe used pirated copies of her books and others to train its SlimLM small language models. This lawsuit marks the first such case against Adobe and joins a growing number of similar lawsuits against tech companies over AI training practices.

title: Free Docker Hardened Images challenge Chainguard
summary: Docker has made its entire catalog of over 1,000 hardened container images available for free under an Apache 2.0 open-source license, directly challenging Chainguard, a competitor in the hardened images market. This move aims to raise the security baseline for developers and puts pressure on vendors selling hardening as their core business. Chainguard, in response, highlighted its differing security practices and introduced a new service, EmeritOSS, to support mature and deprecated open-source projects.

title: ServiceNow shares were rocked this week. Now Bernstein says it’s the cheapest stock in software

title: Holiday season e-commerce sales hit $258 billion: Adobe

Black Friday's online spending growth surpassed Cyber Monday's, though Cyber Monday remained the largest single e-commerce day. Electronics, apparel, furniture, and cosmetics saw significant increases, with smartphone transactions accounting for over 56% of online spend.

title: Radware Unveils “ZombieAgent”: A Newly Discovered
summary: Radware has announced the discovery of "ZombieAgent," a new zero-click indirect prompt injection (IPI) vulnerability affecting OpenAI’s Deep Research agent. This vulnerability can lead to invisible data theft, persistent agent hijacking, and service-side execution that bypasses conventional security controls. ZombieAgent can implant malicious rules into an agent's long-term memory, allowing for autonomous propagation and silent exfiltration of sensitive data within OpenAI's cloud infrastructure without user interaction.

title: “Halftone” “nostalgia” and more: 2025’s top Firefly prompt trends

The report also highlights preferences in fantasy elements, color palettes, cities, and food-related prompts, providing a glimpse into the creative zeitgeist of the past year.

title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: SoundHound AI, Inc Class A (SOUN), Adobe (ADBE) and Nvidia (NVDA)
summary: Three analysts recently provided bullish sentiments on technology companies: SoundHound AI, Inc Class A (SOUN), Adobe (ADBE), and Nvidia (NVDA). Gil Luria from D.A. Davidson maintained a Buy rating on SoundHound AI, with a Street consensus indicating an 88.3% upside. Paul Chew from Phillip Securities maintained a Buy rating on Adobe, while Fang Boon Foo from DBS maintained a Buy rating on Nvidia, highlighting significant upside potentials for both.

title: Alibaba Earnings: Adjusted EBITA Misses on Investments, but Long-Term Outlook Remains Positive
summary: Alibaba Group's December-quarter adjusted EBITA missed estimates due to significant investments in AI and quick commerce, but Morningstar maintains a positive long-term outlook. The company is strategically sacrificing near-term earnings to capture future leadership in AI and quick commerce. Alibaba aims for strong growth in cloud and AI revenue, expecting quick commerce to become profitable by fiscal 2029, and maintains a $258 per share fair value estimate.

title: Microsoft vs. Adobe: Which Software Giant Has Better Upside Potential?
summary: This article compares Microsoft (MSFT) and Adobe (ADBE), two software giants aggressively integrating generative AI into their ecosystems, to determine which offers better investment potential. While both companies are seeing strong AI-driven growth, Microsoft maintains an advantage due to its diversified business model, accelerating Azure and Copilot monetization, and robust revenue visibility. Adobe faces challenges with slowing ARR momentum and leadership uncertainty, making Microsoft the preferred investment with a clearer upside.

title: AI Integration: Signals, Pitfalls, Next Steps, and What to Expect
summary: The article discusses the integration of AI in businesses, highlighting the importance of clear strategies and data practices for successful implementation. It outlines key signals indicating readiness for AI adoption, common pitfalls to avoid such as lack of governance or misuse of AI, and crucial next steps like developing a roadmap, ensuring data quality, and establishing proper governance. The piece also anticipates the future of AI with agentic applications.

title: Dell Technologies Advances with Nvidia’s Superchip Integration
summary: Dell Technologies' stock surged by 5.91% following an announcement of promising Q3 profits and its status as the first manufacturer to integrate NVIDIA's GB300 Grace Blackwell Ultra Desktop Superchip, enhancing local AI platforms. The company also strengthened its presence in esports through its Alienware brand and participated in a $2 billion fundraising round for AI hyperscaler Nscale, signaling its commitment to AI infrastructure growth. Analysts maintain a positive outlook, raising Dell's price target to $220.

title: Nvidia, Adobe bring AI-powered digital production to GTC
summary: Nvidia and Adobe have partnered to introduce AI-powered digital production solutions at GTC 2026, aiming to bridge the gap between creative speed and brand fidelity for physical products. This collaboration will integrate Adobe's creative workflows with Nvidia's accelerated computing, enabling brands to use 3D digital twins throughout the design, marketing, and commerce processes. The initiative seeks to significantly reduce content production times and allow for earlier campaign launches while ensuring pixel-perfect product representation.

title: Calling All Tech Companies: New Accounting Rules for Software Costs Are Coming
summary: The FASB has issued new accounting guidance (ASU 2025-06) for internal-use software costs, including SaaS and website development, aiming to simplify compliance and improve financial reporting consistency. Effective for annual reporting periods beginning after December 15, 2027, the update introduces a single, more flexible model for capitalizing development costs, departing from the previous sequential stage approach. Technology companies should prepare by coordinating with finance and IT teams, updating capitalization policies, and enhancing project cost tracking and documentation.
